eps.name: The End of Past and Present

lines.st: 'Universe'.
How should we interpret this word? With mathematics, physics, faith, imagination—
We exhausted our minds attempting to define this still unfamiliar word in a variety of languages, and arrogantly claimed to have mastered true 'knowledge'.
So why are most of us silent when destruction is so near?

You cannot turn back from knowledge. Since we have glimpsed the partial truths of countless fallacies, it is only natural that we cannot retreat into the darkness of ignorance, and ignore the advent of the end.
I think, perhaps I've touched on the key to resolving this ultimate question—
Use the language you and I created, Doctor.
'It's time for this war to end, Dr. Doctor.'
'The purple flames ignited by that Draco have accelerated the course of the war. Once the Exemplars and the ducal alliance's troops push onto Nezzsalem's final stronghold—'
'Our goal of concluding this war will finally be within reach.'
'But... Dr. Kal'tsit, how long will it take for us to cover this short stretch?'
'There's no way to tell, Amiya.'

lines.loc: Londinium outskirts battlefield, wreck of the Gloriana

lines.x: Originium...
Bloody Originium's really chopped this battleship in halves. I have to remain here and continue the fight with the local garrison...
Sorry, kid. I can't go on with you.
This whole time we've seen nothing but ruins and dead... no garrisons at all.
Also! Please don't call me kid! The monastery's already celebrated my coming-of-age!
Haha, you remind me of my daughter.
See the light shining up high there? That's part of Her Grace the Duke of Caster's Gloriana.
That gun's still working, which means the news I got from the broadcast is true. My squad served the Duke, so I have to go back.
But your wounds...
I can walk, since you helped patch me up.
But you, are you sure you want to go back to the city? It's the most polluted place there is. The active Originium in there will be the death of you.
The Bishop told me that the kind person who saved me from the mines went through hell and back to deliver me all the way to the monastery.
So I knew from an early age that salvation never comes without a cost.
Don't worry about me. The clergy taught me many ways to keep myself safe.
As long as I can save one more person, anyone, before I reach my end...
To be frank, this is the first time I've seen a cleric as devout as you... Your kind heart is the pride of Victoria.
The Bishop says faith is not at all for pride or glory... the faithful believe with sincerity that good deeds will better this land...
Better this land...? Hah, maybe.
But anyway, thanks, little Cautus. For everything.
Could you help me out one last time, kid? If you make it back to the city, if my family... and my daughter's still there...
Please tell her that papa's always protected her.

The Victorian soldier struggles to raise his injured arm, and gives a non-standard salute to the Cautus, before calmly turning towards the distant wreckage and vanishing into the thicket of Originium.
The Cautus looks to the faint light emanating from the ship's gun and cannot help but to sigh.
Artillery sounds in the distance, and the tiny Cautus continues towards the city on the horizon—Londinium.

lines.st: 'I hear the cannon fire too, Dr. Doctor. Madam Catherine's Crawler is already assaulting the Nachzehrer's front.'
'But will the war end once we beat them? And the innocents who've suffered... what will happen to them?'
'There's no way to save every single life in a war, Amiya. What we can do is help the Londiniers end this misery as soon as we can.'
'And that means the Nachzehrer aren't our ultimate problem.'

lines.x: Mary, Davey, please don't tell anyone else this kid is hiding here.
I met him at the city walls, and he has nowhere to go. His parents are Londiniers, he said, and he met you two in the park a long while ago, yes?
I don't remember. You helped us, and I promised to help you. But he's not you!
Thank you. He'll just hide here and won't run off or make a sound. I guarantee he won't give anyone a fright.
(Nods quickly)
I'll be back soon. I promised someone I'd find his daughter.
It's impossible for me to protect you out there when there's so many hostiles—
Sorry, I couldn't find anywhere better for you to settle down. I've helped quite a lot of people here though, so I trust these kids to keep you safe.
The tiny Sarkaz tugs sharply on the Cautus's arm, but touches the pointy Originium crystals instead.
He pulls back his hand on reflex.
(softly) S-Sorry! When I was with my pa and ma... we rarely came in contact with Infected.
...I'll come back for you.

lines.st: The Cautus is not gone for long.
When she arrives at the soldier's home as directed, there is nothing left.
But letters found in the ruin tell her the soldier's daughter joined a force rebelling against the Sarkaz in the city—the Exemplars.

It is a difficult journey for the Cautus as she returns to the refuge. When the Victorians joined forces to attack the Sarkaz front at the Palace of Westhaleg, Originium continued to spread throughout the city.
There are also those mad Sarkaz mercenaries wandering recklessly around, as if they've lost any semblance of humanity.
Her head hurts, from the city's terrible condition, but also from the horrific black crystals on her body.
'I might be nearing my end now, Father Bishop.'

lines.x: Excuse me... were you the troops who protected the people here from those mercenaries?
Yes. We have both Exemplar and ducal alliance troops in our squad, and we got a distress call while we were gearing up to enter the Palace.
Trust me, this mess will be over soon. Thank you too, for taking care of the kids and refugees.
Uh, are you looking for someone?
The Cautus shakes her head.
Right, we did mop up some of the devils hiding about. Probably here because their side's about to lose.
See you. We have to hurry on to the front.
...I didn't see that kid.
...Follow me.
The child leads the Cautus through bunker after bunker within the factory, and the latter spots other refuge residents silently carrying away Sarkaz corpses.
They walk up to a pile of containers, and the child struggles but manages to push the messed up lid open.
The cowering Sarkaz child from before looks at them in fear.
She's here.
I promised you I'd help you, didn't I? It all happened so suddenly earlier, so this was the only place I could hide him.
But he can't stay here.
On my way back, I met a group called Reunion. They can take him in.
Don't worry, we'll take good care of that one—but what about you?
Your Oripathy's serious... you should come with us too.
I... still have some things to do.
Alright, I won't force you. You remind me of a lot of my comrades.
Here's some suppressants and protective gear. Good luck.
...You too.
Victorians advance in waves from afar towards the Nachzehrer King's last stand as life fades away.
That is not her war. She feels fear, and has no way of comprehending how these people overcome their fear of death, what keeps them pushing onward.
But she hopes to save people too, to continue numbly... one life after another.
...Even if it's all in vain.
She recalls the day she left the monastery. When she and the Bishop bid each other farewell by the riverside church.
What is faith, you ask?
Heheh... I traveled to Iberia in my youth, and saw with my own eyes how the lives of those around me collapsed during the Profound Silence, but that tenacious nation has never once yielded.
At first, I only admired the country's vitality, but as I lived my life, I came to realize...
That vitality, that is faith.
Who was there to teach me, or even the other Iberians, what path to take in life? Did anyone promise us we would surmount such a terrifying disaster?
No, but faced with calamity we always convince ourselves. Everything we go through is inevitably for something, suffering will eventually end, and we will enjoy a wonderful future.
That is faith.
Faith does not reside in scripture, nor in our prayers and hymns, but in our lives.
Faith is in our bodies and minds, in every heartbeat, in every breath. It is the source of all strength.
...Only because we believe.
Father Bishop, if it is as you say, that all suffering eventually ends, then where is our wonderful future you're talking about...?
Father Bishop... I think... I've tried my best.
The Cautus collapses from exhaustion among the ruins, to the sounds of gunfire and cries from afar.
An intense pain reminds her of a cruel truth: this body has met its end.
She waves to those nearby whom she saved from death, staying alone in a wide open space far away from them.
Her body begins to disintegrate.
...I'm willing to believe...
The light caught in the Cautus's eyes begin to dim, until she sinks into endless night.
Then, she seems to see someone hold her almost shattered hand, and light begins to flow back into her eyes.
I think I heard the sound of an Infected disintegrating... again.
I could almost even see their final burst of light. Not too far from us.
We've seen this happen too often lately...
Let's go, Doctor.
Doctor?

opts: What exactly does... stasis within the Assimilated Universe mean?
Does that place we've been prevent even time from escaping?

lines.x: A surge of information rushes forward, trying to engulf him.
But Theresis does not budge.
I finally see with my own eyes what great feat you've managed, Theresa.
He looks up at the tower standing in the void as falling fragments turn back into irregular information and return into the nothingness.
Theresis grabs a fragment that has drifted down to him, but it instantly turns to nothing in his palm.
You opened this door for me, so I'll finish this last part of the journey in your stead, Theresa.
Once we Sarkaz seize the Originium that belongs to us, we shall for the first time hold the power to determine our own fate.
He grips the blade in his hand, and stares coldly at the large rhombus approaching him.
Then, he walks toward the crumbling tower. He knows well his opponent will be there.
Destroy anything, just to create a ladder. Theresa has left a path for him.

lines.st: 'The myriad souls who never gave up resisting until they completely dissipated, did one last thing for the Sarkaz within the Originium—'
'They fought for a future where everyone has a right to survive, for the ones still alive.'
'Onward, everyone.'

lines.x: Just as always, my blade will cut our path forward. And you will lead the way.
I never break my promises, Theresa—
He walks up the flight of stairs, towards the top of the nearly collapsed tower.
(Unknown language) New redundant information?
All that remains is... nothingness?
He looks at the rhombus, it appears to be—
...An eye staring at me.
For the first time in his life, Theresis feels a shiver run down his spine.
But it isn't his own instinctive reaction. He feels that a force, a will he can't see is trying to make him submit.
Be you a god, or my creator, come out and face me.
Dead silence, cold. There is no response from the void.
Master. You shouldn't have come here.
Her Highness has already made a terrible, reckless mistake. I do not want to lose another person I care about.
Master, leave this place. The time is not yet right.
You should not use her to mock me, oh self-proclaimed Creator.
The Ascalon standing before him is no illusion. This marvelous space has materialized his memories.
In my memories, you are the same as before, just a little less mature.
Since those who truly control this place dare not face me directly, then Theresa has indeed succeeded. They must have already been subjected to numerous restrictions.
Otherwise, those arrogant beings wouldn't have tried to get you to stop me.
Theresis raises his sword.
You still do not wish to leave with me, Master?
If you really want to take me away, then defeat me.
As you wish.
You're skilled, but you're merely using the skills I've taught you.
'Ascalon' disappears into a purple haze. She's used her Originium Arts.
Theresis thinks back to the day he and Theresa led the remnants of the Sarkaz army into that storm...
The day he walked into the purple smoke alone and took the knife from that girl's hands... just as he does again.
It has been some time, Ascalon.
The rhombus-shaped eye is collapsing.
Theresis raises his head and looks up at the golden sea above. The platform's 'descent' is slowing, but it will still eventually crash into the ocean.
As he steps onto the platform, the sky and the ground, up and down, instantly reverse themselves.
In this barren void, without any rules, what does 'destruction' even mean?
Just what should he do now? Theresis isn't sure.
You will vanish, 'Ascalon'.
I've severed the connection between you and a certain 'will'.
Master, I've just seen a great many images flash by.
They were all images of me, but why do I feel like I'm having an out-of-body experience? Why do I feel like I'm so far away?
That's just my memory of you.
Of me?
You aren't a false phantasm, but you're not the real Ascalon either.
They're mocking me, and you as well.
I will put an end to this farce myself.
Master, does this mean you are going to die?
What difference would it make?
I'm sorry. I wasn't able to save you.
No. You did well. You did plenty well enough.
Theresis swings his sword with all his might, cleaving the memory construct, and the already broken platform, in two.
'Ascalon' begins to shatter into pieces. Her shards, along with the other debris, 'fall into' the golden sea, transforming into more chaotic information.
Go.
At least take a few of my memories when you go. Catch up to Theresa and the myriad souls on their journey.
He can no longer hear Theresa or the myriad souls, but he still has a slight sense of familiarity with the information scattered around him.
Everywhere, although it's vanishing quickly—
She's fighting against it, as are the myriad souls.
The tower has completely collapsed.
Everything has returned to the sea of data. And the sea is slowly calming down.
Theresis is also falling into the vast, golden ocean. He is silent, and miniscule—
This is by no means the end, but do we have enough time left?

lines.st: 'Theresis, our blades will not be enough to destroy the gods. However, we may manage to destroy them with their own methods.'
'But I can't promise you this will work.'
'I have no reason to doubt your judgment, Theresa.'
'Tell me what I must do.'
'Face the gods, directly.'

lines.x: Theresis turns around and, without any hesitation, swims for deeper waters, disappearing fully into the black abyss of data.
After a moment, the surface of the sea begins to stir and boil again.
Then, the sea splits open, as if sliced by a sharp blade—chaotic streams of information surge endlessly into the abyss.
(Unknown language) Connection... unstable.
(Unknown language) —It's you?
She opens her eyes and sees the small, frail creature.
Then all the violent scenes come to a screeching halt.

lines.loc: Throne Room, Palace of Westhaleg Autumn 1098

lines.x: (Angry low moan)
Ascalon does not need your help.
(Disapproving roar)

opts: This is not the proper stage for your fair fight.
Theresis does not have much time left for us.
Ascalon, how are your wounds healing up?
Ascalon, what intel have you received?

lines.x: Don't worry.
With Entelechia's help, I'm recovering quite well.
I was still a step too slow.
Theresis is not here. The only one I've encountered is Manfred.
According to Ascalon's reports, Theresis really has returned from the Command Center.
Nezzsalem surely chose the Palace of Westhaleg as the site for the final battle with an eye towards protecting Theresis.
However, currently, Theresis and Theresa are still concealing their true motives from the other members of the Military Commission.
This includes Manfred, the nominal leader of the Military Commission.
Manfred stands alone in front of the empty throne, facing you down.
Ascalon is fully locked onto him, leaving him no room for any reckless moves.
The standing water from his earlier battle with Ascalon has soaked Manfred's cloak, leaving him slightly disheveled.
Theresa must have already captured Amnannam and handed it over to Theresis. But Theresis is not here, and Manfred seems unaware of his whereabouts...
Doctor, do you think he went there too...

opts: The sea of data within the first Originium.

lines.x: Theresa has been moving too aggressively. If Theresis reasserts himself yet again, the damage could be irreparable.
Terra, as it stands now, is woefully unprepared, regardless of the outcome.

opts: Kal'tsit, what are their chances of success?
Kal'tsit, are we prepared to deal with any and all potential outcomes?

lines.x: Judging by our own past experiences, we can say that Theresa has a full understanding of the rules that govern the Assimilated Universe.
Analyze the rules, rebuild the order, and work with Theresis to seize the first Originium... I believe, with Theresa's abilities, this is far from a pipe dream.
I don't have detailed information on the first Originium either, so naturally, I'm unable to propose any concrete countermeasures.
In other words, with how things have developed so far, it's become very difficult to rely on our past experience and knowledge to analyze the current situation.
Dr. Kal'tsit, do you mean we are helpless to do anything?
Not at all. If their plan succeeds, what Theresis and Theresa are seeking still falls within the bounds of our understanding, and we may not face the worst case scenario.
But if they fail...
Kal'tsit looks at you, her eyes filled with worry.
Dr. Doctor, we must return to the Rhodes Island landship as quickly as possible.
We might still have one last chance to buy Terra a brief moment of respite.

opts: First, we must get Manfred under control.
Instead of worrying about things that have not yet happened, we should eliminate those variables that already exist.

lines.x: (Excited cry)
I will protect the Doctor and restrict Manfred's movements as much as possible.
Settle this quickly. Be wary of the Teekazwurtzen that he's carrying.
Just as the Teekazwurtzen in Manfred's hand begins to glow red, the King of Sarkaz's Arts and Ascalon's blade descend upon him.
This must end.
—Move!
Ascalon instantly dissolves into purple mist, scattering amidst the flash of the blade, before reforming again.
The barrier woven by Amiya protects you and Kal'tsit, but it begins to falter against the blade flash.
(Furious howl)
(Shields with all its might)
The blade's master slowly emerges from the darkness. He looks even more terrifying under the faint light.
You've put yourself in this predicament.
...Your Highness... you...
It looks like Nezzsalem is fulfilling his end of the bargain.
You've come here to see me. Very good.
You see a man standing calmly before the throne—Theresis.
He looks nothing like the man you once knew. He's tightly gripping a vivid, gleaming crystal.
You seem surprised.
You too have set foot in the void. You too have witnessed her departure.
You know full well the heavy price the Sarkaz paid for this.
And yet, you never believed the Sarkaz had any rightful claim to it.
Isn't that right, Dame Kal'tsit?
At least for now, we rejoice in your success.
I thought you would rejoice in my death, Dame.

opts: Just what has he been through?

lines.x: I don't know what he's encountered, but needless to say, the Assimilated Universe's chaotic information continues to rapidly corrupt his will.
It will carry on this way until his entire body is fully recoded in another format.
Originium...
How much longer can he hold out?
Frankly, I'm stunned that he's managed to survive this long.
Your Highness, I shall escort you out. Maybe we can still find a way to—
Londinium does not need me anymore.
Please handle the remaining tasks.
I must do one last thing for the Sarkaz, while my mind is still clear.
Rhodes Island, the equality you seek may be a noble goal for all of Terra—but we Sarkaz will not be there to greet its arrival.
Such bitter irony—you and I brought about that outcome with our own hands, Doctor, and now you're walking the very path she once laid out for me.
Amnannam is responding to Theresis's call.
The entire underground throne room begins to shift. The path by which you entered is now gone.
The hole sealed in the lower layer of Londinium is being filled—at a pace visible to the naked eye.
Stop him!
The surging Originium assimilates Amiya's Arts, something she has never experienced before.
It's not working! Why?
But Theresis still shows the same, rarely seen, pained look on his face.
Cautus, you... are much like her.
He is currently adapting to, and studying, Amnannam. But the more he is exposed to it, the more the chaotic data streams will impact him.
The first Originium, having had its authority seized by force, can't possibly compare to the state it was in when first created—we still have a chance.

opts: We don't have to attack head-on!
Stall! He can't hold out for long.
I need to get closer to him, and Amnannam!

lines.x: You'll never forget how you felt in that moment. You nearly lost Amiya.
However... you feel a torrent of jumbled information rushing through your mind—
Time is slowing down, but you can feel the Originium, the Originium you control.
Maybe, just maybe, you still have a chance, so long as you can remember everything that happened.
If you can do that, you'll be able to stop the Originium.
I will do my best to carve a path for you, Dr. Doctor!
Mon3tr, do not worry about me. Protect the Doctor.
The Doctor is the only person, out of everyone here, who can stop Amnannam.
(Hesitant roar)
Trust me.
(Opens claws to protect you)
The surging Originium activates the pool of dead water that has lain still on the surface for ages. Mist clinging to your mask makes it hard to see the road ahead.
However, you feel a silken thread wrap around your waist, gently guiding you forward.
Keep going!
I told you before; this conflict ended long ago.
Doctor, Theresa and I fully understand that there is no place for us in the Sarkaz future.
However, Ascalon, Manfred... your future does not lie here either.
Originium creeps up Manfred and Ascalon's bodies, pushing them toward the edge, to the end of the battlefield.
Leave. Go to a safe place and wait there.
Remember to take Amnannam back to Kazdel.
—Wait, Your Highness—
Manfred's voice vanishes within the underground throne room. Theresis has already scuttled him off to a safe place.
You don't get to make decisions for me anymore.
Ascalon dissolves into a purple mist, slipping through the cracks in the Originium and remaining in the throne room.
She is everywhere all at once, just waiting for an opportunity.
Folly...
Amnannam is still flashing, but his movements are becoming increasingly difficult.
The Originium is restricting his arms, his eyes, and even his thoughts.
But still, he forces himself to remember one goal——to bury everything here.
He needs to hold on to his last shred of sanity so he can leave Amnannam with Manfred, who is finally safe.
He struggles to lift up his sword, as he once did when he served at the side of the King of Sarkaz.
He raises his sword for the Sarkaz one final time.
Doctor, we meet again at last.
Have you ever... looked up at the sky?
Beyond that sky, far beyond the twin moons, there is a place that we would once return to, where we would sleep peacefully.
But I am the only one left, here speaking with you. In the course of the Sarkaz's eternal search for the remains of dead gods to fend off your enemies, have you ever wondered...?
Why did those gods die?

lines.st: 'In the sea filled with both truth and the unknown, I saw...
'A fleeting vision of the gods before they died, and root of the pain you bear.'
'I now understand you, though you have already passed away. Now I'm the only one left, and I'm speaking to you.'
'Before I retire to my eternal slumber, I will destroy you. I shall build a ladder for the Sarkaz.'
'No longer will anyone toy with the fate of the Sarkaz. We shall reclaim our own destiny, Doctor.'

lines.x: Theresis swings his sword.
A beam of light stings your eyes, and you wrack your brain, trying to recall how to stop Amnannam.
Amiya's barrier breaks, layer by layer, amidst the gleam of the sword. You think you see Mon3tr's huge frame blocking your view.
You suddenly remember everything about the moment you almost lost Amiya. You remember how you stopped it all.

lines.st: You did it, Dr. Doctor.

lines.x: You remembered—
You—!
You told us that we would only have one chance to strike.
You really have slowed down a lot...
Theresis struggles to lower his head and sees the blade that has pierced his heart, as well as Ascalon, with a determined look on her face and tears in her eyes.
The figure steps out of the smoke and pins Theresis to the throne with all her strength.
Amnannam and his sword fall to the ground, lying in the shallow water reflecting the dim light.
Master... this is the end.
Theresis raises his hand.
Careful, do not let him touch it again.
Theresis clutches Ascalon's assassin blade and uses all his strength to slowly draw it from his chest.
There is no blood. There is no warmth. All that's left are a few shattered crystal shards.
Well done. You've finally found your conviction.
...Your heart...
It stopped beating a while ago.
I've repaid my debt.
Take it...
Theresis, straining, shifts his gaze towards Amnannam laying on the ground.
Find Manfred...
And Kazdel... go home...
You—
Ascalon feels herself being pushed away by a very weak hand.
I will stay here.
I cannot let the Sarkaz know... my fate...
He hears faint cheers from above, the cheers of the Victorians.

opts: You missed Logos's elegy for the Nachzehrer.

lines.x: His war ended just before mine.
Doctor, I was able to gaze upon the true face of the gods.
I only regret that I was unable to completely defeat.... you.
Assuming he speaks the truth, he may have bought Terra a moment's respite.
Regardless, we must continue our preparations, Doctor.
Then, Theresis...
He will remain here. I'll ensure that no one disturbs him, and no one finds him.
She holds up Amnannam, and Theresis's sword.
Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit, Amnannam belongs to Kazdel.
It must return to Kazdel.
We must consider this matter carefully.
The damage the Sarkaz inflicted on Londinium is bound to stir a new wave of hatred—and without proper guidance, it could lead to an even darker nightmare.
This war must end, but Kazdel must also take measures to protect itself. We can go to Kazdel together and watch how they use Amnannam.

opts: This really is the best move, Kal'tsit.

lines.x: There are many things we need to discuss, but not here.
First, we must use Amnannam to leave this place. Give it a try, Doctor.

opts: Strange... it's not responding to me.

lines.x: Let me try. I can feel... a faint connection. I think it's responding to me.
The sealed passage reopens yet again.
Is it assaulting my thoughts?
Enough. We can't just keep using this thing haphazardly. We need to do more research on both its uses and its potential dangers.
Ascalon looks at the figure on the throne. In the darkness, she cannot make out his face.
Her eyes are a bit blurry.
I'll go with you.
I will respect his wishes. I don't need to witness his end.
Ascalon...
The Cautus hugs Ascalon tightly. Ascalon suddenly realizes that the frail Cautus she once found in the wilderness has grown up.
Let's go home together.
...Doctor?

opts: You all go ahead. I'll catch up.

lines.x: I'll stay here to protect you—
Mon3tr is staying here, and it should be sufficient. Theresis is no longer a threat.
We'll wait for you outside.
It's very quiet. All you can hear is dripping water. You silently creep closer to the man on the throne.
You do not fear death, Doctor?
Yes, that is assuredly true.
That is why you remain here and mock me?
Haha——*cough* Haha...
Such a prideful stance.
His voice is hoarse.

opts: You're forcing yourself to speak with me.

lines.x: You have won my respect, Doctor.
You're more determined than I thought.
But you are on... a suicide mission.

opts: I never imagined you could seize Amnannam.
You are heroes, in the eyes of the Sarkaz, but—

lines.x: *cough* Hahaha...
I'm laughing at you, Doctor.
You... know nothing.

opts: What did you find in the Assimilated Universe?

lines.x: That is my secret. You have no right to ask.
I have no desire to belittle Rhodes Island's yearning to heal the wounds caused by Originium.
Yet it is still so ironic, hahaha—
In the end, if you find that the real monster… is you—
Then what will you do, Doctor?
*cough* Alas, I will not live to see that day. You and I never really did get along.
It was so then, as it is now.
He grabs at his clothing. It seems like he's trying to find something.
There are deep cuts on his breastplate, wounds left by Ascalon.
He finally finds what he's looking for—the stone blade he always carries.
(Warning roar)
Calm down... monster of Kal'tsit.
He's just trying to throw you the blade.
It's Ascalon's.
Leave.
You are not worthy of witnessing my end.

lines.st: Directly below the Palace of Westhaleg throne room, on the lowest throne in Terra, the Regent of the Sarkaz accepts his fate, alone.

This first Victorian throne, which has been eroded by time and bears little resemblance to its original form, was moved here intact when this nomadic city was first built and became part of the foundation of the Palace of Westhaleg.
The king who built Londinium said, 'This symbolizes that Victoria's royal authority is everlasting, from beginning to end.'
According to official Victorian records, the Sarkaz mercenaries, led by an organization called Babel, withdrew from Londinium in October 1098.
However, the leader who ultimately directed all of this, Theresis, 'vanished without a trace.'

lines.loc: Throne Room, Palace of Westhaleg The End of Kazdel's 'Heroic Age'

lines.x: His ears can no longer hear anything, but the sound of the ocean waves floods his brain, just as it did before. This is the voice of the Assimilated Universe.
An endless stream of information crashes into his consciousness. It is frenzied and unclear; it is beyond his understanding.
Theresis has very little time left to think. His final shred of consciousness surrenders to the vast sea of data.
His consciousness comes to rest beside a lonely monster.
The ugly Teekaz, wearing a black crown on his head, walks out from the silvery mountains. He looks up at the vast starry sky.
The eternal starry sky. The wild, primal continent. From every corner of existence, countless lives look beyond the earth and stars—they all gaze up at the same universe.
He's not lonely. He feels more calm and peaceful than he's ever felt in his life.
At last, I see the past, as you once saw it...
As well as the future.
He wants to say something more, to call that name one last time, but he can no longer speak.
He's totally exhausted, but as he prepares to close his eyes, a faint white ray emerges from the darkness.
We insist that you know the parting words she left for you.
As Theresa left with the myriad souls… she heard your voice among the many who called out to her.
'Our time has finally come to an end.'
'Goodbye, Theresis.'
He strains to open his eyes. His field of vision grows ever darker.
But he recognized the figure in white some time ago, as soon as it first appeared.

lines.st: 'Goodbye, Theresa.'

lines.x: He doesn't need to hold on any longer.
He flashes a slight smile and allows his last wisp of consciousness to dissipate.
The light dispels the darkness.
The light brings forth the darkness.
When the light pours into his eyes once more, time seems to have gone backwards, back to the moment before the collapse.
(Unknown language) You are lost.

lines.st: 'Who speaks to me?'

lines.x: (Unknown language) It has been a long time since this place has seen a new visitor.
(Unknown language) Or are you the last survivor of the ruined planet?

lines.st: 'I remember... my consciousness was shattering...'
'And then... disintegration... death... but, why?'

lines.x: (Unknown language) In any case, welcome.
(Unknown language) Come. Seek me.
...I remember, a hand reached out to me, before I died.
This place—
The Cautus, who should have perished and returned to Terran soil, looks around in confusion.
She has never seen a place like this before. Light falls upon her, and she feels warm.
Where are we?
The land beyond death… the home of the gods?

eps.name: In the Name of a Miracle

lines.x: Bells...? Why am I hearing them ring from all directions?
Her voice is so soft that it does not even echo off the walls.
Whenever the foggy light comes, lots of tiny holes appear in the walls, opening and closing, breathing. When the light disappears, the walls turn white and silent.
That light... is very different from the Holy City's light that the Bishop talked about...
(Unknown language) That light is a simulated F spectrum.
I can hear you. How can I see you?
She presses her nails to the wall. It feels warm and soft, as if she's touching someone else's fingertips—
—so she places her whole palm against the wall, pressing closely to the wall's fine patterns and textures.
Wherever her hand moves, the patterns stretch along with it, as if they're quietly guiding her by the hand.
You're... responding to me?
She runs forward for a bit, and the wall extends with her, leading her.
There are so many doors and so many different paths here. Where do they all lead?
The Cautus stops walking. She sees a 'god' standing in midair, a short distance in front of her. Its silhouette is massive.

The surface of the god's body carries a murky sheen, unlike that of any metal. It nimbly arranges a vast array of glowing fragments into patterns. As the patterns change, the fragments shift color in turn.
But the girl notices that there is no halo on the 'god's' head. The midair colossus completely ignores her, gliding past without hesitation.
Another similar being approaches and stops in front of her.
It seems to be asking her a question.
I don't understand what you're saying.
The 'god' is quiet for a moment. Then it switches from its own language to another one and continues talking.
(Unknown language) Your use of language is primitive.
The 'god' extends its supple limbs to cover the Cautus's head. The Cautus nervously shuts her eyes.
(Unknown language) There's no need to fear. I am analyzing your brain frequency and will upload it to Anseta.
Language. You. Sufficient to communicate.
...Not yet vectorized, extrapolation, deduction. Language, temporary adjustment. Resonance, necessary.
I... can't understand you. Can you understand me?
Indubitably.
—Then are you... God?
The Cautus nervously asks the question that she is most desperate for an answer to.
'God', question. Definition, necessary.

The Bishop says that God is the embodiment of the Law and the Lord of the Holy City, who protects the sick and the dearly departed. But these things that he says were never recorded in the history books.
Forsake, god.
God, not, necessary.
If you aren't God, then who rescued me? Why have I been brought here?
Or is this Kingdom Come? Are you here to guide me into the Kingdom?
Questions. Too many.
Resonance, lacking. Communication, inefficient.
Raydistorter. I.
Partial answer. Show you.
The being who calls itself 'Raydistorter' uses its limbs to draw a window in the air and drag it to the nearest wall. The image in the porthole quickly fades from view.
A pale, eerie light wells up from nowhere, flooding the entire window. The image sharpens gradually—and the Cautus girl looks out the 'window'.
A shattered sphere floats in the dark expanse of space, dotted with countless stars. It is frozen at the moment of death.
All the pasts that have not had time to be told shatter into dust and are cast into the frozen void.
The dust particles find each other and circle around the former garden. They clump together into rings that float in the air.
The Cautus girl and Raydistorter 'sail' atop these dust rings into the dead silence.
We see. Sail. Salvage.
You, shudder. Fear?
The faraway things, the twinkling things, they look like eyes. It's really scary.
They're just stars, but they feel alien to me. There are too many of them; they are too close, too bright.
You're not scared?
Fear is unnecessary.
Fear, too limiting, too inadequate, to describe.
The Cautus looks through the window and sees that tiny holes have appeared in the walls of the ship.
A white light appears somewhere far, far in the distance. It starts growing, and growing until it covers up almost the entire window.
The blinding light caresses the surface of the land of death, covering and hiding everything from any would-be watchful eyes.
The Cautus girl looks directly at the light, as a fear is continually invading her heart, a fear she does not understand.
It's the pale light of a dying star.
A dead planet still orbits the star, and the great ship clinging to the star ring must endure its light and its wailing, again and again, without end.
See, yes?
Only, grave. No, 'Holy City'.
Only, cycles, only, laws. No... 'gods'.
Universe. Welcome.
You. Traveler. Name?
Hierda.
'I don't know when it started, but the nickname the clerics used to tease me with became my name.'

lines.st: Administrator permissions confirmed.
Unlocking restricted-access project: PRTS Repaired Audio Log #0422
Access restrictions unlocked.
Date: Unrecoverable
Play Log: Under-Development Area
Subject: Kal'tsit
The dust has settled in Londinium, but hopes for peace in Victoria seem dim.
How much time do we have? I know you're listening, PRTS.
I need you to do everything you can to restore monitoring the Cradle Process, in the middle decks' under-development area.
And... prepare to grant Closure access to my permissions.
Authorization—Abyss. Thank you.
There's a lot left to do. How much will I manage myself?
Playback finished. Erasing access log...
3... 2... 1... Log has been erased.
Administrator, do you want to unlock the next classified log?
Operation confirmed. Unlocking next log.
Three years after the Londinium incident

lines.x: (Sliding quickly)
(Proudly claiming credit)
Good work. It seems like Closure and Mechanist's upgrades were effective.
(Spins happily)
Hey. The repair work on this sector is nearly complete.
She feels a small body make contact with her back, hugging her neck and giggling.
Alanna knows the giggle well. Sometimes, it makes her happy, and sometimes it makes her anxious.
She takes the girl she loves so much up on her back.
Potlid, I smell cream stew!
Lanns, you're going to ruin the surprise!
It smells too good! I couldn't help myself!
You sure are happy. I guess the Doctor agreed?
Yes! I saw there was an opening for volunteer work in the Rim Billiton office, and I signed up as soon as I could.
The food I cook now can't cure a miner's Oripathy, sure, but our Rhodes Islander friends can, and I can fill their stomachs.
If Savage and Ray hadn't vouched for me, the Doctor might've rejected my application on the spot.
Well, it's my responsibility as your guardian to accompany you, obviously.
Savage and Ray are with me. No worries!
But even when I'm not around, you still have to eat right, Lanns. I've left a full week's worth of soups of the day in the kitchen—all your favorite flavors.
My colleagues in Logistics will keep an eye on you for me.
Haha. You're the boss, Potlid.
But will you really be able to stand leaving your friends?
(Exaggerated nod)
Yes. The Beepy's also a good friend of mine.
But lots of the people I know have applied to serve as backup in this place or that, and I'm not about to fall behind.
Everyone's looking forward to touring our new home after the mission is over. Lanns, you promised me that Rhodes is gonna look even more beautiful once the renovations are done!
(Shhh—Of course, I peeked at Closure's blueprints before.)
Also...
Also?
I was already brave enough to say goodbye to my family and set off on a long journey, just like my dad did. Lanns, you just wait for my return!
A giant cookie? Is this from Ceobe?
Lots of operators getting ready to head out to support posts got cookies.
'You gotta eat good when you're traveling.' Kay was really firm with me on that.
I just wonder if she's gonna end up eating most of the cookies before she gets to share them.
I set it up with Vulcan to bake a batch of cookies as quickly as possible, so Kay could pass them out to all the operators going on field missions.
When the time comes, I'll have Sonya go with Kay to distribute them.

Why—
Forget it. I'll just drag Leto along with me.
Haha. I'm fine as long as you give me the honey drink you owe me once we get there.
With a drink in my hand, I'm at your command, General Zima!
Gah. What a headache. All this talking-to-people shit... This is Natalya's work.
But Rosa can't be everywhere at once—she has a lot of specific plans to go over with Amiya too, you know.
This trip's going to be a bit more trouble than usual—after all, we're going to—
(In Ursine) Ursus.
Or did you give the task of planning with Amiya to Rosa because you were afraid you couldn't do it?
Who said I couldn't do it? It's just—
Ursus, the place they all left together.
So much time has passed since then. Zima believes they are ready to go back and face the place once more.
Gummy, what's wrong with your hand?
N-Nothing, Zima. It's just a little sore, is all. We had them baking too many cookies. The bakers in the canteen got worn out.
Even Kay, waiting right outside the door, had to get dragged in to help like a good girl.
Don't worry about me, Zima. She wants to fill the bellies of the operators who took good care of her and are now leaving home. I feel the same way.
We'll do our part as well.
Anyways, Natalya also needs to spend some time—
You need help? Count me in.
See, Little Cub? Look what I can whip up in the kitchen.
Instructor Dur-nar?
The Doctor showed me your application. I took the liberty of applying to travel with you—as your 'guardian.'
Truth is, the Doctor was too worried about sending you back. I'm worried enough myself.
Zima...?
Welcome!
Rosa, the Doctor didn't want to approve your application, at first.
Medical assessed all of you and recommended that we be a bit more careful with your itinerary.
But, in the end, the Doctor respected our wishes?
That's because of Zima.
She burst into the Doctor's office and vowed to protect you, promising your safe return to Rhodes Island.
Just like before.
I kept them alive in Chernobog, I got us through it. This time's no different.

opts: This isn't just about your safety.
This encounter with Ursus will be very different from before.

lines.x: Natalya and I have it worked out—If we operate under her family's Rostov Industries, we'll have an easier time moving around.

opts: I'm not worried about your status.
Rhodes Island has ways to deal with things like that.
The assessment says you aren't fully prepared yet.
There are other logistics tasks you can choose.
There are always other ways you can help Rhodes Island.

lines.x: We're all nervous about going back, at least a bit. But we can't keep hiding from it.
This isn't some 'journey home for self-healing.' We looked over the risks and volunteered because we think we're a good fit for operations in Ursus.
We decided to call Rhodes Island our home a long time ago.
So I can guarantee I'll bring them back home, safe and sound.
Sonya has always been braver than me.
Amiya, as far as the details of our collaboration with Rostov Industries, I'd appreciate it if Dr. Kal'tsit and the Doctor could take a closer look.
I believe this will only be beneficial to our operations.
Consider it done, Rosa.
However, I still recommend that you avoid conducting any operations in Chernobog for now.
Ursus is still patrolling the area as closely as ever, and your identities are still an issue.
We'll stay in contact with the ship—there are still many aspects of the Ursine operation that will be up to you to assess.
Have a safe trip, Rosa.
The ship will be sending additional Logistics and Medical staff to accompany you—not only to support your operation, but also to explore the possibility of establishing a permanent office in Ursus.
By the way, someone else came to find me after your travel plans were announced. She hopes to join you on your journey.
Oh?
(Stomach growling)
Gummy, put the pot down. You eat your fill first!
Huh? I haven't gone hungry in a very long time.
(Stomach growling)
Hehe. Maybe I was just too busy today and forgot to eat, Istina.
You and Zima are here, so I'll be fine.
Then get to eating, you hear me?
Open wide, Gummy.
Mm-mm, I'm full, Leto!
Come to think of it, do you have anything you need us to bring, Zima? Personal thingies? I brought some big crates with me.
Well, let's bring the guitar Natalya gave me. Don't want her asking about it later.
Anything else, just leave it at home. Not like we're never coming back.
Leto, didn't you say we weren't taking anything?
That reminds me—I've got some junk to give to someone. Mind if we swing by Kjerag on the way, Sonya?
Should be fine. I'll submit my travel plan to the Doctor.
Excuse me, might I join you on your trip to Ursus?
Sorry, I know this request is sudden...
But I...
Sonya, let Zoya come with us.
This rescue mission will be a long one. We can take care of each other along the way.
You already know, Natalya?
I talked to Rosa about it.
Haha. Don't sweat it, Zoya. This isn't our first joint trip.
Ceobe, wait, that cookie is still a bit raw in the middle!
Huh?
No! Stop her! She'll give herself a tummyache!
Huh?
(Politely stops her)
I'm gonna be late!
(Spins angrily)
Kay made so many cookies.
Those cookies should go to very, very good people.
Lots of wonderful people are setting out on a journey. They took good care of Kay at home, so Kay wants to make sure they don't go hungry.
That's the deal.
Doctor, the copy rooms on each floor have been packed with people these past few days.

This round of field deployments, going out to so many different regions, is the perfect opportunity for us to pick up local souvenirs—the wishlists everyone's been making are long enough to start a whole Terran Expo.
In the past, Closure has suggested that the Procurement Department make unified purchases for the operators, but I rejected the idea.
Let's keep the spirit of the tradition alive. Procurement will be sending out a notice soon regarding reimbursement.
Do you need me to bring you back anything from my trip to Victoria?
Kal'tsit shakes her head. She looks at the cookies Folinic is holding with a bit of suspicion.
Ceobe shoved these cookies at me just now.
She just made a gigantic mess in the kitchen, and Amiya is still trying to help clean it up.
Poor Amiya.
Folinic, you're going to have your hands full on this trip to Victoria.
In addition to recovering data from several offices in the ducal territories, you'll have to monitor Na Saoirsí and Reed.
You'll have to coordinate with all the different offices to provide her as much support as possible.
Of course.
And Londinium?

Let Heidi know that we are temporarily delaying our return to the ship for debriefing. Ever since Londinium's Parliament restored contact with the outside world, it has resumed collecting messages from the Core nations.
The intelligence network that Heidi has been cultivating is extremely important. This is also why I am sending you to provide support.
So overall, things aren't really that great? I see. I'll pay close attention to all of this.
Folinic, you need to take it easier.
Wha?
You have a habit of overloading yourself.
I saw your itinerary. You're stretching yourself too thin. I deleted a few of the tasks.
Try not to dwell on the things you fear—and don't try and force yourself to take control of them, either.
Folinic, I never expected you to be like your mother... or me.
I don't support encouraging those who have experienced suffering to forget the hatred and violence they've endured. If we want to build a better future, we mustn't surrender.
Try to slow down your pace a bit, Folinic. Wanting to have more control over your life is good, but getting all anxious about it is just tantamount to self-torture.
It's a long road to Victoria, but I need you to remember the smile you had when you first walked in this room.
This is my one and only requirement for you.
I will remember that.
I'll pick out a souvenir for you myself when I get to Victoria.
Farewell, Dr. Kal'tsit.
Red, you don't need to hide.
I was afraid you would be angry.
You told me not to be sad about Siracusa... but I made you sad.
I don't want to leave Rhodes Island this time. I'm afraid you will force me.
I haven't prepared the transfer order for S.W.E.E.P. yet, Red.
Stay here on standby. Ascalon's orders will arrive soon.
After we receive the orders, can I stay by your side?
Yes. Didn't you arrive some time ago?
Yes. I arrived a while ago.
Aunt Jemma and Mum prepared these canned golden radishes for me to hand out to my colleagues.
Sorry. There might be a few too many of them. Should we maybe store them in the kitchen first?

opts: There's a veritable mountain of cans here.

lines.x: I'll leave them with you.
All this stuff happened all of a sudden. It's been too long since I've been home. Mum's stockpile of canned goods is getting out of control. She just keeps dumping them on me over and over.

opts: Didn't you just tell me they were prepared specifically for our colleagues?

lines.x: Gah... Yes. Right.
Anyways, they're all mine. I'll decide how they're used.
Doctor, might I trouble you to help me distribute these to our colleagues, when you see them? Warmy's on a tight schedule, so I don't have time to do it myself.
Oh, right. The two cans with names on them... I saved them for you and Amiya. Don't give them out by mistake!

opts: They look more or less the same.

lines.x: —You... Of course they're not the same!
Oh right. I'll also need to take thirty cans on my own trip.
No. As gifts.
Warmy and Ray have an old friend, Jerry. Ever since he married Keira, his career has really taken off.
I saw the last photo he sent. His family has been blessed with quite a litter of kids lately. Warmy made plans with us to stop and visit them on the way.
And we haven't really changed much, eh? Well, Amiya sure does seem like she's matured a lot, ever since she returned from Kazdel.

opts: Please take good care of Warmy and Ray.
Please take care of yourself as well.

lines.x: I'm well aware of the situation in Rim Billiton. I'll make sure they're cared for.
Don't worry, Doctor.
I look forward to seeing you again after the field missions are complete.
Still, this field assignment covers a pretty wide area—I'm afraid it'll be quite a while before I can sleep soundly in my own bed again.

opts: Don't worry. Rhodes Island will wait for you to return.

lines.x: Righto!
Keep the lights on for me, Doctor.
Hey. What are you still doing here?
I just thought of something. We still haven't been given our medicine/health maintenance manuals yet. We need this guidance so that we can remember our health regimens.
I mean, this is going to be a long trip abroad. Will we all remember to eat our meals and take our medicines?
The medic operators will take care of all that. Why are you worried about it?
But we often do forget to take proper care of ourselves. You yourself, Lava, don't you forget to take your food rations with you, sometimes?
I... uhh... meh... whatever.
Then there's the other members of A1. The squad is going to be taking on some dangerous tasks. Kroos has to go on a long trip all by herself. Will they be okay?
Ugh... Enough already! Will you quit treating them like children?
We've been at this for so long. We're all adults already!
I know... I get it...
It's just that—leaving a place I've spent so many years with everyone, day in and day out… and not knowing when I'll be back—it's a lot to take in.
I just feel a bit... empty inside.
Don't worry. We'll be back.
Rhodes Island is dealing with some stuff right now. I trust the Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit, and Amiya can handle it all.
All we have to do is complete our own tasks and be patient.
Rhodes Island is our home, and we're not gonna lose our home. No matter what happens, we'll be back.
Someday.
Right...
We'll be together again.
Your body temp hasn't come down. It seems like the old cocktail of drugs is no longer effective.
However, your metabolic rate has not been impacted too much. After the concentration of the old drugs in your bloodstream goes down, I'll administer some new medications.
These new drugs will decrease your pain threshold, but don't worry, I'll up your pain meds.
Alright.
There are a few new empty beds?
They left yesterday. Don't worry. They've just been transferred elsewhere for more targeted temporary treatment.
By the way, a few people left some notes for you. They're in your bedside drawer.
Your condition is rather unusual, and we should keep you in a controlled treatment environment. Staying here with me under my observation is the best option.
Okay. Thank you.
What are you looking at, Chloe?
There are little circles on the ceiling that keep spinning. Looking at them makes me feel safe.
The crystals are still interfering with your vision. My next surgery will be an attempt to remove the crystals near your retinas.
Every time I stare at them, they look like they've crept just a bit closer. It's like they're talking to me.
Can you hear them?
(Closes eyes) Their sound... it's like that old bell I used to hear.
You mean the one you told me about before? From the bell tower in your hometown?
There's more than just one bell tower. I was on a quest to repair all of them, and had already repaired quite a few... when they were all destroyed by cannon fire.
I reached out to the operators we've got in Victoria—they looked into it for you. The town's been rebuilt, and the locals even repaired that old bell.
That's great news! Now that the bell's tolling again, life can continue on. Quite a relief, isn't it?
Warfarin double-checks the instrument readings one last time, then injects the prepared medicine into the IV system.
The buzzing of the instruments mixes together with the noise of the IV system and Chloe's occasional gasps of pain.
Dr. Warfarin, how many different types of medicine have we tried over the last three years?
Let me worry about that. All your vital indicators have stabilized. That's what's really important here.
Before I contracted Oripathy, I often cared for infected soldiers in the bell tower. I know exactly what it means when the crystals start spreading across your skin.
Chloe tries to move the muscles in her face, but the tiny spikes embedded in her skin twist her expression. Her wounds split open, and bright beads of blood begin to seep out.
(Wry smile) You see? They won't even let me smile.
As a doctor, I cannot guarantee outcomes. I can only promise that every medic in Rhodes Island will provide you the best possible care.
Thank you, Dr. Warfarin.
I just... miss my hometown.
This report, it—!
The first time I saw this patient's charts, I thought there was some kind of error in the data then too.
But we checked the data several times, and it was correct.
By all logic, no patient with this high a Cell-Originium Assimilation rate should still show any signs of life.
It goes against everything we know about Oripathy.
And yet, the Originium is keeping the patient alive, in a way no one could have predicted.
Some of her internal organs have completely crystallized, but they're actually functioning more efficiently and stably than those of previous patients we've seen.
I haven't figured out what's causing this condition yet, but based on the data—the more her infection progresses, the healthier she seems… and the more pain she's in.
We've never seen a case like this before. This may be some kind of new mutated form of Oripathy. If we can make some sort of medical breakthrough while administering her treatment...
Maybe these people we rescued from Victoria really can bring a few miracles to Rhodes Island.
Closure, I have the right to refuse the data module inspections and upgrades you're proposing.
I will not be treated like THRM-EX and Lancet-2. You know very well that I am different from my 'colleagues'.
Haha. Relax. I'm not trying to trick you into a module upgrade this time.
Then please restore the power supply to my mobility module. Restricting a respected scholar's personal freedom is really unbecoming behavior.
Take a look at the expression doodle on my faceplate. It indicates how I feel.
'Angry'.
This won't take too much time. Justice Knight is already lined up waiting at the entrance.
Mr. Friston, I guarantee this maintenance will be quick.
Alright. There we are.
You've blocked me from accessing PRTS data?
Blocked you? No. I just disconnected you from PRTS. To be more precise, I physically removed your reception module.
(It looks like that went well enough. I thought there would be some irreparable failures.)
You just did that, without any sort of planning?!
Haha. Well, how do you feel now, Mr. Friston?
I recorded the entire experiment. You violated protocol, and I'll be discussing your negligence with Kal'tsit shortly.
Not to mention your reckless disregard for my life. She'll hear about that too.
Mr. Friston, please do one more self-check and to be certain you're not getting any transmissions from PRTS.
You should just be thankful nothing went wrong. I checked, and I indeed cannot connect to PRTS any longer.
Ugh... it's like I have blurred vision. It's a very subtle feeling, and I don't like it one bit.
Perfect!
Now we can talk, Mr. Friston.
If I wanted to disconnect all the retrofitted operation platforms from PRTS, would there be a safer way to do that?
I refuse to speculate about such dangerous work. That would be no different than killing all of my precious 'colleagues'.
What if... we had no other choice?
Please tell me what might cause you to draw such a conclusion.
Have you discovered something?
Closure, can you determine the root cause of the last few PRTS operation errors?
The logs show that most of the errors were caused by human error, operational mistakes made by team members.
This again?
Either way, backup systems are being deployed as quickly as possible.
The framework for independent terminal command system has been upgraded as per Kal'tsit's suggestions. I've named it ZOOT.
Temporary admin permissions have been given to the Doctor, Kal'tsit, and Amiya.
And me, of course. A lot of the ideas I had when I first designed this system feel a bit outdated now.
I really want to scrap it and start over, but Kal'tsit shot down that application!

opts: We don't have much time left.
Can we seamlessly switch to the new terminal command system?

lines.x: I... I'm still working on that.
We still have no way to replace PRTS's current role in Rhodes Island's daily operations. Much of our medical data and resource allocation calculations still rely on PRTS.
But the biggest issue I'm facing is how to construct a data center and energy hub reliable enough to support the new command system.
Nothing comes close to what PRTS can do here—even while handling the massive, high-precision computations Rhodes Island requires, it still has power to spare.
Unfortunately, I still can't make sense of PRTS's underlying logic… Even ZOOT, which completely mimics its design, keeps throwing errors in weird places you wouldn't think of.
What if we used Amnannam?
Closure, I reviewed the comments you added to my report, and the theories you outlined in response.
According to the Kazdel office's logs and experimental results, there is—at least in theory—a possibility that Amnannam could function as a data center and energy hub.
But...

opts: We still aren't totally familiar with Amnannam.

lines.x: But it's not like we haven't learned anything from all this.
At least we verified that there's a possibility of using Amnannam for super-long-range communications and data transfers.
Even though she'd been informed in advance, Wiš'adel was still freaked out when your 'greeting' showed up out of nowhere.

opts: And the operators who went to assist Kazdel, have they settled in alright?

lines.x: Everyone was surprised when they first got there. They assumed the city would be in... worse shape.
They found their footing quickly, and when the time came for Midnight and me to head back, it was hard to say goodbye to the new friends he'd made.
Doctor, do we really have to keep sending people out to the various branch offices?
I've never seen the ship as deserted as it is now.
We've come to a mutual understanding, Closure.

We're sending more personnel to the regional offices partly to reduce the number of people at risk in the event of an accident aboard the ship, and partly to ensure those offices can respond more effectively when needed.
I know that, of course. I'm just not used to having to say goodbye to friends all of a sudden.
I don't know when it started, but my schedule is packed with farewell dinners now.
Once the risks aboard the ship are cleared, it'll be up to you to decide when the operators return.
If everyone is here, then we'll start the meeting.
Also, don't forget to delete the voice recordings and data logs from today—wipe them from PRTS.
From here on out, every decision we make will shape the future of Rhodes Island.
We've reached a consensus: if we want to guarantee the safety of Rhodes Island's people and assets, there are certain decisions we can't avoid any longer.
Yes. We need to segregate Rhodes Island's important resources and help the personnel stationed on the ship settle in.
This will help ease the concerns of those stationed on the ship while also improving everyone's response time in the event of an emergency.
Uh, but I still have some questions, Kal'tsit.
This means we'll have to dismantle the vast majority of the ship's structure.
Dr. Kal'tsit, did you accelerate Rhodes Island's defense planning only because of PRTS's recent irregularities?
The recent irregularities have made one thing clear—
PRTS is slowly spiraling out of control.
And as time goes on, this will only accelerate.
However, PRTS is only Rhodes Island's terminal control system.
If it's no longer in our control, then who is it taking orders from?
You're worried about internal threats?
Amiya, that possibility has always existed.
...I trust you, Dr. Kal'tsit.
Phew. What a headache. What do you think, Doctor?

opts: She and I have already rehearsed hundreds of contingency plans.

lines.x: Okay. Then what can I do, Kal'tsit?
You've already played a big role in the ship's reconstruction.

The mechanical structures connecting the different core areas have been almost completely replaced with cyclic hydrocarbon prefabricated materials over the past five years, which can support a certain degree of modular disassembly.
The power systems on the lower deck and the uppermost deck cannot be separated for now.
When Rhodes Island was first excavated, the limitations of available technology and other constraints prevented us from restoring it to its ideal condition.

It wasn't until seven years ago, when Closure and I drafted the blueprints for the restoration, that we discovered it would be very hard to modify Rhodes Island's structure. I'm pleasantly surprised that we've managed to do as much as we did.
The Catastrophe warning system and the medical center are particularly crucial modules. Both involve technologies that are not yet ready to be disclosed and are also the hardest to deploy.
We have a close working relationship with Rhine Lab. Appointing them to take care of the warning system and medical center was an obvious choice.
But Mark Max has long maintained a high level of interest in Rhodes Island, and I can't predict how the President of Columbia will view the collaboration between our two companies.
In addition, the situation in Trimounts is still complicated; I am afraid that Rhine Lab might not be able to shield itself from the fallout.
I have an idea...
It's risky, but well warranted.
Leveraging Clementia's and Gladiia's private channels, we won't even have to establish contact with the Ægir authorities. I can ensure this matter is handled discreetly and properly.
We might even be able to conduct technology transfers. I reckon that would make Warfarin happy.
That is the path I believe we should take, Doctor.
The Londinium Parliament's reconstruction work these past two years has been difficult, but the results have been surprisingly impressive.
Not only have they cleared most of the Originium crystal clusters from their route, they've also managed to stabilize the city's supply of medicine and other resources.
Londinium can now indeed provide a secure operating environment for the medical center, and we'll also be able to obtain a large amount of firsthand case data.
But this also means we'll be officially cooperating with Parliament again, and the dukes will once more put heavy pressure on Vina.
That's not exactly good news for Londinium, which has only just begun to get back on track.
Doctor, I have another risky idea.
Ægir. Looking at all of the factors, this is the best choice we can make.
I will personally communicate with the Inquisition and Ægir personnel to ensure the safe arrival of our own personnel and assets.
Most of our patients have now been temporarily placed in treatment facilities in various offices along with reinforcements.
All that is left is for the remaining patients to be transferred to Ægir once the medical center is operational again.
Leave this matter to me. The support vehicle for long-distance patient transport is already prepped and ready to go.
As for the transfer arrangements for Logistics and Engineering...
I talked it over with my people, and everyone was enthusiastic about temporarily moving to Kazdel.
Many of them are old friends who stuck with us back then, and returning to Kazdel means a lot to them.
Yes. I talked to Wiš'adel before leaving Kazdel.

Babel will assign personnel to escort both our people and our key assets throughout the entire journey. We'll be flying the banners of Babel and the Military Commission. The mercenaries won't dare try anything.

opts: And then there's our Mining Module.
The militarized mining modules need to be handled with caution.

lines.x: A long time ago, I purchased a semi-decommissioned mining ship from Rim Billiton and registered it under Rhodes Island's name.
The ship's mining module can now be legally transferred to the new mining vessel to continue operations in Rim Billiton.
At the same time, I placed an order on the market to buy the mining module.
Making any major changes, in the short term, is sure to attract attention, especially from our rivals, who will use the opportunity to use them against us.
While calling it a 'refit and upgrade' may be something most factions will accept, anybody with their own plans—the ones watching us more closely—won't be fooled.
We need a few more sophisticated explanations.
I stopped in Siracusa to resupply before returning to the ship, where I visited Vigil and Penance in Nuova Volsinii.
They helped me dig into the current state of affairs in Nuova Volsinii.
As long as the famiglie are not publicly pressuring Nuova Volsinii, City Hall will be able to protect us.

I propose inserting a business department into Nuova Volsinii by means of investment. Establishing a cooperative relationship with City Hall will counter both overt and covert maneuvers from competing companies.
Doctor, what do you think?

opts: Perhaps we can establish some backup warehouses or supply stores.

lines.x: We can build a backup warehouse in Nuova Volsinii as well.
The logistics system of Nuova Volsinii has changed a lot in the past year, and the port's cargo throughput is extremely impressive.
With City Hall's cooperation, it will be easier for us to conceal the transportation and storage of important assets.
Doctor, let me handle coordination for the rest of the support facilities.
There is one last painful task on the list...
Everyone looks at the blueprints spread out in front of them, as the command center falls silent.
This is the key to everything we're doing.
Kal'tsit points to a region in the center of the blueprints.
Abyss.
On the blueprint, a new area has been added to what was once a blank space in the central section of the mid-deck—
Abyss.
Kal'tsit has never told you what this Abyss really means.
But you still have a vague feeling that this area marked on the blueprint, this area that has never been recorded by PRTS—
—Is inextricably linked to you.
A long time has passed since it was last opened.
Even back then, when I had to get close to that place to bring back someone important, I didn't dare go near the deeper part, the one that's been sealed away all this time.
However, now I'm not even sure what kind of changes have happened in there.
As I just said, if the PRTS abnormalities have a specific cause, then the Abyss must be involved somehow.
How do you think we should deal with this section, Dr. Doctor?

opts: I need to go there and investigate.

lines.x: Dr. Kal'tsit, I can go with—
I'll bring the Doctor there myself. Amiya, Closure, I need you two to make sure the surrounding areas are completely cleared out.
Set up the first isolation zone around the Abyss itself, and a second within one kilometer of the ship.
Ascalon and the elite operators will stay aboard on standby. I need all other personnel on board to temporarily leave the ship and prepare for countermeasures.
Amiya, you and Closure must leave temporarily as well. Be prepared to activate our contingency plans at any time to deal with unexpected situations.
How long do we have? A mass migration like this will be hard to get done in a short period of time.
Fifteen days.
During this period, you need to complete a full body data scan and comparative analysis, Dr. Doctor.
Dr. Kal'tsit, what is actually in the Abyss, anyway?
The beginning of everything, and the end of everything.

opts: Kal'tsit, I want to go to the Abyss and see for myself.

lines.x: Kal'tsit seems to know why you want to do this, and gives a slight nod.
Alright.
But please keep in mind: until we are fully prepared, we can conduct preliminary explorations on the periphery.
We can't go any deeper. I can't tell if getting closer to the core area of the Abyss will cause some unexpected changes.
We are still some distance from the actual Abyss. I also don't have permission to open the way to the Abyss.
I'm still trying, but to this point, I've made very little progress.

opts: So you're saying this is where I first woke up on Terra?

lines.x: Physically...? Yes, but your situation is a bit more... complicated than that.
Does coming here bring back any specific memories?

opts: Is this some sort of test?
Is this some sort of joke?

lines.x: Kal'tsit shakes her head.
I just want to confirm that you are in good health.
After withdrawing from Londinium, our operations in Trimounts and Milliarium inevitably forced us to confront the history of this land… and your history.

opts: In the Originium Assimilated Universe, I caught a glimpse of my mission.

lines.x: —The savior of this land.
—The destroyer of this land.

opts: Kal'tsit, I cannot avoid it, nor do I have any reason to.

lines.x: Doctor, I stopped questioning your resolve long ago—which is the only reason we're here now.

opts: Kal'tsit, I have a theory about the true nature of the Abyss.

lines.x: This is why you chose to leave Amiya and Closure out of this conversation.

opts: I still haven't figured out how to tell them.
After all, I don't fully understand it yet, either.

lines.x: Yes, but we would have had to prepare for it anyway, sooner or later.
Amiya is still young, but every new trial we face helps her mature a little bit as a leader.
You can always rely on Closure's knowledge and skill, and she's taking more responsibility over Rhodes Island as well.
They are all worthy of your trust and reliance. They will stand by your side for a long time to come.

opts: But right now, we need to figure out—

lines.x: Our experience in the Assimilated Universe left us with a many unanswered questions—and ever since Theresa and Theresis joined forces to seize the first Originium…
I've felt uneasy about what's to come.
Perhaps she had already awakened when Amnannam arrived.

opts: But that was three years ago.

lines.x: I cannot be completely certain.
It is very likely that Theresis used some method I cannot trace to cut off the controller from the Originium.
I'm afraid 'delayed' is not the most apt description for it.
Nonetheless, I must admit that they did something I never imagined they'd do, and, objectively speaking, they bought us more time.
However, we will eventually awaken from our beautiful dream.

opts: It sounds like we have to open the Abyss.

lines.x: Indeed. After all, PRTS is still operational to this day. Its core server and power sources are buried within the depths of the Abyss. We can't touch them or analyze them.
We can never be too cautious when dealing with such an unknown space.
I'll share everything I currently know about it with everyone involved in the operation.

opts: Can I help you with anything?

lines.x: No… For the version of you that exists now, even approaching these hazy fragments of the past takes a heavy toll, and right now, I have no way to help you shoulder that burden.
Go help Amiya and the others—leave the Abyss to me.
If you choose to trust me, that is.

opts: I just want you to know...

lines.x: Thank you for your concern, Doctor.
...I understand.

lines.st: Visitor: Kal'tsit
Hello, Kal'tsit, we meet again.
You look like you need my help.

lines.x: I do.
Please search for a technical document, keyword 'Slumber Bridge'.
Are you still there?

lines.st: I'm here.
Sorry, Kal'tsit, I still can't output the answer you're looking for.
You have crossed the line.

lines.x: I know.
But we need it, 'Theresa'...
The evolution rate of Oripathy is accelerating, and the threat from Originium is increasing. Rhodes Island must find a more effective method to respond to it.

lines.st: I get what you're saying, but I cannot provide what you're asking for, as your request violates the underlying logic of DWDB-221E.
But I do have a way to circumvent its limits, Kal'tsit.
'Deep Dive'.

lines.x: 'Universe'? 'Deep Dive'?
What you're saying and what I've seen.... it's all beyond my imagination.
I have just far too many questions and opinions about this Kingdom, but...
Language. Yours. Cannot describe. Mine. Cannot explain.
Commonality. Thought. Existence.
Hack your thought. Hack your language.
Thought. Is. Language.
Follow me.
The massive instrument instantly catches the Cautus's attention.
Her vision swims; the instrument distorts the light, and her consciousness.
To the Cautus, it sometimes appears to spin faster than her eye can follow—other times, it appears completely still.
No, it, it still appears to be moving, very slowly... very slowly.
She stops looking directly at the device that seems capable of devouring her consciousness, yet she still can't stop herself from falling over.
The ice-cold co-traveler catches her.
Thought. Resonance.
Accept. It. Forsake. Resistance.
Hear. Yourself.

lines.st: 'Death'.

lines.x: The Cautus instantly hears several different tones at once.

She hears several foreign languages she can identify: Rim Billitonian, Ursine, Standard Victorian—and Gaulish, which she's only ever heard spoken by the clergy. But what she hears above all else is a strange language she's never heard before, a language made up of tones.
Yet somehow, she understands what the strange tones mean.
(Strange tone) 'Death'.
(Strange tone) ♫
I, I can understand you... how—
Thoughts can be anchored, and words can be deciphered.
Language is a tool, a right, and one of the key elements that helps shape our existence.
Anseta is one of the greatest inventions of our time. We've erased the barriers of language—we've bridged the gaps between civilizations.
That symbol...
An anchor for thought, the meta-script that deciphers all known languages, and a tool for interpreting unknown languages.
'Death'.
That is the language that, up until now, you have thought the most about, Hierda.
'Reincarnation'.
That is how you all communicate...?
Communicating via voice is inefficient. However, it is very intriguing and a good way to express emotion.
Many still stubbornly hold on to this tradition, deepening their emotional bonds with one another.
...I don't fully understand you.
Can you tell me where we are right now?
A ship that salvages death from the grave.
We seek answers to the ultimate questions among the ruins illuminated by light... before the same destruction befalls us.
Before 'death' befalls us.
The place you have visited, its master calls it...
Rhodes Island.
The Cautus is confused.
She's no longer confused by the strangeness of her surroundings. She's perplexed by a different question:
How can a god die?

lines.st: 'Time'.

lines.x: You asked me why the first 'language' I shared with you after our thoughts resonated was 'death'...
I really don't know either.
Aboard this strange, colossal vessel called 'Rhodes Island,' the Cautus can barely feel the passage of time.
It makes her uneasy. Ever since the day she became Infected, time has meant everything to her.
Time will bring her death—and perhaps, liberation.
However, the beings who dwell in this strange place—yet deny they are gods—seem utterly indifferent to it.
Maybe it's because I've never been afraid of dying. Death is nothing more than a passage to a new environment.
(Rim Billitonian) Pessimist.
That's how they would describe you in your own language, yes?
A pessimist? I don't think so.

The Cautus blurts it out, and regrets it—just a little. She had agreed with many of the clergy's arguments back during those debates… but now that she's here, does she still need to think in those terms?
Does she really care about everything she experienced before the collapse?
After all, even time has forsaken her now.
I once worked in a mine that seemed to be endless. To maintain efficiency, the overseer would kill or drive off any bothersome Originium slugs.
Originium slugs? A mine?
Yes. Those Originium slugs are even weaker than me, and the mines... the mines were once my home.
But the overseer didn't expect that the kindest worker in the mine was secretly feeding and caring for the Originium slugs they were trying to drive away—which only drew more of them into the mine.
Eventually, the slugs disrupted the mine's foundation, leading to a collapse.
But you survived?
The slugs' secretions fed me.
In such a harsh environment, many of them lived short lives, yet new ones kept being born. They gave me strength—
Miners, Originium slugs, and yourself—but your story ignores the 'death of Terra,' Hierda.
You do not treasure your 'Terra'.
That's not true at all! In our language, 'Terra Bless Us' is a phrase that invokes something almost divine—
Hmm... you still have a very limited definition of 'life'.
I don't understand. You've shown me so many individuals, each starkly distinct from the next… You all feel love and hate just as we do—and yet, are still vastly different from each other.
'Salvaging death from the grave'—you all live united by a purpose that I can't understand.
But why have I come here?
The Cautus ponders this question, but her intuition tells her the answer lies with the person who just extended a hand to her.

She looks out the porthole at the top of the cabin into the cold, silent, endless expanse of space. The light from the distant, giant sphere once again passes over the dead planet, piercing the Cautus's eyes.
However, despite her blurry vision, she spots a small silhouette within the light.
There is... someone out there, in deep space?
Is that the one I'm looking for?
Someone out in the depths of space seems to hear the Cautus's voice—a shadow within the light stirs ever so slightly.
