(Unknown language) You're looking for me?
One circle, two circles, the little dots are spinning in the darkness.
They're accelerating, coming closer and closer.
They rip a hole in the darkness, and a bright, blazing beam of light shines through.
I extend my arms and embrace them.
Core body temperature is elevated; nerve blocker has already been administered.
Body temperature rising rapidly. Prepare to administer muscle relaxant and replenish blood volume.
Repeat the medication again, and let's start the operation—
Dr. Warfarin?
Did I doze off again? Maybe I'm just really exhausted.
Don't try and speak now. Your throat is badly injured.
You feel like you went to sleep?
Yes, and I had a long dream.
You didn't feel any other discomfort?
Erm... no. Did something happen just now, Dr. Warfarin?
Nothing major. Did you dream about anything... special?
Anything special? That's hard to say.
In my dreams there were balls of mud spinning around each other on black velvet...
They are now big, now small. One moment they were smaller than children's marbles, the next larger than the whole of Victoria.
Someone picked them up and flung them back out. They started smashing into each other and bursting like fireworks, but the whole time, I didn't hear a sound.
Anything else...?
I also saw threads—stretching from one ball of mud to another, each one made of light. And on every thread, there were people—running outward, racing along the light.
I followed them. I ran to a white patch of earth. It was completely empty...
Save for a single stone tablet standing there.
Stone tablet? What was written on it?
Nothing, Dr. Warfarin, and that's when I woke up.
What a strange dream, but then again, you remember it quite clearly, which means your brain's memory module is still functioning properly.
The more clearly you remember your dream, the closer you were to waking up when you dreamed it. As I see it, the fact that you were able to remember your dream is a very good thing.
Say, I haven't seen Dr. Touch today...
She has other matters to attend to. She told me to give this to you. Look.
Huh? The spring, the gears... the hands... all the parts that make up a clock!
She said it would help relieve your stress.
Dr. Warfarin, is Rhodes Island in some kind of trouble again?
Little one, you've come to the wrong place.
(Unsurprisingly, Beepy got lost.)
(Hiding in fear)
It seems like Closure failed to examine your program modules.
Little one, this item here should definitely not be on your maintenance list.
The Goliath lifts it gently and examines it curiously in his hands.
(Angrily spins treads)
What a huge change. It was empty when Her Highness left.
Now, there are so many interesting people and things here, right?
Ascalon.
Tell them not to worry. I'll take care of the mobility layer.
Okay.
Ascalon, you seem in poorer spirits than you were when you returned from Victoria.
What about all the people working under you?
They have been dispatched to create the Isolation Zone outside the ship. I'm remaining on this ship, and that's sufficient.
Rhodes Island does indeed need a blade that moves through the darkness, but that doesn't mean that you need to consciously remain silent.
Come chat with me when you have time. I have liquor here.
Besides, I want you to tell me about Theresis.
I witnessed the beginning of that heroic epic, and you witnessed its end. What we saw was very different.
Okay.
I will convey your thoughts to the Doctor and Amiya when they have time.
The Goliath places 'Beepy' in front of the exit passage.
Stop wandering around—the mobility layer is dangerous. Don't underestimate what high-concentrations of Originium can do to someone like you.
I'll keep my eye on the work here, and that'll be enough.
(Runs away in a panic)
Dame Kal'tsit, what could've gotten you this tense?

lines.st: PRTS Operation Record: [Confidential] Escort Catastrophe warning system and medical center core components
Executor: [Confidential] Mechanist, Logos, Raidian
Contact: (Records erased)

lines.x: Situation abnormal. Enemy weapons technology exceeds mission parameters. Risk of exposure present.
Possible enemy signal detection capability. Shutting down drone transmission temporarily.
Logos, I'm counting on you for the drone's live feed.
I'm ready.
'Circuits.' Etched into Originium through spoken words—drawn by hand to reveal what the swarm perceives.
Logos's bone pen hovers in the air, automatically transcribing the images recorded by the drone.
Twelve flexible vehicles buried in the sand. Thirty-one armed militants.
The five sniper's nests have already been marked. There seems to be a signal isolation device in the tent, which the scanner cannot penetrate.
All are carrying unidentified weapons. Threat level unknown. Further on-site confirmation required—
Raidian, you're up next. I'm starting the drone recall procedure.
In position!
Wait a second. Can you see me?
The bone pen taps lightly—its strokes weaving together the rough outline of a human form.
Raidian gives a tentative wave to the two people watching the feed, and even in floating text, her smile comes through clearly.
We are standing by, ready to assist you.
It's no problem. In ninety seconds, your drone and I will leave together.
On screen, Raidian walks into the signal-blocking tent as if no one else is there—and the transmission cuts out.
Bzzzz—
Logos?
We're ready to launch a full assault at any time—the incantations have already drifted on the wind to the edge of their camp.

lines.st: Ten seconds.
Twenty seconds.
Thirty seconds...

lines.x: Bzzzt—Done! All intel's been transcribed. I'll be back in a sec!
It didn't create even a single stir—I overwrote the visual signals received by each target's brain.
Chances are, all they saw was a mischievous little sandbeast slipping into the tent to escape the wind and sand.
Mechanist, how goes it? Did you discover anything while parsing the intel reports?
All weapons have had their origins erased and have been modified to suit their operators.
But I'm familiar with how to operate it. Doctor discussed its rather rare design with me just recently.
They are from Ægir.
Ægir? But the people who are going to interface with us, aren't they also...?
Regardless, the threat has been cleared.
We must not yield to such a threat and allow it to interfere with our equipment transfer—nor can we create any disruption that could alert the personnel involved.
The matter is of great importance and needs to be resolved quietly.

lines.st: PRTS Operation Record: [Confidential] Ensure the transfer and installation of mining modules
Executor: [Confidential] Misery, Stormeye, Sharp
Contact: (Records erased)

lines.x: There is no way. They're wearing special protective equipment to ward off our physical attacks.
This was arranged in advance—they even knew our personnel layout.
Orchid, stay back, they're clearly after our mining module.
Figures. I knew I'd end up working overtime again.
Spot, support Midnight and Catapult.
Popukar, have you called for reinforcements?

Yeah... I called already.
Orchid, be careful!
Hmm... futile. As for the others...

lines.st: 'There is no one else.'

lines.x: Where is that sound coming from?!
You really have prepared well. Unfortunately, you're not quite strong enough to handle Arts.
No, that's not right. It shouldn't be you.

lines.st: 'Shh. Even your heart has gone silent.'

lines.x: I'll deal with the injured. That said, Midnight is getting too worked up again.
Orchid, I'll have to trouble you to deal with him.
Leave him to me.
The Doctor guessed correctly. Someone leaked the details of our operations.
Wrap this up quickly. Tow the backup mining ship to the nearest nomadic city and get the energy bay patched up.
Then return to the ship at once to avoid any mishaps.
P-Pith, why are you our backup?
PRTS told me it would be Misery...
You followed a hunch?
A last-minute change in plans right before departure; I'm just worried that...

opts: Misery, Stormeye, Sharp and such have other plans.

lines.x: Do we need to inform the rest of our colleagues via PRTS?

opts: If everything goes well, I was worrying over nothing.
If anything goes wrong, resolve the issue as quickly as possible and then return to defend the ship.

lines.st: PRTS Operation Record: [Confidential] Escort Logistics and Engineering Department members to Kazdel
Executor: [Confidential] Touch, Pith, Mantra
Contact: Hoederer, Ines

lines.x: How many waves of mercenaries have attacked us already today?
This is the seventh wave now.
They may not be too threatening, but it's really strange how many of them have been attacking us every day this past week.
We've only just crossed Kazdel's border.
These guys do not fear death. They remind me of the mercenaries we met in Sargon.
Faithless.
Misery, what's taking you so long? We planned for you to be back by now.
You should know that this place is still quite some distance from the Scar Market.
Even if I knew every shortcut around here, it would still take me some time.
What has the investigation revealed so far?
For the past two weeks, someone's been routing the majority of Scar Market's orders here, through a network of outsourcers.
There's no way to trace the client—they've covered their tracks well.
In other words, it was just 'bad luck' that we ran into these mercs?
They're not actively coming to attack us?
I'm afraid so.
It seems that not all of the worries Doctor had before we departed were unfounded.
Everyone, there's news from the sentries.
Another group of mercenaries is shifting toward our position. There's a high chance we'll be engaging them again soon.
(Sighs)
Misery, something's not right here.
These unending waves of mercenaries don't seem to be trying to harm us. Instead... it's like they're...
Trying to delay us.
We can stay here and continue escorting the team. You all can return to the ship now.
Don't worry, Fang.
Some folks are going to find it harder than they thought to put their schemes into motion.
The air is fluctuating.
Is that wind?
No, is that... the Lifebone?!
High above the sky, a massive skeleton has emerged, stirring the dust of history and casting a vast shadow below.
For many at Rhodes Island, it is their first time seeing the massive skeleton that once appeared during the chaos in Victoria.
What a sight...
While investigating the Scar Market, I took a trip to Kazdel to meet a few old friends.
Let's leave the rest to them.
We should go home.
I've already silenced all communications in visible range. Engaging now.
(Blows bone whistle)
Hmph. Their weapons counter my gear, but they offer little resistance against your bone whistle and incantations.
Far from it. They just don't quite understand the rules of my speech yet.
I'm curious about the thought process the Ægirs followed in developing such precise weaponry.
Then I'll grab a few pieces to take back and dismantle—I'll help you study them.
Come on you two! We are carrying out a mission here!
There are at least three squads waiting in ambush up ahead. We can't take them all on.
I'm afraid we can't handle this cleanly and quietly anymore—we'll need to tell our people to brace for the assault.
Hey! Hey! Hey! The reinforcements the Doctor called for are here!
Raidian, Mechanist, mark the location for us. The Bad Guy will be there soon!
Blaze?!
Check your communication devices. I synchronized our location with you.
Acknowledged.
Wait a moment, Rosmontis, didn't we agree I'd rush in first and draw their fire away from you?
I'll control the Arts.
Finished?
I checked the signals in the surrounding area. They've all been cleared out.
Uhh... who can tell me what this thing really is?
When it tried to self-destruct, I had to use all of my strength just to stop it.
The people interfacing with us are also Ægirs. Why are they attacking our people and equipment?
Let Dr. Kal'tsit and the Doctor determine that.
Ensure that we return to the ship as soon as our personnel and equipment are safely transferred to Clementia.
We need to deal with these issues as soon as possible.
There are still other unknown leakers out there. This is just the beginning, I'm afraid.
That's strange, why is PRTS responding even slower now? Or is it just my imagination?
This isn't right, no. Let me check it again.
PRTS, output all back-end logs from the past two weeks.
Uhuh. No problems here....and no problems here either...
No problems anywhere?
Doctor, Mantra and the others have already returned to the ship.
Your guess was correct. Someone is targeting our mission.
This instrument is from Ægir?
Dr. Kal'tsit, can we discover anything from its remaining parts?
No. The Ægir Academy of Technology put a great deal of care into this delicate piece of equipment—Given their pride, I'll bet they made sure no one could ever crack their Little Handy.
But the appearance of the Little Handy itself is enough to explain many problems.
Doctor, do you remember the Joint Marine Corps?

opts: The reason you met with the Ægir consul was to facilitate cooperation.

lines.x: Both the Iberian and Rim Billitonian authorities have responded to our call. The problem is that once cooperation begins, subsequent developments could spiral beyond what we've anticipated.
In recent weeks, the Ægir fleet has not only constructed ports in Iberia, but also established landing sites along the southern coast of Rim Billiton.
The Ægirs from the deep sea made no immediate move to land. Instead, they quickly carried out a number of operations, including hydrographic surveying, under the banner of 'technical cooperation'.
They also quickly assembled a 'Land Patrol' through a mix of hired personnel and official partnerships, relying on more neutral intermediaries to facilitate cooperation with land-based forces.

opts: So you're saying the attackers are the 'Land Patrol.'

lines.x: We're still only speculating—but right now, the Land Patrol is the only one that fits all the evidence we've collected.
Dr. Kal'tsit, some consuls don't back Clementia, yes, but that doesn't prove that Ægir is behind all of this.
No, I am more inclined to believe that someone took advantage of the internal conflicts within Ægir.
The Land Patrol near the Iberian border, the mining ship being handed off in Rim Billiton, the mercenaries on the Kazdelian frontier...
The breadth of their deployments and the depth of their knowledge about Rhodes Island's classified missions—that's more than enough to prove that Ægir isn't behind this.
However, there is no possibility of a rebellion within Rhodes Island.

opts: I don't believe that will happen either.

lines.x: Doctor, do you think there's anyone else in Rhodes Island who could do that, besides us?
That's right. No one else could do that.
Dr. Kal'tsit, the threats that Rhodes Island faces now are unpredictable.
Dr. Warfarin also warned us several times—the special patients she is caring for—their conditions are deteriorating faster than she can control.
Whether to save our patients or to respond to potential crises, we need more aggressive responses.
I've reviewed the records Theresa left in the Black Crown—she also made major breakthroughs through her work on Civilight Eterna.
I can visit Civilight Eterna to look for potential help, just like Theresa did.
We must carefully assess the risks involved, Amiya.

opts: Amiya, are you sure you're ready?
Are there any other more effective means at the moment, Kal'tsit?

lines.x: I cannot give you a definitive answer, Doctor.
But I will never stop trying. Any advantage I can gain may help Rhodes Island progress further.
I want to try.
At this moment when the situation is still unclear, I cannot come up with any more effective measures.
But diving recklessly into Civilight Eterna may not lead to the outcome we hope for, Doctor.
Doctor, I cannot agree to such a risky plan.

opts: Can Civilight Eterna link to my thoughts?

lines.x: Doctor, that's too dangerous. No one has ever done something like this before! I agree with Dr. Kal'tsit that you shouldn't take the risk.
Kal'tsit recalls the experience of her dear friend, which is not something she is happy to recall.
Yes, Civilight Eterna can do that.
She did it before.
Doctor, your mind is incredibly complex, but you're not the only one at risk here—Amiya could be exposed to completely unforeseen shocks as well.
I will not agree to this preposterous proposal.
Dr. Kal'tsit...
Amiya, this is my opinion. I cannot interfere with yours and the Doctor's final decision on this matter, but I hope you understand my stance.

opts: Amiya, I can open my mind to you completely.
We don't have to force it in one. We can give it a few tries.

lines.x: But, Dr. Kal'tsit...

opts: When it's all over, we'll go apologize to her together.
It's not this plan she's opposed to, per se.

lines.x: I trust you, Doctor.

opts: I'll leave the decision of when to stop entirely up to you.
Let's start after I finish arranging the defenses.

lines.x: (Muffled roar)
Are you worried about me? I'm not mad at all.
Once again, we've arrived at a critical turning point. And last time… Babel met the fate I dreaded most.
It turns out that I can't make sure everything is running on its ideal track, Mon3tr.
(Raises claw)
(Rubs gently)
Our own limitations have begun to affect our usefulness in more important endeavors.
We may be able to witness the changes of the stars and the untamed cosmos, but when faced with a real crisis, we're no different from any other creatures on this great land.
(Dissatisifed growl)
Thank you for comforting me. I can sense your good intentions.
(Anxious growl)
Yes, I suppose Amiya and the Doctor have already begun preparing to delve deeper into Civilight Eterna.
I will not interfere with Amiya's decision. She is mature enough to make the most correct judgment.
And the Doctor... the Doctor isn't quite the same anymore either.
Maybe I still have a glimmer of hope in my heart. Maybe, this time the outcome will be different.
(Subdued bow)
It is true that my own sanity is constantly warning me.
Touching the Doctor's mind again with the help of Civilight Eterna is not a sound decision, but Amiya is right, we don't have much choice.
(Passionate cry)
Mon3tr, I will protect the Doctor and Amiya.
At this moment, Mon3tr can sense even Kal'tsit's slightest emotions.
Ever since Kal'tsit's latest awakening, Mon3tr has been at her side.
Mon3tr is her companion, the symbiote she personally guides... and more importantly, herself.
(Affectionate murmur)
You're wrong. I do not feel hopeless, Mon3tr.
I am delighted that the Ancients overthrew the rule of the Elders, and I am also pleased that Lugalszargus set the starting point for a new civilization.
I have made mistakes out of arrogance, but I have also regained my confidence by making dear friends.
Maybe yours and my basic structures will always be alien to this land, but that doesn't mean we will always walk alone.
I've found my home, in Rhodes Island, on this great land.
I've fulfilled her expectations, Mon3tr. I've learned to love. I've found myself.
(Moves confusedly)
(Wraps tail around Kal'tsit)
I must protect Amiya and the Doctor.
I must protect the true variable here... the future I cannot even glimpse.
I've communicated with Civilight Eterna before, yet I haven't been able to break through my own limitations.
But Amiya can. Of that, Theresa and I are certain.
I'm sure she can pluck significance from the massive trove of data. With Theresa's blessing… she can do it.
She is still Amiya, after all. I have seen her grow into a leader with my own eyes.
Following in footsteps, she might find some words that one left behind.

In the past, when Originium had not yet taken shape, there was an era when luminaries competed to argue with the epoch itself. Their wonderful ideas, born of a desire to celebrate and save lives, still persist.
All is not yet lost, Mon3tr. Terra has a chance, no matter how faint, to keep that hope alive.
(Excited howl)
Me? No, Mon3tr, I'm better at memories... remembering the history I witnessed, and have seen erased by time.
I am not special at all.
Kal'tsit calmly inscribes the important info into Mon3tr's spine.
If the outcome I fear most does come to pass, if the one I least want to see stands in front of us once more—
Protect the Doctor. Protect Amiya.
I will engrave this directive into your basic behavioral strategy matrix.
(Agitated howling)
Come, Mon3tr. We still have work to do.
If the threat does come, I must ensure that Rhodes Island is Terra's first outpost against the crisis.
...I will be your final safeguard.
I've never tried to use the Black Crown to access your thoughts before, Doctor—

opts: I've opened up my heart and mind for you.

lines.x: ...Thank you.
She looks to her side, at the beam of bright, warm light gently tugging at her hand.
She shuts her eyes, and slowly lets out a sigh of relief.

lines.st: 'We're in this together, Amiya.'

lines.x: I don't know where this journey of ours will lead us, Doctor, but I'll protect you.

lines.st: 'I'll protect you too, Amiya.'

lines.x: Under the watchful eye of Civilight Eterna, she gives your brow a light touch—
Your memories and emotions flow towards me.
...It's strange.
They billow so gently as they flow, and the memories' edge is close at hand...
What did Theresa see back then—
Amiya sees you, and Kal'tsit, and herself, and the many people aboard Rhodes Island.
As she walks deeper, the boundary of that one memory comes within reach. Curiosity and concern nearly cause her to forget her mission. She wants to reach out to touch it...
Touch upon the start of your memories. Chernobog, the Sarcophagus, and you, weakly stretching out your hand—

lines.st: 'Doctor... Take my hand!!'

lines.x: It looks like there's other memories too? Why are they so blurred?
Who was—

lines.st: 'Amiya!'

lines.x: You grab ahold of Amiya.
I-I don't know. This is the first time I've ever dived so deep into someone else's mind. It's a very... odd feeling.

opts: Good thing you pulled it off.

lines.x: Thankfully, yes. Now, I can sense even the faintest of your thoughts.
Aha, you sure are relaxed right now!

opts: I've got nothing to worry about with you around.
So, what should we do next?

lines.x: Hold my hand tight, Doctor.
I'll protect you on the road ahead. Theresa taught me everything about the role of King that day.
'She' awaits us, at the start of that path.

lines.st: I get what you're saying, but I cannot provide what you're asking for, as your request violates the underlying logic of DWDB-221E.
But I do have a way to circumvent its limits, Kal'tsit—
'Deep Dive'.
Unfortunately, not even Theresa was able to penetrate this deeply. The risk was far beyond what she could bear—
You're not here for answers? Then why are you so insistent I access this data that is useless to you?

lines.x: 'I have no way of knowing if there will come a day that Amiya asks you the same question. But I hope that, in the darkness, she will find the way I lit for her.'
That was Kal'tsit's answer to me, Amiya.
...Could I ask you to look through all of Dr. Kal'tsit's access records for us?
I would be happy to.
Her every doubt, her every insistence, each and every time she left in disappointment. I will show it all to you, unreserved.
You have dove into the Civilight Eterna before, Amiya, but this time, what you seek lies far beyond your limits.
It is a place where life on Terra cannot tread, for the time being, but perhaps you will find the chance to touch upon this knowledge that is not yours.
Because you have someone who walks alongside you. Dr. Doctor.
You will quickly understand what I mean, Dr. Doctor.
Follow me, Amiya. Follow me to—
'Civilization's Singularity'.
A great hallway of pure white circles around a central point that defies comprehension. The Singularity.
An unending stream of complex information flows from it, but the strands you manage to snatch hold of are still within your understanding.
Amiya cannot see the way you entered, but after an interminably long time walking down the corridor, she finds herself back where she started.
You noticed, Amiya.
The stagnation of civilization does not mean it stands still. It continues onwards on time's linear flow, unable to turn back.
But all that life discovers and creates is doomed to fall into an inextricable cycle.
Mathematics, physics, philosophy, theology... These theories and practicalities, among others, cannot inspire hope as they once did.
The ascending spiral has failed. Life squanders its future as it stumbles blindly in the dark.
So... this is as far as we can go?
Only as defined by Terra's current knowledge. It serves as a limit, yes, but it is also a safeguard.
A safeguard?
Cognitive?

opts: Like how we can't make sandbeasts calculate wave dynamics.
Even if we gave them all the data and formulae...
All forcing it will do is hurt their brains.

lines.x: As keen an intuition as ever, Doctor, but not entirely correct.

DWDB-221E enlightens life at a steady pace to bring it forward. It does not simply reveal fragments of the future to the seeker to create evolutionary leaps that violate the laws of nature and ethics.
Knowledge cannot be unlearned, Amiya. The dreamers who glimpsed the true sky are no longer happy to slumber in ignorance on the ground.
Information beyond civilization's cognitive limits will simply blind them to reality, a deadly poison luring them into annihilation's embrace, throwing their civilization into the abyss.
And so, this boundary was born.
But why does it... look so much like a prison made from some giant data server?
It appears in a form your mind can comprehend, like how DWDB-221E appears as a black crown in the material world when accompanying you.
To a minuscule ant, it is an ouroboric river, forever unable to flow to the sea.
But to a celestial body whose lifespan stretch to the trillions, it is a gravitational field that will stay with them their entire life, unfathomable, inescapable.
You walk towards a section but one step away from the void. The distant singularity at the core calls to you.
Information washes past you like gentle waves, showing you glimpses of many fascinating sights.
They are fleeting shadows of your past, a life you have lost and forgotten.
Stray visions of an 'unfamiliar' era.
Be careful, Doctor!
Amiya tugs on your hand.
We don't know what will happen if you fall off this boundary—
Are these... what you saw...?
—'The Originium slug egg' 'went through the bridge between stars'—
...So all those stories really did happen.
When I was really, really young, I met someone who was incredibly caring.
That care was matched with a great weakness, but also a stubborn insistence in carrying me across a road laden with Originium shards.
When I was dizzy with fever, yet too scared of nightmares to sleep, a patient voice regaled me with stories I had never heard before.
In a dark and cramped cave, difficult-to-find ore samples were smashed without the slightest hesitation—
And the shining stones were scattered across the walls, and used to tell me tales of the stars, and the heavenly paradise above the clouds.
Would you like to hear one of those stories, Doctor?
There was once someone who drove a mysterious vehicle shaped like an Originium slug egg on their lonesome, who went through the bridge between stars.
On the most quiet of nights, guides would borrow glowing clouds from old stars and make round mirrors out of them to light the way.
On the other side of the bridge, spires faded in and out of the clouds, and the stars wore crowns of flowers.
When descending on that crown, you'll see that it's a really, really big playground, one where lovely little children gather to play.
But it was hard for the vehicle's driver to stop...
The driver changed over to the most miraculous vehicle on hand, and drove it for many, many years...
But the years came to an end, and nobody was left.
Nobody but the driver.

opts: Is the person in your story... me?

lines.x: Amiya grips your hand tightly.
Yes.
Thanks to my infection, it's hard for me to remember much from that time, and there's a lot from those stories I don't understand.
But I will do everything I can to remember every single one of your stories.
Because they are my stories. Tales you told to me.
I don't want to forget them.
I never thought today would be the day I finally get to share your gift to me with you, Doctor.
This time, we witnessed it together, you and I. We got to know this past life of yours once more.
Do you long for this kind of life, Doctor?
I'm not surprised. I'm very much the same. When I first thought of such wonders, I longed for it too.
It's the kind of life I could never truly have for myself.
I couldn't help myself from sharing them with Theresa, and the two of us would add many wondrous details of our own imagining—
Until it changed beyond recognition, flying ever further out of reach...
But now that I've had a glimpse with my own eyes, I don't think I'm that far away from your past life.
Do you believe that one day, this great land will be just as beautiful, Dr. Doctor?

opts: Perhaps it will be even more beautiful...

lines.x: It will, and we'll be there to greet it.
Rhodes Island will be there to see the light of dawn.
You don't? Strange, it was such a beautiful life.
One without the pain of Oripathy, without conflict or war, where children could live their lives in joy...

opts: Maybe I didn't want to show you the crueler side of things.
There's always two sides to life, Amiya.

lines.x: Maybe you're right. The cruelty behind the happiness, the joy behind the suffering. We've experienced it all ourselves.
I'll admit, I shared these wondrous stories with Theresa, and she was just as amazed as I was.
She always hoped that everyone in Kazdel... no, in all the lands would have the chance to experience such a life. She did her best to see it come true.
We can't let things slide back, Doctor.
There may come a day that Rhodes Island will be able to see this beauty flourish across the land.
'Deep Dive'.
The intact data stored within DWDB-221E is decoded by the singularity and exported in a moderated and suitable format as information.
However, Amiya on her own will not be able to break through the limits of the baseline logic.
I am not certain, but the possibility is there.
But how are we supposed to get close to the information once it's exported?
Forgive me, Amiya, but I have no further information on this topic.
I will search through the clues Kal'tsit left behind, and sort anything that may be of use to you so that it is easier to find.

opts: Perhaps we could build a bridge?

lines.x: A bridge?

opts: Hasn't Kal'tsit already pointed the way for us?

lines.x: Has anyone done something like that before?
There is no precedent in the records.
...It's worth a try, either way.
Then you must be aware of the risks. The closer you get to the singularity, the denser the information will become. You may possibly experience it gaining mass.
Information with mass?
It is possible here, yes.
It is also possible that information not yet fully decoded will flood into your mind and destroy it.
And a destroyed mind means...
(Unknown language) Don't mind me, traveler. I'm simply watching yet another bout of destruction is all.
(Unknown language) Hierda. That's your name, right?
Did you just say my name?
I don't know whatever language you're speaking in at all. Also, how'd you know—
As the Cautus passes by the porthole, she sees the figure out in the darkness of space slowly approach the ship, then disappear from her sights.
Who is that, Raydistorter?
(Fervent singing) ♪—♪—♪—
—Is something wrong?!
There's not many of us who like her!
Sorry, traveler, I simply can't handle being in the same room as her.
If my friends saw this, they'd give me the cold shoulder because of it too.
When the bewildered Cautus snaps out of it, she finds herself the only person in the cabin. Upon foreseeing the arrival of an unwelcome soul, everyone else had made themselves scarce.
Then the Cautus sees her.
No need to look so amazed. I learned how to communicate using long-distance resonance a while ago.
At the very least, before destroying that odd race, I kept the unique language patterns they had evolved.
...D-Destroying?
(Sighs)
Do you detest destruction as well, our newly-come guest?
I... am not too sure what happened, to be honest.
It's nothing too complicated. My people once ate stars to live. Others may hate us for it, but it's simply our survival instinct at work.
But now, that past only exists in horror stories passed around the metaphorical campfire.
My people are on the brink of extinction. I fear I may be the last of my kind, in fact.
We destroy to live, but when true destruction finally bears down upon us, we are powerless in the face of it...
From that point onwards, I have become nothing more than a beggar. With the aid of some geniuses, I have changed the method I use to obtain the energy I need to live—
It sounds like... You've been trying to blend in with other lifeforms too.
So when you were out in space looking at that dead planet, were you reminiscing about the destruction you experienced?
Reminiscing?
Haha, nothing of the sort.
Everyone here has accepted the ruination chasing us with serenity and hope in their hearts.
The Cautus has never thought that oncoming doom, especially one pressing down on their neck, could be something to be accepted calmly.
I-I could never be so indifferent if I had to live through everyone and everything I love being destroyed.
I don't understand how you—
It's not indifference, but instead a belief in the absolute equality of the universe. It's a shame you arrived just a little bit late.
This ship had just encountered a star going supernova several thousand light-years away and made a smooth withdrawal.
If you see that sight with your own eyes, perhaps you will understand my meaning.
The instruments in the hall begin to spin rapidly, until they are moving too fast for the Cautus to see with the naked eye.
Then, she sees a multitude of lights and shadows appear and float through the air.
I see a giant sphere emitting a deep, dark red light, and a small indigo dot speeding across its surface.
But... it seems like it's the big sphere rotating around the blue dot?
The one you call the 'blue dot' is the pulsar that just went supernova I mentioned.
The red star which is gradually being torn apart is its partner, now doomed to die as well.
They once harbored many interesting primitive lifeforms. I have many fond memories of wandering through the planets of that star system.
It's a pity they were snuffed out before they could see the world beyond the sky.
...Why are you showing this to me?
Simply sharing the knowledge we have with you, traveler.
One-sided paranoia and ignorance are often barriers to effective information transfer. I have no knowledge of your history, either.
The Cautus has barely had the time to comprehend these words before suddenly, her newly-acquired knowledge begins to assault her mind.
The land... may see life be born twice? These stars fallen from the sky... can harbor life of their own?
Ruination's end... is not despair.
We're... not special, when it comes to the land?
A celestial body that can sustain life loves all that live upon it equally, no matter where they hail from. This is a law of nature.
Don't be too greedy in lapping up that knowledge you can't fully understand yet.
I saw you among all that information. You once had many of your people by your side, but suddenly—
'The Presence'. That's what a lot of people call you, right?
I am just a wandering presence, true. Like I said, when true destruction bore down on me and my fellows, I was powerless.
...What exactly is this destruction you keep talking about?
I've seen plenty of things here I can only describe as 'divine acts'. What could possibly threaten people like you?
I'm afraid that nobody on this ship can answer your question, traveler.
In your eyes, we possess knowledge that many lifeforms in this universe would consider 'divine acts'...
But in this boundless cosmos, even we have limits to what we can explore and investigate.
Are you disappointed?
So there's definitely no eternal kingdom, a paradise where everyone can experience endless joy and happiness?
Would you like such a place to exist?
The Cautus hesitates for a moment before nodding.

Nothing lives forever. Looking back through the history I know, I have come across legendary civilizations that have conquered countless star systems, spreading across the cosmos for millions of years.
But as far as the universe cares, what they left behind was, in the end, utterly insignificant.
We once blindly chased after non-existent principles and ideals... In the midst of that golden age beyond recollection, we believed our existences to have a sure purpose.
But in the end?
Any lifeform born with intelligence insists on declaring itself the center of its world, but whether or not it is right, in the universe's eyes—
From start to finish, we are nothing. This is the first thing you must learn before you can understand it.
Remember that brilliant supernova you just saw?
From that tomb, life will be born anew, and there will come a day when those beings will understand what we have carved on our grave.
That we once had lives of our own. That we existed.
...I don't understand anything that you're saying.
How were we born on this land?
Was there an even more glorious civilization here before us?
Are we going to be wiped out in the foreseeable future?
But what I really want to know is, all this pain and suffering that Originium brings with it... Can it be eliminated?
I beg you, show me the way to the answers I seek...
I can't answer any of your questions. Your first three have been asked in a myriad of languages in a myriad of ways across the universe. Only you can answer them yourself.
As for your final question... I'm terribly sorry, but I've never heard of this 'Originium', and I can't find anything related to it in our databases.
However, I do know someone rather interesting, who also happens to be the master of this ship. Perhaps she will be able to solve your riddle.
The master of this ship?
She's a linguist of wide renown. Her name is—
'Priestess'.
No time to be lost in your thoughts, Doctor!
Grab onto me!
I can't break my focus right now. Weaving together a way to the singularity is incredibly difficult.
Even if Theresa were here to help...
Did you see something, Doctor?!
You see a familiar figure.

lines.st: Go, 'Doctor'. Go solve your conundrums.

lines.x: I will! I'm doing everything I can right now to stabilize the connection points, Doctor.
Theresa has marked all the records Dr. Kal'tsit searched through earlier. I'm trying to link them all together.
But I still need an anchor point, one that can guide me back—
I will keep watch here, and serve as your anchor.
Press onwards, Amiya.
Pink, silken threads begin to gather.
The meandering threads interweave and spiral, gradually spreading out from the boundary into the distance.
Beyond its walls, the path to the singularity slowly takes shape.
It's working!
Originium Arts don't seem to work here, but applying the logic behind casting them to the information here does!

opts: Can you build the 'bridge' using completely unknown information?

lines.x: No, this place is full of information I can't analyze, not even with the help of Civilight Eterna. I wouldn't have been able to make this way forward alone.
But thankfully, Dr. Kal'tsit's access left records, and with Theresa's help, I can grasp onto her research data.
That's more than enough for me.
The pink threads continue to slowly spiral, linking together the many pieces of complex data that Kal'tsit left her mark on.
String by interwoven string, the path forward gradually forms.

lines.st: 'I can tell how you're feeling, Kal'tsit.'

'By analyzing the emotional matrix I constructed of you without your permission, I see your tenseness is not only because of my sharing an appearance with Theresa. You have something even heavier on your mind.'
'I will try to adjust. I don't want my personal emotional changes to interfere with the objectivity of those who come after me when they query these records.'
'Yet they are still changing, Kal'tsit. Are you afraid?'

lines.x: It's not the most efficient way to build a bridge, but it should hold.
I'm going to try to get closer to the singularity.

lines.st: 'Yes, I'm afraid.'
'I'm afraid Amiya and the Doctor won't be able to find the information I leave behind, and I'm afraid...'
'That if the situation worsens to the point that they are forced to take such extreme measures, the trail I cleared for them would no longer be of use...'
'Or that I might mistakenly lead these seekers of knowledge towards a threat I dare not speak of.'

lines.x: No, Doctor. I won't let you, not before I check its condition myself.
Sorry, I know I'm being stubborn about it, but this is my decision.
The bridge, formed from countless strands of entwined information, stretches out into the distance to touch upon the singularity.
Without the slightest hesitation, Amiya steps onto it, her first step outside the walls of the boundary—
A surge.
This feeling, it's like the information has formed some sort of great wave. But why—

opts: This is only the beginning, I'm afraid.
'The closer you get to the singularity, the denser the information will become.'

lines.x: It really is like Theresa said. The information here has mass.
We'll most likely find greater resistance the deeper we go.

opts: We need to come up with a better plan.

lines.x: I can't maintain the stability of a bridge this weak. Going this deep has taken a greater toll than I expected.
I need to keep going forward. As long as I follow Dr. Kal'tsit's beacons...
The silver mountains the Teekaz had entered flit by her; the black shadow cast by the Feranmut plunging into icefield vanished momentarily.
She sees more scenes fly past her, as if she were fighting the current of the river of time.
More and more incomprehensible, indescribable sights assail her mind.
She does her best to block out the unending, chaotic stream of information.
Concentrate, Amiya, concentrate... Just keep focusing on Dr. Kal'tsit's beacons... Press onwards...
I just need to touch the singularity—
Silence.
Amiya cannot even hear her own breathing.
What... is this place?
That giant sphere... is disintegrating?
Looking at the shattered sphere makes her feel dizzy, but it is the bizarrely-shaped ship floating in the air that causes an even greater bout of lightheadedness.
What... is going on?
Why am I seeing—
As she is trapped in her spell of dizziness, a rotating boulder slowly but inexorably hurtles towards her.
Its black shadow quickly fills Amiya's vision. She subconsciously tries to defend herself, but it is immediately followed by the feeling of freefall.
Did she fail...? Was it a surge of information?
'Amiya!'
...Doctor...?
Amiya senses a hand supporting her, keeping her thoughts steady by bracing her.

opts: Try to block out the interfering information.
Sorry, I couldn't sit back and do as I was told.

lines.x: Doctor... Thank goodness...
That moment just now...
You do your best to support her shaking body as the information wave surging from the singularity continues its assault.
The information, so dense it has nearly taken true shape, washes over your thoughts, leaving behind jumbled afterimages in the void—
It is the past long gone, the present being recorded en masse, and what seems to be the future, foreseeing your next steps.
It's a shame that right now, you do not have energy to spare to pay it any heed.
The two of you have already made the long, long, long trek from the boundary to this point without even noticing, and now, the singularity...
The singularity is right in front of you.
Did you see that terrifying scene earlier, Doctor?

opts: I don't know what it means, though.

lines.x: I'm certain it's not something that took place on Terra...

opts: But at least it means we're getting close to the singularity.
Even more information will show itself to us.
Can you still keep going, Amiya?

lines.x: Y-Yes, yes I can.
You are well aware of the situation you are in right now. Despite knowing it will have minimal effect, you still subconsciously stretch out your arm to try and shield Amiya.

opts: The attacks here are focused on our thoughts. I can handle it.
I've got a lead on what we're here for.
I've run the calcs on Kal'tsit's access records.
You can read the key points from my mind.

lines.x: Found them.
Using 'Slumber Bridge' as a filter, Dr. Kal'tsit conducted a total of 79,346 searches...
Although all the searches are related to 'Originium', according to your speculation, this data is not strongly related to its actual essential nature.
If the results are restricted and give us nothing, doesn't the trail run cold here?

opts: Kal'tsit retrieved the same entry over and over again.
This is, in itself, some information for us.

lines.x: A repeated retrieval? Wait, can the infinitely-looping code made from the repeat query count be deciphered too?
...You already know the answer, Doctor.
I've never heard of this name. Neither you nor Dr. Kal'tsit has ever said it before—
'Priestess'?
—Doctor, are you seeing what I'm seeing?
'Priestess...'
You have never seen this scene before, much less seen—
(Unknown language) You're here, Doctor.
(Unknown language) It took some time to refine your design for PRTS.
(Unknown language) I thought you had gone to take a rest.
(Unknown language) No, I've been thinking about your words this whole time.
(Unknown language) 'There is no language that can describe God, as the existence of God itself cannot be understood.'
(Unknown language) You know that was just mindless chatter.
(Unknown language) Suppose I take it as an axiom.
(Unknown language) With it as my starting point, would creating a language that can describe God mean we have the means to understand It?
(Unknown language) Does the prospect not excite you?
(Unknown language) Perhaps, in the pursuit of the answer to this question, we have been going down the wrong path—trying to understand the nature of the universe with its own laws.
(Unknown language) You want to use a language wholly created by us to interpret the universe. A far too audacious idea, Priestess.
(Unknown language) Haha, is it?
Without any further hesitation, she traces out a perfect rhombus in the air.
(Unknown language) Let's begin, shall we?
What are they discussing, Doctor?
You have seen this woman before in the Assimilated Universe. She once accompanied you—

lines.st: But I have faith... Doctor...
That when you are truly confused by everything, you will remember: the answers you seek were here all along.
I will be here.

lines.x: The scene plays out before you, but no matter how hard you try, you cannot get any closer.
Doctor, that person she's talking to... Is that you?
(Unknown language) I don't believe I'll be able to create a language that will allow us to derive the ultimate answer. Not by myself, at least.
(Unknown language) I stand on the shoulders of giants, after all. From the base I put together with the greatest discoveries of our colleagues' respective fields, I will continue onwards.
(Unknown language) Even if nothing will come of it in the end?
(Unknown language) Of course. It wouldn't be the first time.
(Unknown language) Does it look like we have anything better to do while we quietly wait for the darkness to swallow us?
(Unknown language) Besides, I'm sure you won't say no to me, Doctor.
(Unknown language) So, where are you starting your research from?
Priestess does not answer, electing to instead look at the faint dawn light peeking over the horizon. The one next to her does not bother her any further.
The two of them simply stand aboard the deck.
You look at her, and subconsciously tell yourself: 'She's pondering something again'—
Why did you think that all of a sudden?
What is it, Doctor?

opts: I don't think we'll be finding any important clues here.

lines.x: The information Dr. Kal'tsit wants isn't here?

opts: This is most likely a fragment of history from before Originium was born.
Based on what I experienced in the Assimilated Universe, at least.

lines.x: Doctor, you mean what we're seeing right now is because you...
—Then that huge ship floating in the sky and that giant, shattered sphere are also...

opts: I'm not too sure what relation I have with them.

lines.x: We'll make it clear, then.
But since you're sure this isn't our main target, we should continue onwards.
The singularity is right in front of us, but the bridge I built is reaching its limit.
The information density here's too high. Pressing onwards is like drowning in a deep river.
...How are we going to find the information we need in all this chaos?
Your thoughts come infinitely close to the singularity, all while the near-physical deluge of information presses down on the two of you...
While you yourself seem able to adapt to the pressure, you sense a great strain on Amiya.
You don't have much time.
You pull out every keyword the 'Priestess' in the Assimilated Universe said, and quickly connect together every clue left behind by Kal'tsit.
Calculate a new result as quickly as you can, then add a new condition... Continue this cycle until you have isolated the information you need.
Your thoughts are like diving into an endless ocean of building blocks. What you need to do is not particularly complicated.
Find the blocks that are different, their surfaces finely engraved with the words Kal'tsit left for you—

lines.st: 'I never forgot.'

opts: I think we did it, Amiya.

lines.x: Where... Where are we?

opts: The Rhodes Island of the past.

lines.x: But this place and our Rhodes Island are—
Dr. Kal'tsit once said the ship was excavated in Rim Billiton and had to be repaired before it could be used.
Just... Just what exactly has Rhodes Island gone through?
(Unknown language) Looks like you're in good spirits today. I guess our efforts paid off.
(Unknown language) Were you scared, waiting by yourself in the lab?
(Proud purring)

(Unknown language) Good timing then. I'm currently trying to put together a model that reduces the loss of meta-information during transmission through special mediums. Can you try helping me do some calculations?
(Restless twitching)
(Unknown language) Alright, that's enough. I terminated your process.
(Unknown language) You are to never harm your own core unit, even if an order comes from me or her.
(Unknown language) Protect yourself, AMa-10. Survival is an instinct the living are born with.
(Stubborn purring)
(Unknown language) Haha, at least your emotional module is iterating quickly.
Do you understand their language, Doctor?

opts: Ignore that person and the thing by their side.
What we need is somewhere else.

lines.x: You're right. Since Dr. Kal'tsit believed we would be able to find the key, she must have anticipated the language barrier.
Look at the glass next to that person, Doctor. It's got writing written all over it.
(Unknown language) It's a shame the development of Originium isn't going very smoothly.
(Unknown language) We're venturing into uncharted territory here. It's hard for others to keep up with us.
(Unknown language) While I have been engrossed in debate with her more often than not lately, our experimental progress has slowed.
(Vigorous consolation)
(Unknown language) No need to worry about us. If this model's operational efficiency meets expectations, we might be able to cross the first hurdle on Originium's development.
(Unknown language) Oh, right, Lu's sent us a message. There's been a rare breakthrough in the Caerula Arbor, and she'll be able to come visit us again soon.
(Heartfelt joy)
(Unknown language) Looks like you're very fond of her.

opts: Amiya, we need to note down those formulas!

lines.x: I'm trying—

opts: Is the bridge's stability under attack?

lines.x: Theresa warned me about this. It's Civilight Eterna's baseline logic trying to eliminate loopholes!
Because we're trying to transcribe information from outside the boundary.
Doctor, I don't understand what the characters in these formulas mean—

opts: We just need to record them down!
I'm memorizing them as best I can too!

lines.x: The information of the boundary begins to reorganize. The loophole is being repaired, and the scene you are witnessing begins to rapidly fade away.
(Unknown language) I've foreseen it, AMa-10. This model she and I developed together will succeed.
(Unknown language) I look forward to the day that language's creation is truly complete—
(Kindly reminder)
(Unknown language) DWDB-221E's synchronization is still underway? *sigh* I know that. It's not like I forgot myself, right?
The speaker starts to fade away as well.
(Whispering in unknown language) No matter where in this vast universe you came from, I bid you to witness the beacons we left behind, remnants of a civilization now gone to eternal rest.
(Whispering in unknown language) Perhaps it will be our blessing... and the final gift of hope.
You begin to feel like that person is... looking right at you?
(Anxious murmuring)
(Unknown language) ...We will see it come to be, AMa-10. I believe it so.
The formulas are beginning to vanish!

opts: Retreat, while we still have the bridge.

lines.x: But I haven't—
A massive attack strikes your thoughts, and you sense yourself falling.
The last act of consciousness you remember is keeping Amiya as far as possible from the singularity, and that familiar figure—
Thank you for bringing Amiya back here. Now...
Leave.
It is at this moment that the mental link between you and Amiya breaks. Your mind is so exhausted you do not remember anything that happened afterwards.
The two of you leave Civilight Eterna.
The information erupting from the singularity does not cease, and among the flow, there is an insignificant scene that has never been accessed since the day of its recording—
In the quiet of space, a ray of light illuminates a field of stars, bringing with it darkness and a despair beyond doubt.

When sensors tens of thousands of light-years away receive the image of this starfield from the distant past, the planets that these stars shed their light upon are already filled with graveyards and ruins.
It has come.
Did the Presence tell you to use these words to defend her?
No, she never asked me to do such a thing when she left.
Then keep your wits around her. Don't let her confuse you.
...Because if you let down your guard thanks to her charm, she might put you in danger. That's how her people hunt. It's their instinct.
As for the person you're looking for, Priestess, the master of the ship...
She's doing some important research right now. I'm afraid her consciousness is shuttling back and forth between various research locations in this starfield.
It'll be very hard for you to have a moment with her if she's not willing.
Hierda, are you so desperate to find her because you want to thank the person who rescued you?
Not exactly.
I understand that I'm in some fantastical place called the 'universe', and it's not the afterlife, either...
I even have a wild guess that my home of Terra might be somewhere out there in this universe.
But the more I know, the more questions I have. I want to ask her—
Why am I not dead?
Where exactly is my home, Terra?
The Cautus looks out through the porthole at the stars in frustration.
Perhaps it is because her bishop and the rest of her fellow churchgoers are still praying for her back in her land, but she has no way to respond.
She curls up against the wall, allowing the light of the stars to quickly sweep across her face.
It turns out you can see time passing by with your own eyes.
She has met many people aboard this ship. No matter if they were the respected, hooded genius tucked away in the lab, or the wanderer of the universe who had survived ruination...
Their optimism and kindness have dispelled her initial fear, but she knows that she will never truly be one of the crew of this miraculous ship.
'I miss Terra already.'

lines.st: Administrator permission found.
Preparing reboot process from hibernation. 3... 2... 1...
PRTS reboot diagnosis complete—
Warning: Unauthorized 3rd-party plugin found. Restore default settings?
Administrator key cognition signal verified. Restoring...
Vital sign detection module activated. Automatically loading repair module. Repairing...
Progress: ■ ■ ■

lines.x: Doctor...
Doctor! I'm so relieved...
You're finally awake.

opts: How long have I been out?

lines.x: The evacuation hasn't been smooth. Our neural link was abruptly severed, and it's hard to maintain stability due to the information impact at the boundaries.

opts: What is my current condition, Amiya?

lines.x: I just woke up myself.
Don't worry about me. I'm doing much better, thanks to Theresa's protection.
I'm not entirely sure.
You should get a full check-up from Dr. Kal'tsit.
Good thing you never let go of my hand, or I would be even more worried.
Don't go off on your own, Doctor. We need to stay in the emergency observation area that Dr. Kal'tsit set up.
Soon after we began, Dr. Kal'tsit cleared out an area that she is personally protecting, in order to ensure our safety and Rhodes Island's.
She didn't want any accidents to happen when we entered Civilight Eterna.
I started preparing cognition baseline tests as soon as I confirmed that you're awake.
Initial testing showed no obvious signs of damage for either of you.
Diagnosing thought isn't quite as simple as diagnosing biological tissue, though. Further analysis will be required.
Welcome back, Doctor.
A bit of distraction happened during your operation. I'll update you on the latest developments later.
The first priority is to make sure that you're safe.
Establishing a neural link through the Black Crown is extremely risky. I take it you've long crossed the 'boundary'.
Then, my first duty must be to ensure your cognitive stability and biological health.
I won't allow the mistakes of the past to be repeated, nor will I allow the continued existence of any potential threat to your life and safety.
Follow me, Dr. Doctor.
You'll need to do the cognition baseline test too, Amiya.
First, though, get some rest.
A-Alright, Dr. Kal'tsit.
That's not part of my plans, Doctor. I'll arrange for Amiya to be tested as soon as your tests are complete.
Let's go.

opts: I don't remember it at all.

lines.x: A prototype that the Medical Department developed. Theresa and I designed it together.
But the limitations of our understanding of cognition and perception have prevented it from being put into service as a way to measure cognitive stability.
Under current circumstances, however, it's the most effective method available.
Go inside the isolation pod, Doctor. Relax. I've tried it myself, and it won't cause any significant physical distress.

opts: Anything else I need to know?

lines.x: No. Just follow my instructions. I'll need to calibrate the machine first. Relax and do as I say, but do not hesitate.
Tell me your name, Doctor.
A good start.
Relax, Doctor. We're just calibrating.
See the chronometer behind me? Count the time in silence and try to synchronize your counting with the ticking of its numbers.

opts: (You silently count and feel the passage of time)
I'm not sure what the point of this is, Kal'tsit...

lines.x: It helps reinforce your perception of reality. It's been twenty seconds. Are you feeling any discomfort?

opts: Is the machine showing something abnormal?

lines.x: Let's continue the calibration.
Are you able to continue to perceive Amiya in your consciousness?
Likely residue perception from the neural link being severed improperly.
Good, we're getting used to the flow.
Continuing with the calibration. Do you agree with Rhodes Island's ideals?
Good. We're almost done with the calibration.
An interesting answer, Dr. Doctor, but this is not a lie detector. Unless you were expecting a more dramatic outcome.
Continue. You saw her at the cognitive boundary.
As I expected. You're calmer than I thought you would be, though.
No need to hide it from me, Doctor. You can trust me.
Final question. Do you have doubts about the one who is like you?
A reasonable emotion, but I cannot resolve your doubts in any way or capacity. You will find the answer, sooner or later.
Maybe you've already formed your subjective opinion. It's not my place to interfere with it, but I trust you will not act contrary to Rhodes Island's interests.
Looks like you've formed your opinion. I will not comment on it.
We're done, Doctor.
Cognition baseline testing indicates that your cognitive stability remains within safe values. The rest of it is secondary.

opts: Wait, I thought that was the calibration.

lines.x: It was necessary to ensure that the data collected is accurate.
I trust that you're capable of perceiving my thinking and camouflaging yourself accordingly.
It would not be hard for you to fool this prototype.

opts: ...Is this part of the test too, Kal'tsit?

lines.x: The remaining checks will be complete soon, Dr. Doctor. I have time to answer a few questions.
You understand, of course, that there are certain questions that I am not in a position to answer.

opts: ...What are the origins of Rhodes Island?
How were you able to predict our movements ahead of time?

lines.x: If you're referring to this ship, it's even older than the history known to me.
In a sense, it's the carrier of hope, hence the comprehensive protective systems capable of supporting our every operation on Terra.
It is thanks to the initial design that it has survived the most brutal challenges with its core structure intact.
We have yet to discover its full potential.
However, I remain cautious about trying to unlock this potential in its entirety.
You mean the hints that were left behind at the boundary? There was no guarantee that it would be you who would solve them.
Nor am I able to predict things that have yet to come to pass. It was for another reason that I made such preparations...
Someone I respected very much had computed all the possibilities that could occur in the future. Someone whose words I will never forget.
Perhaps I have been quietly influenced too.

opts: The current uncertainty is not the only reason why you're nervous.
I can't describe this strange intuition.
I've never sensed such complex emotions from you.

lines.x: I'm not the one getting tested—
You see your reflection in the window, almost as though you were still standing with Kal'tsit.
You look at your own reflected image, but cannot help recalling the figure at the boundary, who treated the construct with such kindness...
Your gaze turns towards Kal'tsit.
Why are you looking at me like this, Dr. Doctor?

opts: Did you also put your utter trust in me?

lines.x: I still do. You've proven yourself.
Whatever you see at the boundary, Dr. Doctor, belongs to a past that is dead and gone.
It is enough that you and Rhodes Island are walking on the proper path. You and I, we both have the right to our subjective definition of this relationship that exists between us.
You don't need to be concerned about how I view you, not before, not now, not ever. I respect your understanding of our cooperation—

opts: I'm apologizing for my and Amiya's reckless actions.
I know what to do next.
I just have one more question, Kal'tsit.
(Unknown language) 'Perhaps it will be our blessing'...
(Unknown language) 'And the final gift of hope.'
I don't understand what it means.
I seem to sense good intentions behind the words.

lines.x: It means 'blessing' and 'gift'.

opts: You guided us to that one.
Whoever it was, there is nothing fake about your trust.
Then we have a lot of work to do.

lines.x: A regular-shaped Originium crystal emerges in the space.
A short curved line appears on the corner of the crystal.
Line expands into surface, then into a perfectly smooth sphere.
It is an eye that observes this dimension. All it does is record all that it sees; the organ that makes decisions has not fully escaped the Originium.
Until now.
(Perceives silently)
Doctor, Dr. Kal'tsit has determined that there is no major issue with me.
But she was concerned about whether I was subject to any shock during the neural link.
We should get on with analyzing the formulas that you retrieved.
Like I said, the abnormalities of PRTS persist. It's very likely that we'll be getting even more anomalies in short order.
Closure has been trying, to little avail.
Deployment of the backup command system has begun, but it will take time to synchronize it across all equipment and individuals.
As for the information you brought back from the boundary, I can help you decipher it.
Do you understand its meaning?
I'll have to see the formulas in their entirety to be sure.
You and Amiya attempt to reconstruct the words and formulas written upon the glass of the corridor by memory, written in a language that you do not understand.
Based on the context, I believe I can fill in the blanks here.
Largely accurate, but there are small errors here and there. Let me fix them.

opts: It is like putting together a jigsaw puzzle.

lines.x: Thankfully you were able to remember the last parts that I didn't have time to.
That should do.

opts: Do you understand the meaning, Kal'tsit?

lines.x: It's a heretofore unnamed model, the origins of which may be traced to a project by the name of 'Slumber Bridge'.
It was a radical project that attempted to employ a self-transcoding model in order to combine all possible realities and tangible, organic life into a single 'meta-reality'.
The project was ultimately a failure, but the experiments on digitizing matter were the predecessor to various subsequent research, including this model.
It has proven to be incredibly efficient at reducing the loss of meta-information during transmission through special mediums, and increasing data transmission efficiency.
Now you're getting to the heart of the matter, Dr. Doctor.
Originium is the medium used for information collection. It reformats the information and continues to propagate until its mission is accomplished.
This model, likely the one used by Originium itself, could lead to major breakthroughs in Originium research and Oripathy treatment.
I will decipher this information for classified archiving.
Until its risks and uses are ascertained, however, it must not appear in any blueprint or storage medium that exists in reality.

opts: Can you get it done as soon as possible?

lines.x: Do you have something in mind, Doctor?
The project that Logos and Mechanist proposed after the war in Victoria?
Boosting the adaptability and combat ability of elite operators in high-Originium environments with the use of external equipment. A radical idea, and a difficult one to achieve.
The current version, developed two years ago, represents the limit of what we are able to achieve.

opts: Now, we've discovered a new possibility.

lines.x: It's an excellent opportunity. At the very least, it will allow elite operators to deal with the Originium threat with greater ease.
I will need your help in the decipherment process, Doctor.
*cough* *cough* Thanks for the barrier. A life-saver.
Are you sure that Originium circuit has been properly set? I've checked it three times. It shouldn't fail...
No semantical deviation. The circuit conforms to the simulations.
There should not have been any error.
How about another possibility... it's not we who are wrong?
There are no flaws in the blueprint produced by the Doctor and Dr. Kal'tsit. The incantation simulation is effective.
What I mean is that there's something wrong with the material used in the Originium Arc itself!
I seem to remember that Closure kept some good stuff in the warehouse—
Bad idea. We've made enough of a mess in the Engineering Department as it is.
Well, we're doing it on the orders of the Doctor and Dr. Kal'tsit!
But how do we validate its effectiveness?
Assuming that safety is assured, there is one ideal candidate for testing.
Doesn't feel like there's been any real change in strength.
How about you go harder, kitten?
It's not entirely up to me. Raidian?
According to the statute of experiment, I can only increase the synchronization between the Originium Arc and Rosmontis in steps.
It's not just to protect you, Blaze. It's to protect Rosmontis as well.
Hehe, of course. I know.
I just wanted to test its limits as soon as possible, because Dr. Kal'tsit said this was urgent. How about you, kitten?
I can control it.
No problem. I'll keep the risks in check.
Raidian, be ready to overwrite the Originium circuit at any moment and disconnect Rosmontis's control.
...Nothing keeps one on edge like working with you.
How long will it take to finish the preparations, Dr. Kal'tsit?
Assuming that the Doctor and I complete the decipherment as quickly as possible, and isolate a functional technical blueprint...
It's cutting things too tight.
There are numerous key points in this technology that are virtually impossible for us to achieve.

opts: The surest way to fail is to not try.

lines.x: With the Black Crown, I should be able to help.
We must explore any avenue to gain an advantage for Rhodes Island. Perhaps it will make an impact in the not-so-distant future.
The Cautus feels like she has slept for a long time.
Her gaze is lost in the stars of deep space, but they offer no guidance as to where home is. She refuses to give up until she falls asleep from exhaustion.
Then, the light rouses her, and she opens her eyes.
There is no one around her.
(Unknown language) You are lost.
(Unknown language) It has been a long time since this place has seen a new visitor.
(Unknown language) Or are you the last survivor of the ruined planet?
The voice of Raydistorter? But why—
(Unknown language) In any case, welcome.
(Unknown language) Come. Seek me.
She remembers this scene. She should have died after disintegrating, but light rushed into her eyes again.
She came to this place, the domain of the gods... it all happened again.
H-How could this be?
She looks out of the porthole in terror, and sees the dead planet.
Roaming through the ship, she encounters that being by the name of Raydistorter once again, and the Presence that gazes upon death, alone in deep space...
Every trace that she was ever here has disappeared.
No... no... no...
Am I still dreaming?
This is not a dream.
But it's not reality either.
You were looking for me, weren't you?
The Cautus sees a hand reach towards her and take hers—
Like the hand that held hers when she disintegrated.
She saw 'God'.
Friston does not understand why his daughter chose this as their meeting place.
This is no vacation spot. Too much sand in one's shoes.
I should take a break.
He sits on a dune and removes his shoes, only for the wind to send the sand he pours out into his face.
*cough* *cough*...
This is most undignified—why am I in full dress?
Why did she ask me to come here?
There is no answer on the radio. He wanders aimlessly on the yellow sands, calling his daughter's name.
No one answers until night has fallen.
...A party?
He follows the sound to find the camp on the dunes in the distance, filled with lights and ribbons.
He hears his daughter's familiar laughter, and runs towards her in joy.
You made it to my wedding, dad!
Friston almost cries at the sight of the golden hair.
I've been looking for you for so long, my sunshine.
Is this your wedding?
Of course. The ceremony is about to start. Let's go.
We'll walk the red carpet, arm in arm. Give me your blessings, dad.
Friston tries to take her arm, but he can not reach it.
Will you not give me your blessing?
No... no, that’s not what I meant... I—
(Unknown Language) I—
Will you not give me your blessing?
(Unknown Language) ...Sunshine?
You did not want us to see the truth of the stars?
(Unknown language) ...You're not my sunshine.
(Unknown language) Have I left home? I remember this bunker—
(Unknown language) 'The preservation room'.
You will not answer any more of my questions?
(Unknown language) Kristen. You're Kristen...
(Unknown language) I've heard of you. No, I've seen you... no. How did I know you?
(Unknown language) I've never known you—
(Unknown language) Who the devil turned off the light?!
(Unknown language) Damn it!
Why have you imprisoned me here?
Closure! Is this another one of your damned jokes?
Friston realizes that he is buried in the 'photos' that fill the room. A volume of 'A Complete Bestiary of Terra' covers his camera module.
Are they even photos? He has never visited the places in them, nor could he ever.
There are only fragmented images on the countless photos.
Sand dune, bonfire, camp, golden hair, and the preservation room where the sun does not shine.
A dream...?
Friston dreamed. Friston-3 dreamed.
He printed his own dream.
It should not be possible for me to dream. Did Closure upload a new firmware without telling me?
I don't have time to argue with you right now, Mr. Friston!
I guarantee you in the strongest possible terms that I have not uploaded any firmware that could have caused you to dream.
The PRTS anomalies are getting out of control. It will take time to deploy the backup command system.
All the work has to be assigned manually. I wish I had six or seven pairs of eyes! We've completely lost contact with some areas and we have no idea what's going on—
Relax, Closure.
I'm not trying to argue with you. I just want to make sure that all possibilities for my unusual malfunctions are accounted for.
We've eliminated firmware bugs and hardware malfunctions as possibilities. Then—
What is it?
Answer me, Closure... this is important...
What is the energy source that powers my core?
Huh? Originium, of course.
Are the majority of your retrofitted operation platforms Originium-powered?
...Your tone is starting to scare me, Mr. Friston. What are you trying to say?
You're a genius, Closure.
Huh?
Excellent thinking, cutting off the link between the retrofitted operation platforms and PRTS. But that's not enough, not by a long shot.
Huh? What's going on? Why are things shaking?
Popukar was awakened by the continuous flashing. She looks out of the porthole, but sees nothing unusual outside.
Looks like a nice day. No sign of rain.
Maybe somebody was training noisily outside?
Hm... PRTS comms are still down?
Looks like today's assignment is in the isolation zone outside the ship. I should report to Orchid.
Morning—hey, where did everybody go?
It's so quiet... where is Orchid? I can't reach her...
I should check the cafeteria.
The cafeteria's... this way—
Popukar's sleepy eyes are only half-open, but she could find her way from the deck to the cafeteria even if they were completely shut.
That is how well she knows Rhodes Island—
Wh-Whoa, whoaaaa!
O-Ouch, that hurts...
She is tripped by a toolbox from the Engineering Department left in the corridor.
I thought Engineering was done with working in this area...
So careless!
She kneels down to rub her knee, which doesn't hurt so much anymore, then puts the scattered tools and boxes away to the sides of the corridor.
That light again... what...?
Popukar stands up and looks to the porthole next to her—
She sees many strange people.
Who are they?
New visitors...?
The other side of the porthole is supposed to be the deck. Are these people lost?
She walks briskly through the corridor, but begins to slow down as she becomes increasingly confused.
Why do I see them on every porthole?
O-Orchid? This is Popukar.
Seems like communications are still down. I don't know if I'm getting through to you...
Are you seeing anything weird?
There is no response from the communicator.
(Unknown language) When true destruction... on me... powerless...
(Unknown language) Equal end...
A-Are they talking? I can't hear...
Th-The mission comes first. Maybe Orchid is already waiting for me in the isolation zone outside.
A left turn here...
Go straight, then push the door open...
Then the exit passage—
Let me get to the ground first...
Huh?
I think I'm here...
Popukar turns around to look at the exit passage that she came out of, only to find that it has disappeared.
Popukar! We were looking for you. Everyone else is here—
Is something wrong?
O-Orchid... is the exit passage here?
What exit passage?
—*Bzzt* All operators... receiving this message...
Do not... trust...
Communications... blocked... Rhodes Island... awake...
—Repeat... *Bzzt* Warning—
...Closure?
(Advances hesitantly)
(Hostile posture)
I'm sorry, little fella, but I can't let you hurt anyone else.
(Spins angrily)
Why did you attack us?
I'm not sure what's going on, but something must be wrong with the ship.
You went missing for a while, and this is what became of you when you came back.
Besides, the structure of the ship seems to be changing. I could hardly find my way.
Such a strange feeling. It's like... like the ship is—
Like it's alive.
Impossible... we had set up an isolation zone around Rhodes Island. We should have gotten a warning if any enemies had approached.

opts: Unless... the enemy was from within.

lines.x: I'm not sure... we're in the command center, but we're not getting any information about what's going on anywhere on the ship!
No response from Dr. Kal'tsit or any of the elite operators to our messages, either...

opts: Let's get everyone together. Then we can think about how to deal with this.

lines.x: Okay. I'll protect you.
(Approaches silently)
What is—?!
Is the backup power online yet?
The junction box broke. Try cutting off that damaged circuit. Maybe short-circuit protection is still kicking in.
Watch your feet.
I can see.
We're in luck.
The Originium crystals all appeared in an instant.
Still no word from anyone else?
Comms are still jammed. Even the emergency frequencies are out.
I have even worse news. For some reason, Rhodes Island is currently headed northwest at maximum speed.
At this rate, it's only a matter of time before we collide with a mobile mining platform.
We must eliminate all possible dangers along the course. Protect the ship from the outside, and prevent collateral damage.
Let's get out of here and check our vehicle inventory in the Bad Guy's cabin.
Space around here has been transformed. Existing maps are no longer reliable. Stay close to me.
(Comes to a sudden stop)
Is something wrong?
They're coming again.
Hardly had he finished when an arrow whooshed through the air.
Missed?
There is no answer.
Stormeye?
Sharp turns around to see that the door to the restaurant has turned into a metal wall crawling with Originium.
But it is not Stormeye standing behind him—
(Approaches soundlessly)
Is that it?
Communications are still out.
I wonder how the others are doing.
Pith gazes at the communicator silently with a furrowed brow.
The footsteps disappear into the distance.
But the fallen Originium entities rise again, moving in perfect unison, striding over the damaged communicator on the ground.
'—*Bzzt* All operators... receiving this message...'
'Do not... trust... communications blocked...'
'*Bzzt*... Rhodes Island... awake...'
'Awake'?
Watch the sides.
Very strange. These things won't die.
Ascalon points her sleeve-blade towards a wavering shadow not far away.
Silhouettes manifest from the shadows like ghosts, every step accompanied by the sound of crystal cracking.
The clothes they wear—Reunion? And Nachzehrers too... nothing can surprise me now.
Their bodies are composed entirely of Originium. We cannot rely on past experience.
Guess we'll have to improvise.
We can't just stay here. We have to find where these things came from.
The intention of these enemies is unclear, as is the situation on the Doctor's side. If...
Amiya is with the Doctor. No need to worry.
We continue with our mission—ascertaining the source of the abnormal events.
If this is what Kal'tsit was guarding against, then these things must follow the command of some unknown actor...
Communications are still out. We can't reach anyone else.
