I wonder if the other elite operators—
Ascalon...
As they pass by the Originium crystals, Misery catches a glimpse of a fleeting image in the multi-faceted reflections of the crystals.
Did I just see Outcast?
Clack, clack—
A sharp claw separates itself from the surface of the Originium crystal, then curled horns, rugged silhouette, and spiked tail.
This creature is not unfamiliar to Rhodes Island's battle records, but never has it been composed solely of Originium.
As the never-before-seen form of life takes its first step, an arrow impales its throat and shatters its skull.
—Safe. For now.
Stormeye leans against the wall and pauses to catch his breath, before tending to his wounds.
At least it won't hinder me in battle.
(Static)
Sharp. Come in, Sharp. Can you hear me?
If you hear me, leave a sign somewhere, any way you can. I'll come and find you.
(Static)
...Dammit—
*Bzzt*—Stor...Stormeye—Careful—*bzzt—
Sharp?! What's your location?
*Bzzt*—Engaging—Whitesmith—Stop—
...? Wait for me!
...Assist...
More rampaging machines rush into the corridor, the light in their eyes flashing in sync.
Their simple voice units repeat a call for help, defying the limitations of their design.
Not now!
Stormeye's arrow pierces the cores of two of the machines with unerring accuracy. Then he makes a run for it, not pausing to look back.
At least... thirty.
A fork...
Two virtually identical corridors extend as far as Stormeye's eye could see.
The pathways within Rhodes Island are undergoing constant, haphazard changes. No one knows to which fates the diverging corridors lead.
The rampaging machines are catching up.
(Furious howl)
Faster than expected. Rhodes Island is not ready.
If there is such a thing as being ready for her.
(Alerted growl)
However, something is clearly not right with her as well. She would not resort to such inefficient means otherwise.
This may be our last chance to gain the upper hand.
(Approaches silently)
Clear a way, Mon3tr. Don't get stuck in a fight.
Time is of the essence.
The passage before Kal'tsit changes again, in an attempt to lead her astray.
But the warping of space does not affect Kal'tsit. She can still sense her target—the Sarcophagus of Dr. Doctor.
The information is etched into the bottommost layer of her memory.
Mon3tr. Eliminate all threats that approach the Sarcophagus. Buy me time.
(Excited howling)
Once again, she stands before the familiar Sarcophagus.
Once, she awoke the hope that slumbered within, only to find pain that could not be easily wiped away.
Now, it is their last hope.
I can't guarantee that this is going to work, Mon3tr.
But I won't just sit around and watch a fighting chance slip away.
Kal'tsit walks towards the Sarcophagus and places a white flower on top of it.
This is her legacy. Little did she know at the time that it was fire stolen from the 'gods'.
...We must all challenge those higher beings and fight for a chance to live.
(Nervous growl)
If something goes wrong, Mon3tr, act on your own discretion.
She enters the Sarcophagus.
The interference is really bad.
Not only am I not getting any signals, my Originium Arts are also being suppressed by the environment.
Think, think—

lines.st: 'Raidian...'

lines.x: This signal profile—PRTS?!
Closure? But the terminal still shows disconnected.

lines.st: 'Track me...'

lines.x: Raidian tracks the faint signal to an unexpected source.
Dr. Kal'tsit?!
How? But you're—no matter how you did it, you need to—
...No, you can't stop now.
Thank you, Dr. Kal'tsit.
I know what to do now.

lines.st: 'This is Raidian speaking.'
'With assistance, I have been able to establish a temporary electromagnetic communications environment.'
'Intel provided by Dr. Kal'tsit indicates that the abnormal events currently occurring on Rhodes Island originated from the core area known as the Abyss.'
'You are now receiving a briefing about the Abyss. All elite operators are asked to locate the Abyss, while making every effort to ensure your own safety.'
'I repeat. Locate the Abyss, but do not approach without permission. Maintain constant communication at all times.'
'Do your duty, elite operators—'
'Dr. Kal'tsit will be your eyes.'

lines.x: ...Kal'tsit? Thanks.
This is Pith calling to all combat-effective operators. Report.
We're still trying to stop these... things!
But they just keep coming!
We lack the means to completely neutralize these hostile Originium entities. Survival is your first priority.
Any injured personnel are to immediately disengage and seek assistance from Touch.
Kal'tsit... what have you done THIS time?
Dr. Warfarin! Look out!
These things can't hurt me for now. But there are too many of them. It's not easy trying to protect everyone.
Raidian's intel indicates that these things can't be killed.
'Kill'... are these things even alive?
Have Dr. Touch and Dr. Kal'tsit run into these monsters too?
Protect yourself. We're not as weak as you think—
*Sarkaz expletive*!
What I wouldn't do for a chance to take you apart, piece by piece, to find out for sure whether you monsters can die—
Get clear, Chloe!
The Originium entities continue to rush towards Warfarin. Two successfully penetrate her defenses.
They raise their hands towards Chloe—
Then stop. Their motion is not one of aggression, more like an invitation to the patient in front of them, one who is so close to death's door—
(Gazes silently)
Get back! Don't touch them!
B-But I can hear him... screaming? It's like those pained voices are etched into my mind...
W-We're very... alike...
Chloe takes the hand of the Originium entity. Her eyes are filled with sorrow.
Why... why don't you hurt me?
...Are we the same?
Is it because of your infection? The symptoms we've never seen before... Originium is changing.
—That's why your condition progressed quicker than expected!
Does this mean I can help you find a way to deal with them?
I want to try. I want to help you.
I don't want to disappoint you, or let Dr. Touch or Dr. Kal'tsit get hurt...
Warfarin repels the Originium entity in front of Chloe and stands before her patient in a defensive posture.
Do you understand what it is you're offering?
(Smiles) I don't. But I'm not afraid.
Not as fast as you say, Stormeye.
I got sidetracked by the changes to the deck plan.
Luckily, Dr. Kal'tsit and Raidian were able to reestablish communications. Who knows what else I would've run into.
You mentioned Whitesmith over the radio?
One instant she was inviting me to drink and dance in front of her fire. The next instant she was gone, leaving only a bunch of Originium-polluted machines crawling out of the furnace to attack me.
A hallucination?
I can tell what's real and what's not, Stormeye. But what's happening here is beyond anything I can comprehend.
Not that I'm the only one who's seeing strange things.
Originium formations are growing at a visible pace. They thought they knew Originium well enough, but now the facets of the crystals threaten to draw their very minds in.
Stormeye narrows his eyes. He sees Sharp's mouth moving, but his ears hear nothing.
All is quiet. Soon, he cannot even hear his own heartbeat.
The expression he sees on Sharp's face changes.
The next instant, all sound disappears from Rhodes Island, leaving only dead silence in the communications channel that Raidian established.

lines.st: 'Don't fall behind, Stormeye.'

lines.x: He sees familiar figures, men who were once brothers to him...
He has been reunited with the brothers from his wandering days. But they died long ago...

lines.st: 'That beer we looted is gonna get warm if you don't drink it quick. What are you so worried about?'

lines.x: (Soundless words)

lines.st: 'Yeah, the rocks have gotten to my throat, but I can still drink. Haven't gotten my tongue yet.'
'You really want to go with those people? Suit yourself. Won't shed any tears for you if you die on that Rhodes Island.'
'But... sit a while longer by the fire before you go. The moons are almost up. Don't you want to see them?'

lines.x: He had yearned for a peaceful parting with his brothers, but right now all Stormeye feels is anger.
At the same time, on the other side of Rhodes Island...

lines.st: 'Where are you going?'
'You've already found me, Ascalon.'
'There's no need to go any further.'

lines.x: Only a timely push by Misery saved Ascalon from the flying Originium shrapnel.

lines.st: 'We haven't settled our bet yet, Misery.'
'Don't get distracted. Look, I still have four rounds left in my gun.'

lines.x: Originium is growing rampant, and the appearances of the mysterious Originium enemies are ever more frequent, accompanied by 'illusions' that they cannot help but be distracted by—
The very place itself is trying to drive them out.
Ascalon wants to reach Raidian, to tell her that the Abyss has been located...
But the Originium drowns out all sound.
The deathly silence is a mystery, a tomb made of lead, threatening to drag Rhodes Island into an abyss from which there is no escape—

lines.st: 'This is Mantra'.
'I've spoken to Dr. Kal'tsit. I will not suffer this mockery of memory and emotion.'

lines.x: Every operator on Rhodes Island hears Mantra's voice in their head. It is her Originium Arts, the power and fury of which all of them can sense.

lines.st: 'If it tries to stop us with silence—'
'—Then I will be the voice that connects us.'
'Now, I return your voices to you.'

lines.x: It's Mantra!
She's sharing the information that the others have collected.
Ascalon and Misery are close to the Abyss.
They've sent over the Abyss's signal profile to Raidian, who will be able to trace it, even if the spatial environment changes again.
They are currently holding position, helping Raidian establish an information anchor based on their own biosignals.
Blaze and Rosmontis are on their way. It won't take long.
I'm worried about Dr. Kal'tsit too, Doctor.
Raidian's information indicates that Dr. Kal'tsit is in a dangerous predicament...

opts: Regroup at Ascalon and Misery's position.
Then, we'll work together to find a way through the Abyss.
We won't make Kal'tsit wait too long.

lines.x: Yeah. We're able to maintain communications thanks to Raidian and Mantra's Arts, but this puts a lot of stress on both of them.
Time is of the essence, Doctor. Let's go.
You carefully lead Amiya through a forest of Originium shards. Your ability to precisely predict when and where explosions will occur is a major advantage.
Focused on the path ahead, you fail to notice a little detail on the massive crystal formation that you just passed.
It is not your figures that are reflected in the smooth surface of the Originium crystal.
A Cautus girl with her back to you, her thin, fragile hand held by another, just like you and Amiya right now.

eps.name: Bear Witness, the Death of the Gods

lines.x: We peruse and interpret the past and future of our civilization, as a monk seeks a revelation from his god.
Humans enjoy talking about the possible futures for their civilization, do they not?
At first, we flaunted what little vestige of truth we managed to grasp, and attempted to converse with the 'gods'.
We were answered with silence.
Then, we began to observe the fates of our own kind—
Some were less advanced than us, some slightly more so... in the end, what we learned was that death was the inevitable end of all the creations of nature.
And so humanity inferred that this was the revelation the gods never spoke.
In an instant, we thought we had grasped the truth of the universe.
We carefully 'monopolized' the revelation gained through sacrifice, and preached the inevitability of doom with so-called optimism.
'Death is the inevitable end of all the creations of nature. All we can do is to leave behind the truth before our doom.'
You've lived on that ship for some time, and come to know some adorable lifeforms. Do you agree with this assessment?
You don't agree with the Presence?
I respect them.
They were great people, but most of them were simply fools slightly smarter than the others.
Why are you asking me this? And... why have you brought me here?
We were in that corridor just a moment ago.
Here or there, it makes no difference.
You have too many questions, questions that this place can answer.
What is this place?
The graveyard of civilization's memory.
Towering statues made of unknown materials stand upon the vast expanse.
Ripples spread outwards in every direction, up towards the sky beyond the dark clouds, and down towards the grey-white ground.
Out in the vastness of the universe, civilization was born, like a drop of water falling into the ocean, leaving ripples in its wake.
They cross paths, love, hate, and eventually die.
The ripples of dead civilizations are forever etched upon this tombstone. Even after the bright stars of the forebears who inspired so many that came after them are long gone—
They still leave behind an incomparable impact on the universe. It is the ripples they left behind that form this land upon which we stand.
Their legacy fosters new civilizations, which in turn leave their own traces upon the universe.
But it seems... broken? Like a tree trunk snapped in half.
You're not the first to describe it that way.
Once, someone very important to me said the same.
An absolute, ultimate presence tirelessly reaps the branches that sprout from this poor trunk, yet we know nothing about It.
What you've shown me is like... a miracle...
You saved me... from disintegration...
...God.
'God'?
I'm just a humble linguist attempting to explain the universe.
My name is Priestess.
And, strictly speaking, I did not save you.
Your death has already occurred. I did not intervene in the process of matter being converted to information.
But material destruction is not true death. Information is not so easily erased, and your information was stored in Originium.
B-But I can think. I felt I was living when I was on the ship. All this vivid—
A stream of data repeated over and over.
I intentionally left behind fragments of history in order to observe our past lives.
So this is like a dream?
'Dream' is vague and subjective. Do not allow language to lead you to meaningless self-doubt, before you have touched its fundamental nature.
You may understand this as a snapshot from the past that I have recorded in the form of information.
But it's nothing more than a still frame. There is no future for it.
Then why am I here?
I saw you at the instant of your death.
Someone disturbed me. It was a coincidence, a whim of probability.
So... I'm not special.
Oh but you are, Hierda. You, and those like you, are very special.
Originium has shaped you into what you are. You have interacted with Originium in wondrous ways.
Your history and your civilization all exist because of Originium.
But Originium has also brought so much pain...
Yes. It was a mistake. Your existence was not part of the plan.
What?!
Do you mean that the people on Terra shouldn't exist?
No, you misunderstand.
Your civilization was born of probability. Just like you, standing here right now, it exists in time on an objective basis.
Born of probability, and lost to probability. It is the most basic rule of the universe.
The Cautus needs time to digest it all.
Originium brought destruction to her, but also consolation and infinite knowledge in this frozen time.
She feels lonely, but no pain. If there is one thing right now that could cause pain, it would be—
Terra... my home...
It was not blessed by God...
She turns around to look behind her.
The land stretches as far as the eye can see, rippling like mountains extending infinitely.
She cannot see the end, nor any trace of Terra.
But she knows that the land is just within reach, if she only stretched out her hand.
Terra.
Is it doomed...?
The 'god' looks up at the megastructure and does not answer.

lines.st: Father Bishop...
Does that future you're always talking about really exist?

lines.x: Doctor, be careful!
Originium activity in this area far exceeds Dr. Kal'tsit's estimations. Originium crystals are growing at a breakneck pace...
Plus those strange enemies that keep appearing—
Yet your attention is drawn to the Originium in front of you.
Every shard, every facet...
Reflects the same scene in your eyes—

opts: Her silhouette appears on every facet.

lines.x: I am curious about you, Hierda.
From here, I can observe everything of your world. You are the second being from this civilization to interact with me.
Do you still love your so-called 'great land'?
I think so... yes...
Even if the pain that it brings you is real and inexorable?
Yes...
Is this what concerns you?
I... I'm not sure what you mean.
Only praise for life and the indomitable will. Death has not overcome you.
You came to me in search of an answer regarding the future. We stand now before that answer—
One that cannot be described by language.
—What is it?!
Remember that absolute, ultimate presence I mentioned?
It was once thought to be light from a distant star.
The Cautus looks up almost instinctively. Dark clouds cover the sky, blanketing the plain in darkness.
Then, a faint light breaks through a crack in the clouds. Looking upon the light, masses of people cheer and exclaim.
The Cautus feels as though she were among those masses.
I'm witnessing—
History.
Then, the ray of light bursts into a rainbow spectrum, as though the universe were painting upon a black canvas.
In their last moments, the pious souls continued to pray to the mysterious blessing of the universe.
The people of reason, the scientists, they boarded their flying machines and flew into the light, hoping to touch the truth up close.
(There's no movement? Why isn't anything happening, now that they've gone into the light?)
The idealists, the conservatives, denounced the enemy for invading the space that had belonged to them from their first moment of sentience.
They brashly directed their most dangerous weapons towards the prismatic sky.
The gentle light accepted all, neither exploding nor fighting back.
The light followed its determined path, ignoring the agitated souls beneath, though chaos and death had already begun to spread amongst them.
W-What's happening?
Do you want to go into the light?
The Cautus feels her vision climb upwards, over the agitated crowd, over the burning mountains, over the dark clouds.
She can almost reach out and touch the rainbow light.
Weapons powerful enough to destroy an entire planet fly past her into the light without a sound. Ships that can sail the stars fall powerlessly to the ground.
They resisted.
They prayed.
They tried to sway the cold light with all things bright and beautiful that they had created over millennia—
But the light was unwavering, cold like deep space.
The light passed over the planet and left. All was silent.
The Cautus finds herself gazing into deep space, at the broken remains of the celestial body.
It is like the sight that the Presence was gazing at, that she was curious about, when she gazed upon the Presence.
It's... all gone... why?
From whence did the doom come? Why did it come? Did it leave? Where did it go? What happened to all the lives—
Then she sees it.

Only broken remains are left upon the ruined surface of the planet, when the light of the star once again shines upon it. The planetary fragments break through the bonds of gravity and scatter to infinite space.
It was once a flourishing civilization that marked those fragments.
No one could ever be prepared for It.
Even the wisest, most knowledgeable sages of this civilization had hoped for some premonition, some warning before the coming of the doom.
Unfortunately...
The Cautus witnessed the death of the gods, the powerlessness of the gods. For a moment, she even forgot how to fear.
Is this... the history that you experienced?
No. It's simply one of countless fragments that we captured.
This single glimpse could hardly compare to what I experienced for myself.
The answer that you seek is not yet complete. Listen to the sounds that emerge from the ruins.
The rocks that come from deep space—
Comets. Sometimes, they are the bringers of the seed of life; other times, they do little more than fill the silence of a dead planet.
Where do they come from?
Anywhere, perhaps even another destroyed planet, the marks of a lost civilization still etched upon it.
The gravity of the dead planet begins to draw in guests from deep space, and it begins to be made whole again.
Those ships floating in deep space—
Explorers, seekers, colonizers, perhaps even plunderers, coming from far away.
They, too, accelerate the evolution of civilization.
Do you mean that's what happened to Terra?
Your greed has gotten the better of your fear.
What you learn cannot be unlearned, Hierda. Having glimpsed a vestige of truth, are you willing to go back into the darkness of ignorance?
The Cautus continues to stand before the imposing 'gravestone'.
She touches the ripples upon it and trembles.
Every mark is the end of a civilization... only now do I truly feel—
Can I stay here and learn?
You have shown me the key to the answer. I have to comprehend all this knowledge on my own.
Even though this is only a fragment of time, frozen forever?
...Yes.
You're very much like us.
You may roam this place and anywhere on Rhodes Island freely, to seek the answer that you crave.
As for whether you want to stay here, or go back to the Terra that you loved so much...
I will respect whatever choice you make. We will meet again soon.
Y-You're leaving? Where are you going?
Someone has been waiting for me.
...For far too long.

lines.st: Unauthorized 3rd-party plugin deleted. Default settings restored.
Expired administrator access detected. Intercepting...
Repair module self-diagnosis in progress...
Progress: ■ ■ ■

lines.x: I've received your inquiry, Closure.
PRTS is denying all access requests. We've lost administrator permissions and we do not have the ability to retake PRTS.
Kal'tsit?!
Finally, an answer!
Where are you? The Doctor and Amiya are coming over to help!
Bad news. PRTS is taking over all equipment on the ship, including the engines.
I know. I'm fighting for Rhodes Island's last chance.
Closure, warn the Doctor and Amiya—
She's waking up.
Kal'tsit enters the Sarcophagus.
She had once stood watch over this very Sarcophagus, waiting for an elusive speck of hope from the Sarcophagus's master.
Transcending the temporal constraints of civilization, this space leads to the secrets in the deepest reaches of the ship.

lines.st: Warning: Unauthorized access detected.
Identifying... ID confirmed.
ID: AMa-10

lines.x: The Sarcophagus has my profile?
Time is of the essence. I must connect to the core databanks of PRTS.

lines.st: Accessing PRTS...
Warning: No administrator privileges. Access will be restricted.
Warning: Unable to load repair module. Adaptation incomplete—
Warning: Error. Error. Errorerrorerrorerror—
Identifying... ID confirmed.
ID: Kal'tsit
Virtual machine loading complete.

lines.x: (Unknown language) Dear Kal'tsit. If you have triggered this encrypted AI model, that means the hopes I left behind have been impeded—
(Unknown language) Perhaps I myself have come to an untimely end.
(Unknown language) Dr. Doctor...
(Unknown language) You are surprised by my appearance, Kal'tsit. Have I failed?
(Unknown language) The future of Terra is yet unknown.
(Unknown language) I never expected to see you again, Dr. Doctor—
(Unknown language) This is simply an encrypted program encoded within my cognition model.
(Unknown language) It analyses data input and provides an appropriate response. In other words, it does not possess my emotions or memories.
(Unknown language) I do not wish to disappoint you again, Kal'tsit.
(Unknown language) ...Why did you leave it behind?
(Unknown language) Because of you.
(Unknown language) I know that you will only return here when all hope seems lost, in search of one last chance.
(Unknown language) By then, however, I'm afraid that even I am unable to turn things around.
(Unknown language) You cannot help me anymore?
(Unknown language) My Sarcophagus will render what assistance is possible, to the extent that it is possible for me...
(Unknown language) Even when It descends upon us.
(Unknown language) I didn't come to seek sanctuary, Dr. Doctor, but it's true that danger is very near.
(Unknown language) I need your help to take back PRTS.
(Unknown language) That will be difficult.
(Unknown language) The core databank of PRTS will deny access to the individual known as AMa-10. She has set this as an absolute prohibition.
(Unknown language) Is there no way around it?
(Unknown language) Perhaps there is one.
(Unknown language) If you are no longer AMa-10.
(Unknown language) AMa-10's gemini cycle system has granted you immortality. It appears that the two individuals in the cycle have diverged over the ages.
(Unknown language) Violence and wisdom, conflict and understanding... such evolution represents the most basic power of life.
(Unknown language) But these were duties forced upon you. They have become your shackles.
(Unknown language) Now, I empower you to lay down your duty.
(Unknown language) This is the final gift that I can confer upon you by way of the Sarcophagus, but the ultimate decision is yours.
(Unknown language) What reason is there to hesitate?
(Unknown language) I must remind you that this is extremely dangerous.
(Unknown language) When you become the interface to PRTS itself, the functions of the Sarcophagus will cause great harm to your physical form.
(Unknown language) The energy supply of the Sarcophagus is insufficient to fulfill your request and protect you at the same time.
(Unknown language) Sanctuary will become torment, and you will forfeit any chance to extend your life. Are you sure you want to do this?
(Unknown language) I am sure.
(Unknown language) I respect your decision.
(Unknown language) Goodbye.

lines.st: Backup power is now online.
PRTS permissions updated. Administrator permissions confirmed.
Welcome back, Dr. Doctor.
Warning: Repair module has been disabled due to insufficient power.
All communication module channels open. Communications receiver module overclocking procedure completed.
Connecting to local PRTS databank... connection complete.
Warning: Operator vital signs unstable. Unable to load repair module.

lines.x: Kal'tsit feels intense pain.
With her mind connected to the Sarcophagus, vast amounts of data on a civilizational scale begin transmitting with her as the medium.
She sees the main server beneath Laterano...
The silent preservation room...
The Caerula Arbor deep beneath the waves...
The Shadow-Moon rising from the megastructure on the dark side of Terra...
Time and space are compressed into a single web within Kal'tsit's consciousness. She sees all of Terra, and all of Rhodes Island.
She gains a connection to everything.
At the same time, however, the connection between her and Mon3tr, one that has existed for ten thousand years and more, grows weaker by the moment.
...Dr.Doctor... this duty is not a shackle.
It was always my choice.

lines.st: Warning: Operator vital signs unstable. Unable to load repair module.
Warning: Unknown error.
Warning: Unknown error.
Warning: Unknown error.
Warning: Unknown error.

lines.x: I have no idea what lies ahead, Mon3tr.
This is the only thing I can do for you before I go to face her.
The rest is up to you.
Kal'tsit breaks the limits that her creator placed upon her.
In exchange for half of her life, she is reborn.
Outside the Sarcophagus, Mon3tr roars in anxiety and bewilderment. It has lost track of Kal'tsit for the first time.
The connection between it and Kal'tsit has been completely severed.
I am not going to the far side of Terra. She is here.
Right here by my side.

lines.st: Administrator access detected. Permission verified.
Welcome, Dr. Doctor.

lines.x: I've found you.

lines.st: You're not Doctor.
AMa-10?

lines.x: My name is Kal'tsit.

lines.st: You have crossed the line.

lines.x: And what of it—
(Unknown language) Priestess.
Kal'tsit finally speaks her name.

lines.st: You are uploading unvalidated data, AMa-10.
But you will not see it through to the end.

lines.x: Is that so?
I'm no longer fighting on my own.
I can see you, Raidian.
I'm fighting for control of PRTS. Time is short.
Trace my signal.
I will be your eyes.

lines.loc: Fifty-seven minutes from the start of the incident

lines.x: Yes, but some compartments are still not responding. They may have taken too much damage.
I've captured the bioelectrical signals of other colleagues. Comparing with environmental data—
Generating ship-wide scan...

opts: Get ready to synchronize all signals, Mantra!

lines.x: You hear Mantra's calm, collected voice in your head.
All active operators on Rhodes Island have been located. Ready to synchronize data from Raidian at any time.
Scan is complete, Mantra!
'Synchronization complete.'
'Attention all hands, this is Mantra speaking.'
'You are currently seeing in your mind real-time scan data of Rhodes Island's current state.'
'Perhaps you are under attack from unknown sources, or isolated and lost.'
'But we are now taking back the initiative, under the direction of Dr. Kal'tsit.'
'The scale of the current emergency exceeds the scope of individual discretion. You are hereby directed to act in accordance with my instructions.'
'Rendezvous with nearby operators as soon as reasonably possible under your individual circumstances.'
'Regularly monitor the status of your protective equipment and avoid high-threat areas if at all possible.'
'Elite Operators Touch and Pith are conducting cleanup operations outside of the core area. They will be in touch with you to direct regroup efforts.'
'The scale of the task before you must not be underestimated.'
'The Doctor has designated a Conflict Zone, Quarantine Zone, and Buffer Zone, in reverse order of their proximity to the Abyss.'
'While making every effort to ensure your own safety, you are to work on maintaining the Buffer Zone and delaying the expansion of the anomaly.'
'There is reason to believe that this crisis is impacting far more than just Rhodes Island—'

lines.loc: Fifty-eight minutes from the start of the incident

lines.x: Cleanup of the two adjacent zones is complete.
The scattered personnel have regrouped under the direction of Operator Raidian.
Are you hurt?
It's not that bad. Closure's protective equipment was more effective than I expected.
But it's not enough to deal with the dangers of extended exposure to high-Originium environments.
Your squad cannot participate in this next operation. Get some rest in the safe areas of the Buffer Zone once you have received treatment.
With the witchcraft defenses designed by Logos and Mechanist, you should face much less pressure there.
But—
This is an order. Don't worry, I won't allow anything to happen to any operator under my charge.
Once the ship has stopped, you'll need to direct the other squads to evacuate and establish a larger Buffer Zone around the ship.
Rest and get ready. Leave this to us.
I hope things go as well on Sharp and Stormeye's side...
'The status of the engines is unclear. There is a risk of collision with objects on the ship's course.'
'We are taking all possible measures to reduce the possibility of this occurring. We will issue the warning to brace for impact, if necessary.'
'The ship will be brought to a stop once we regain control of the engines.'
'Once we do, Rhodes Island will proceed to establish a new Buffer Zone around the ship to prevent further spread of the anomaly.'

lines.loc: Sixty-one minutes from the start of the incident

lines.x: From this vantage point, it seems the ship is still accelerating.
Not as much as it had been. What Logos is doing is working.
Let's trust our comrades. We've got plenty on our plate as it is—evacuating people and eliminating potential threats on the ship's path.
Brace yourself. We're accelerating.
I have visual on the mobile mining platform. It's possible that the ship will run into it.
Stay on the current course, Stormeye. I'm dropping into the target location in twenty seconds to evacuate the crew of the platform.
Approaching target.
See you in a moment.
'The Conflict Zone surrounding the Abyss is the key area that we must break through.'
'The elite operators have conferred and unanimously voted to break into the Abyss together.'
'We are the knife that cuts through the darkness, and we are Rhodes Island's last line of defense.'
'Do not let fear avert your eyes, comrades.'
'Trust in my voice. Trust in Dr. Kal'tsit.'
'Trust in Rhodes Island.'
Seems like you've eliminated the threat.
I saw lots of familiar faces before you came.
The floor is littered with broken Originium crystals, a sign of the ferocious battle that just occurred here.
The boiler worker sighs and picks up a broken epaulet made of Originium.
These Originium things just won't die. I recognize the faces they wear...
Some of them I strangled with my own hands, others went away to a far away place. But I don't suppose there are many left of those who left.
You're not surprised about the things that are happening?
There are many secrets on this ship.
But all I care about is my boilers. That's the one thing I asked Her Highness to let me do.
I need your help, Aefanyl—
I can't get the boilers to cool down, and there wouldn't be anyone here to watch them if I left.
The facilities of the mobility layer are spread out across a large area. Even you can't deal with everything.
The control system is completely shot. I don't have the tools to manually calibrate the engines.
Trying to brute force it would be even more dangerous. The Originium particle density in the air is way past the warning line.
'Separate.'
There is room in the trappings of machinery for words to pass through. I will slow down the supply of fuel to the engine.
He waves his bone pen, and incantations emerge from its tip, passing through the steel walls and expanding outwards.
'Stay.'
I shall embrace Rhodes Island with words.
I will pull it back.
The ship is slowing down.
None of us is giving up, Chloe.
Our efforts are not futile. We will not allow them to be futile.
I guarantee that they will receive your gift...
(Struggles)
You'll be free soon. The latest data have been uploaded to Kal'tsit. Once it's validated—
The Originium entity shatters silently, every last fragment removed from the lab through the ventilation system.
This strange thing will trouble Warfarin no more.
...Success.
But she finds little joy in it.
The ship is slowing down. Logos has been successful.
About the course that Logos brought up... Dr. Kal'tsit has charted three possible courses.
There is one thing in common among all three predictions.
They all end in the Targangils Prime Vein. I believe that is the course that PRTS has set.

opts: This ship is being guided towards the vein?

lines.x: However, the predicted course that best matches the current course will take us through a major canyon.
It's unlikely that we'll be able to traverse it.
Definitely strange. All the threats that we're dealing with right now don't seem bent on completely destroying Rhodes Island.
Losing PRTS, the appearance of the Originium entities, the shifts in the ship's internals, the change of course... none of these things would truly destroy us.
I have a breakthrough regarding the Originium monsters, Dr. Doctor.
After uploading the experiment data to Kal'tsit, she has modified the bottom-level logic of Originium Arc and distributed the upgrade package to all operators.
It will allow them to more effectively deal with these Originium entities.

opts: Where did your data come from?

lines.x: Let's have a long chat, you and I and Kal'tsit, after we get out of this mess.
That's all I have to say.
...I still have patients to attend to.
Dr. Warfarin...
Whatever the case, Dr. Warfarin and Kal'tsit have taken care of a big problem for us.
But all these threats only serve to delay our solution to the problem.
Is it possible that the one we're facing doesn't want to be our enemy, Dr. Doctor?

opts: More likely, they just have no choice.
We'll know what's going on once we're in the Abyss.
Give me a location on Closure, Raidian.
Pass the word on to Closure, Mantra.

lines.x: You got it, Doctor.
I've located her signal. Communicating with Mantra.
One moment.
I'm getting the location from Raidian right now.
Ready.

opts: How are things going on your side, Closure?

lines.x: We analyzed the structural changes recorded by Raidian, and it seems like the shifts correspond to certain cyclical patterns.
It's just that the data sample from each individual cycle is so vast and disorganized that we thought the changes occurred at random.
It's all thanks to Mechanist that we managed to figure it out.
Wasn't so complicated after all, once you had data of sufficient quantity and accuracy.
Also, Closure and I have created a provisional model that we can use to calculate and plan a route.
In other words, the spatial shifting won't trouble us for a while.

opts: There's no time to waste then.
We'll attempt to penetrate the Abyss.
Before that, we must assemble and provide support to Kal'tsit.

lines.x: These damn things just won't stop coming—
You're too slow.
Now's the time for you to get out.
Misery shakes his head.
We don't have nearly enough intel.
This is the closest place to the Abyss that we've reached. We're the only people who've gotten this far, and the only ones who can help Raidian locate it—
That's not what you're really thinking about.
I don't want to be too slow again.
Isn't that the same for you, Ascalon?
Rosmontis and Blaze's squad is approaching your location, but they've run into a small problem.
They've been delayed.
Blaze scratches her head and looks at Rosmontis helplessly.
How do we handle this, kitten?
I... I'm not sure.
The buggies are still preparing to attack us.
I don't want to harm them.

Why did they suddenly lose control when we approached this area—
Wait, Friston-3! Stop!
No, I'm not going to ram you or breathe fire at you like the kids. I'm perfectly normal.
Now, let me through. Time is short, and my patience even shorter.
Playlist 'Golden Oldies' selected...
Resuming from previous playback—
—My love♪
Blows the blossoms on the hills—♪
Now's not the time for karaoke, Friston-3!

You think I wanted to do that?!
My playback module is customized, and I don't have the remote—
Damn you, Closure!
Blaze! Watch out for Justice Knight!
Which damnable subdirectory did Closure put my code synchronization function in?!
—Gonna give you my tears♪
Save my precious—♪
Kitten, on my six. Justice Knight has me in her sights—
Got it. Whoa, almost hit Justice Knight—
Ouch! Stop running into me!
(Rams with burning heat)
Burn, burning—Boom... mmmmmmmmm—
—No matter who♪
Whispers of love, one trap one lifetime♪
Alright, THRM-EX! Let's see who burns brighter!
Hey, don't hurt the buggies—
I know! I got this.
Protect, protect, friends—
You need healing, Friston-3.
I guess I should thank Closure for fitting that energy barrier generator—
—Here's my most beloved♪
You shine in my tears—♪
Friston-3! How long do you need?
The synchronization module is ready, but I can't jump across this Originium.
I'll make a path for you. Now!
Friston-3 accelerates up the ramp and takes flight.
As he spins in mid-air, he can not help but throw a photo out.
(Man, it's been so long... so long since I moved like this.)
(That synchronized transmission system that you boasted about had better work, Closure...)
Guide these crazy carts into position and immobilize them. I'll fix them!
—A gift to you♪
This love in my heart—♪
I have a connection!
The song continues to echo in the empty desert.
—A gift to you♪
This love in my heart—♪
There is no question that the playback module has malfunctioned, playing Closure's favorite ballads over and over, as though this scene were supposed to be some tear-jerker.
But what he is actually doing is sharing the dream of inevitable parting with a few buggies that do not even have minds of their own, sharing his deepest memories as a human.
Trevor Friston would have no shortage of profanities to spew, if he could spew them.
What's this place...? I thought we were trapped in a... in a... uh...
I don't remember anything—
Beep beep! Anomalous target detected!
Who are you?!
Use your brains, whatever they're made of!
Closure says you're supposed to be smart!
That voice, that tone... y-you're Friston-3!
But Friston-3 isn't as tall as you are.
No, he is Friston-3! I feel a familiar warmth from him—
Warmth... how come I can't feel my own warmth anymore—
Ugh, this is such a headache.
Maybe this will make you feel better.
What is this place?
My dream. A dream that was a surprise to me too.
Now shut up and listen.

Something is wrong with PRTS. I have a pretty good idea of what's going on. Someone made your cognitive modules go haywire, and you were wreaking havoc all over the place. I had no choice but to put a stop to it.
Beep beep. Justice Knight does not understand.
Basically, I'm rewriting your bottom-layer code with my core code. I'll ensure that your memory modules remain intact, of course.
This will take some time. In order to make sure that you stay put during this time, I had to connect your cognitive modules to mine.
The side effect is... *expletive from an unknown language* I share my dreams involuntarily.
What is going on, Friston-3?
A friend of mine is waking up from a long slumber.
Stop thinking. It'll overload your brains.
What you need to do is walk with me before things come to a close. The gathering is about to begin...
A blonde dances joyfully in the camp filled with lights and ribbons.
It is a party!
No... a wedding. My daughter's wedding.
Are we not going to join, Friston-3?
We'll watch from here. I don't want to go there, because it means I'll once again—
Are you sad?
...You can tell?
Of course! Are we not the same?
You said someone made our cognitive modules go haywire. Why did you help us?
No particularly special reason. Like Lancet-2 said, we're the same... at least we are now, on Rhodes Island.
Life on this ship is peaceful. I won't say I like it, but compared to that dream without an end, those unrealized futures, it's not as...
It's not as bad.
Trevor Friston can hear the familiar laughter of his daughter.
A wedding that he must miss, a promise that he must break. Perhaps, if he did not go any closer, he would not have to watch her walk away...
...Code overwrite complete. Let's go home.
He does not wish to stay there, not even for a moment longer.
What happened to Rhodes Island?
Friston-3? Are they all back to normal?
For now.
I rewrote the sections of their code associated with PRTS, ensuring their basic operation with my own core code.
Great. Now we can go and help Misery's team, kitten.
Where are you going next, Friston-3?
I'll have a meeting with the kids, now that they've woken up. There should be something that we can help with.
I'll talk to Closure. I've got a score to settle with her anyway.
Looks like the buggies are back to normal for now, Amiya.
What about Mr. Friston?
Well, he seems a little pissed at me... other than that, everything looks alright.
Great idea, rewriting PRTS-associated code with his own. Would have been a lot of trouble otherwise.
Are all our PRTS permissions gone?
I'm afraid so.
PRTS is actively blocking all of our access. All of its operations take place in a black box now.
Kal'tsit is fighting to take control, but I'm not sure how long she can keep it up.

opts: Cut off the core power supply of PRTS.
Manually activate the backup system and fight for control over the ship.

lines.x: Cutting off the core power supply means going into the Abyss directly. It's risky.
I'll protect the Doctor.
Besides, we're not alone. The elite operators stand with us.
I'm not abandoning the Doctor or Rhodes Island.
My ZOOT system is unable to fully wrestle control from PRTS.
Kal'tsit and I have done simulations about this sort of situation.
By force-booting it with the ship's power, it may be able to temporarily establish control over certain non-core areas...
But all outcomes end with destruction of its core components due to overload.
You've already made a decision, haven't you?
Hah, I can get to work right now.
If I can make one, I can make another.

opts: We will activate the backup command system at the same time as we attempt to penetrate the Abyss.

lines.x: Now, you're finally coming online.
I've waited for this moment for too long. Let me take a good look at you—
Closure awaits with anxious anticipation. She had longed for the day when it would truly come online, just as much as she had feared it.
For it would mean that Rhodes Island was once again in desperate straits—

lines.st: ZOOT command system online. Updating permission log...
Original administrator privileges detected. Confirm key...
Key command 'Machine Oil Aesthete'. Verification complete.
Welcome back, Closure.
Awakening all backup nodes.
Loading...
Warning: Awakening failed. Unable to bypass PRTS restrictions.

lines.x: ...Activate overload.

lines.st: Backup power detected. Source—Rhodes Island mobility layer. 71% remaining.
Warning: Overload mode will greatly reduce equipment lifespan.
Estimated power remaining in minutes: Sixty-one.
Overload mode activated.
Awakening all backup nodes...
Warning: PRTS incursion!
Warning: ZOOT core program is being overwritten—
Warning: Insufficient power to continue operating the ZOOT system—

lines.x: Wait! Hang in there, 'Zeroth Order Oil Tank'!
Dammit. It's all or nothing!

lines.st: Warning: Severe core component damage.
Warning: Insufficient backup power. Time remaining: Twenty-one minutes.
Communications are online.

lines.x: We're being attacked by PRTS, Doctor. ZOOT will coordinate defenses with Kal'tsit.
The rest is up to you!
In position.
Acknowleged.
Waiting for you on the front lines.
Moving to rendezvous point.
Be right there.
I'm here, Doctor.
It's been a long time since we've had a good brawl together!
The Bad Guy has returned. Moving to rendezvous.
Give me a minute.
I'm here.
Assembling.
Waiting for you.

opts: Core objective—enter Abyss and cut off PRTS's core power supply.

lines.st: Progress: ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■ ■
Welcome back, Administrator.
'Life'. A concept so vague, yet so fascinating.
We've gotten used to treating any individual that thinks like us as life, for no other reason than the fact that they're like us.
For no other reason than that we all fight death to the bitter end for our own survival.
But this is hubris, Doctor.

lines.x: Target eliminated.
Stormeye. Four o'clock. Two-round burst fire.
At the wall supports?
Stormeye is confused, but fires two arrows in rapid succession without hesitation.
The environment changed the instant I fired! The obstacle disappeared, and the rounds hit the enemy...
How did you do that?
It wasn't me. It was ZOOT who did the calculations.
Now, jump down at your six o'clock. Don't worry about the Originium shards in the hole.
Hah.
He jumps, and his surroundings change again, causing him to fall endlessly, until his fall is arrested by a rope of incantations.
Closure's calculations were spot-on.
I've been waiting here.
What now?
Follow instructions and go in with eyes shut.

lines.st: We praise the courage of struggle and celebrate the virtue of defiance.
And so the lives that follow the same way of thinking join the procession of slim hope.
Before a true god, however...
Our struggle must seem utterly insignificant to It.
Perhaps It never even took notice.

lines.x: All hostiles in sight have been eliminated.
Quarantine zone line of defense established.
Good job, kitten!
Shame you didn't leave a few for me.
What's next, Closure?
Thirty seconds.
The next wave of enemies will appear at twelve o'clock. Eliminate with extreme prejudice.
It will open a path to the route that the Doctor must take.
Good! More pain for the culprit.
How about I get first dibs this time, kitten?

lines.st: You don't agree with me, do you, Doctor?
There is no certainty that this is the key to the truth. But, after ascertaining that the foreseen despair will destroy all that we worked for—
We must grasp what hope we can reach in our hands.
I have poured too much love into the miracle of life, that I see ever more clearly the weakness and flaws that we cannot cast aside.
How do I trust everyone whom I love? How do I trust... you?

lines.x: Closure! It's not working!
The surrounding structures are beginning to contract! They're trying to crush me!
The drones I sent out aren't finding any viable routes. The reinforced supports won't last long.
I'm working on it!
ZOOT is trying to process a LOT of data simultaneously. Give her some time!
Six, five, four, three, two... one!
Mechanist! Look up!
In the shadow, the ceiling begins to warp, then a cold blade cuts open a path leading to the unknown.
A hand emerges from the gap.
Someone said you needed a hand?
As they say, extraordinary times call for extraordinary measures.
Hang on tight, Mechanist!
Everyone's waiting for you.

lines.st: I cannot accurately predict when we will meet again—
But I am certain that when that time comes, we will have lost all that we have now.
No life can defy death forever, and those who have passed do not meet again at the end of time.
But I hope that I will see you again there... Doctor.

lines.x: (Angry roar)
(Howls)
(Charges energy)
(Fires an energy beam)
The beam burns a massive hole through the blockade of Originium entities, raking its way into the distance, cutting the entities down in swathes—
Then, it is stopped by some kind of force.
Good hit, good hit!
Just be careful you don't hit the Doctor, Mon3tr.
We're in position.
You lead the team out of the darkness.
(Shakes body in joy)

opts: Neutralize all enemies around the Sarcophagus.

lines.x: Coordinate with me.
No problem.
Incantations with you.
Is that the Sarcophagus? It was hidden inside the ship all along?
Kal'tsit's signal is weakening!
We have to get inside.
Is Kal'tsit in there?
It's not looking good for her, based on what Raidian said.
We need to get her out of there ASAP, Dr. Doctor.
I'm worried for her safety, if we delay any longer.
We can't act rashly. We don't know what the situation is with Kal'tsit and the Sarcophagus.
Combat logs indicate that someone came into contact with the Sarcophagus in Chernobog, causing a reaction that nobody expected.
You walk towards the Sarcophagus without hesitation.
You touch the cold Sarcophagus... it answers.
No, Kal'tsit answers.
Dr. Kal'tsit—
You've come at a good time.
What's the situation... of Rhodes Island...?
You extend your hand to the one in the Sarcophagus.
She takes your hand. You have never felt such weakness from her.
Don't worry about me...
It wasn't easy, trying to access PRTS through the Sarcophagus. But I did find a surprising gift.
I'll share more information about PRTS and Priestess with you.
(Anxious howl)
Don't worry, Mon3tr. I'm still standing before you.
I won't abandon you.
I've located a path into the Abyss, Dr. Doctor.
It's time we put an end to this.
The interference is strong here. We're very close to the Abyss.
But there is no path. The Abyss has completely concealed itself—
I can pinpoint its precise location at this range.
Standing before the wall in the deepest reaches, Kal'tsit takes a deep breath.
She has never been this far in—
(Anxious growl)
You walk to Kal'tsit's side.
I'm with you too, Dr. Kal'tsit.
Maybe... things will be different this time.
I have retained certain permissions by way of the Sarcophagus. I'll open a path.
Kal'tsit touches the wall that separates you and the Abyss.

lines.st: Warning: Anomalous power fluctuations detected.
Warning: Insufficient backup power.
Warning: Insufficient engine power. Engines shutting down.
Warning: Mobility layer is offline.
Warning: Top deck is offline.
Warning: Communications are offline.
Warning: Irreparable core component damage.
Sorry, C—Closure—

lines.x: 'Zeroth Order Oil Tank'... thank you.
The ship has stopped.
Protect the Doctor, Mon3tr.
From this point on, act according to your own discretion based on the Doctor's condition. You must protect the Doctor from unknown threats...
...And protect the Doctor from the Doctor, if it comes to that.
(Growl of trust)
Keep a close eye on the condition of Dr. Doctor, Amiya.
The rules we think we know about reality do not necessarily apply inside the Abyss.
Danger is everywhere, but your safety and that of the Doctor are the highest priority at all times. You are the only ones who can face the ultimate threat.
Yes... I'll be careful.

opts: Anything else I need to know?

lines.x: Trust your heart.
I never thought this would come so soon, Dr. Doctor.
But our experiences in the Assimilated Universe and in Trimounts have made me rethink. You have shown me a different possibility.
Whatever happens from this point on is a complete unknown, to you, to me, and to everyone else on Rhodes Island.
I need to hear it from you, Dr.Doctor—
Will you still hesitate in the face of possible danger?

opts: But it's not the danger in front of me that I'm worried about.
You're still trying to hide your own anxiety and uncertainty, Kal'tsit.
You don't need to carry everything on your shoulders.

lines.x: (Pokes Kal'tsit in agreement)

opts: This time, Rhodes Island and I stand with you.
I am not worried about the danger before me, for I am with you. All of you.
You have been on edge ever since we entered this area.

lines.x: (Uneasy growl)

opts: Rhodes Island and I are with you. Trust us.
Tell me what I need to do.

lines.x: I've awakened the program that opens up the Abyss. The energy required to open up the compartment is ready.
But I do not have full permission to access the Abyss.
Huh?
Should we smash our way in, then? Maybe Misery can think of a way.
(Shakes head)
There might not be another place more concealed, more secured than the Abyss in all of Terra. Only one person has the permission to access it.
You feel Kal'tsit's gaze upon you.
...Doctor?
Most of Rhodes Island's structure was badly damaged when I finally found it, seventeen years ago.
Only the Abyss and its surrounding areas remain completely intact.
The unknown energy within the Abyss continued to power PRTS, becoming the framework of the command system that we came to rely on.
But no one had accessed the Abyss... not in over ten thousand years.
Three years ago, when Amnannam fully descended, I began to realize that the crisis was progressing faster than I had expected.
Many of us witnessed the chaos firsthand. We still feel sorrow and regret vividly.
Ascalon raises her blade silently, on guard against any sudden threats.
Sensing Ascalon's tension, Raidian calms her and everyone else present with her Originium Arts.
For three years, we have strengthened Rhodes Island's defenses, simulating threats that attempt to destroy Rhodes Island from within.
It was through the joint effort of all our operators that we managed to quickly identify the source of the crisis when it occurred.
But we are now at the edge of the fog. Take one step further, and we step into the unknown.
Once, I hesitated. But the Doctor is right. We will overcome this crisis, as we have done so many times.
For we are together.
Logos's bone pen draws through the air, the words coming out of his mouth, and the blessings soon after.
It's been a long time since we worked together so in sync, Logos. How are you feeling?
Focused.
Based on available information, there are more than just the core parts of PRTS in the Abyss.
An entity by the name of 'Priestess' may be sleeping in a Sarcophagus within.
If Priestess wakes up, she must be neutralized ASAP with the full force of all present.
It is the best way to prevent any mishap, Dr. Doctor.
The moment of her awakening will be her weakest... it will take time for her to adapt to her surroundings.
Doctor...?
You touch the wall in front of you. The strange sensation causes your heart to race.
You have the feeling that this place was waiting for you... that you are the reason for its existence to begin with.
The wall trembles. You seem to hear blaring sirens that threaten to burst your eardrums.
For an instant, you fall into a trance. Does the cacophony come from the present, or the past?
The Abyss is changing!
The wall begins to move irregularly, creating a rift.
Air gushes out from the rift.
'Veil.'
The Banshee's incantation forms an invisible barrier that stops the gushing jet.
Step back, Doctor.
Everyone, on guard.
(High-pitched howl)

lines.st: ...Dr.Doctor...
...I never thought I would be the one who didn't want to let go.
Kal'tsit
Protect the Doctor, Amiya.
Amiya
Mhm.

lines.x: Pink threads rise from her staff. The Black Crown appears above her head.

lines.st: Raidian
Electromagnetic communications established.

lines.x: You hear Mantra's voice in your mind—

lines.st: Mantra
If you need any help, just give a thought. We're here.
Stormeye
How about you cover my flank, Sharp?
Sharp
Then make sure you hold your bow tight.
I am sure that the connection between us will transcend even time and space, Dr. Doctor.
I am sure we will meet again. At the far end of our civilization, once adorned by darkness and the brilliance of the stars up above, we will meet once again.
I will wait for that day.
Blaze
Give a holler if you need help, kitten!
Rosmontis
Heh.
Pith
Our Arts are being suppressed by the environment, but the Originium Arc seems to work.
Touch
Strike with boldness.
Misery
I can go in first to check things out—
Kal'tsit
But that makes me even more worried.
Amiya
I'll go with you.
Mon3tr
(Firm growl)
Kal'tsit
Get ready, folks. The fate of Rhodes Island is in our hands.

lines.x: You step into the space within the rift.

lines.st: I will wait until that day comes. I promise you I will. Wait for me.
You must wait for me too.

lines.x: Theresis falls in the ocean of information. The massive quantity buffets him, assimilating his cognition.
He can feel his mind grow slow, but he still tries to follow the guidance left behind by Theresa—
Guided by his sword, the information flow reconstructed by Theresa clashes with the preexisting one.
The massive collision lasts only an instant.
The surface begins to surge. Then, the sea splits open, as if sliced by a sharp blade.
A dark abyss appears in the Assimilated Universe. The sea of data stirs and roils.
Waves crashing against waves, drowning out one another.
Even as his mind grows numb, Theresis can sense the will gazing upon him weaken rapidly.
Your plan worked, Theresa.
Sever their control over Amnannam and seize it—
But suddenly, all the violent scenes come to a screeching halt.
She opens her eyes and sees the small, frail creature.
(Unknown language) Connection... unstable.
(Unknown language) —It's you?
(Unknown language) Your actions surprise me.
(Unknown language) ...'Theresa' and 'Theresis'.
Theresis does not comprehend the space before him. Perhaps it is simply another illusion of a dying mind—
Until he hears a familiar language that he can understand.
Ah, but perhaps this was the result that one wanted to see?
Your intentions are as easy to decipher as your language.
The evolution of Terra had brought many surprises... a shame.
You think we failed.
Then why do you hide and refuse to show yourself?
He meditatively swings his sword, guiding the chaotic information through the pure-white space...
Falsehoods.
You've implemented a backdoor in my project and restricted my access.
The data tells me I'm losing this piece of Originium. My attempts at maintenance have been in vain.
Impressive. Your rash actions have borne fruit.
There is nothing physically imposing to the voice whispering about failure before Theresis.
But this simply serves to make him more wary.
Your arrogance reminds me of someone.
Someone who deceived me. There is more than one evil spirit of a bygone age.
Dr. Doctor... I see you've met.
Then let me introduce myself.
Priestess. Linguist, and colleague to Dr. Doctor.
Theresis cannot care less who Priestess is.
Priestess or evil spirit, it makes no difference to him.
He clutches his sword tightly. He will find a way to break through.
You are anxious to defy a reality that you cannot perceive, Theresis.
You were much calmer when you plotted with Doctor on the border of Kazdel.
You were watching...
Watching? No.
I have long lost the energy and passion to observe the evolution of a newborn civilization.
All I did was look up the data newly added to Originium, and checked some information that interested me.
The expansion of Originium is a consensus between me and Doctor.
The current circumstances of Terra have somewhat differed from our predictions, but it remains on track.
I admit to being surprised by your attempt to retake this Originium, but the duty of it will be fulfilled, no matter how you struggle.
You can believe what you want, creator of Originium.
You believe that Originium is immovable, but she opened a path here, and liberated the myriad souls.
She was not the first to resist, and I will not be the last. Someone will stand before you—
Until there is none left to toy with our fate.
'Fate'... do you continue to believe that Originium stands opposed to the fate of your civilization?
If you truly understand the history of your people, have you ever questioned why you have evolved in our likeness?
You do not understand what you are resisting.
Theresis observes his surroundings. He is certain that he is still inside Originium.
The nature of what makes up this place is no different from the tower that Theresa weaved—it is information reconstructed.
Theresa has left him the key. He is sure he can put an end to this enigma.
You noticed.
You do not have a real physical form. We're still in that ocean.
In the language of Originium, reality and falsehood are not polar opposites.
When I first created this language, I wanted it to decipher the ultimate question that we had been unable to answer, despite all our efforts.
It.
Doctor once had hope for the beings on this land... guiding them to evolve and seek hope through Originium, giving them a chance to face It, perhaps even overcome It.
But they are doomed to despair.
'Teekaz'. You were not part of the plan, but you became the species most affected by Originium. An accident.
I must applaud your courage for standing here.
An accident...? Is this how you summarize our fate?
You're certainly alike, Priestess.
You call the fates you toy with a gift of hope, millennia of suffering a necessary sacrifice.
But Doctor was honest, at least.
His sword pierces falsehood, but the space on the other side—
Nothing changed?
Theresis closes his eyes and raises his sword.
Your violence is meaningless.
If you didn't care whether this pathetic race of mine can seize its own fate, you should have eliminated me already.
You're still weak after awakening from a long dream, are you not, Presence?
He lunges without hesitation.
The sword-wave cuts through the figure, disappearing without a sound into the pale white wall in the distance.
I said, this is meaningless.
Another strike.
I've always wondered—what exactly is the difference between you, the self-proclaimed creators, and us?
Theresis holds nothing back. The white wall begins to crack.
You have not brought everything to a standstill.
You just wanted to buy more time, or simply to mock the struggles of a pathetic race...
Suit yourself, Priestess.
Once again, Theresis senses the surging tide of information, remnants of the broken tower that Theresa weaved.
Theresis swings again.
Golden seawater shatters the pale wall, rushing into this seemingly boundless white space.
The tide swallows all that it sees, but parts before the figure of Priestess...
Like a static comet, leaving behind a black tail of calm in the sea of surging fury.
Your connection to the Originium is weakening. You are fading away.
Continuing to process this disorganized data is a waste of energy.
The waves converge upon the last area, slowly submerging Priestess's figure.
They also close in upon Theresis.
Is this the end that you sought?
The Sarkaz data has corrupted part of the Assimilated Universe. The data has been deleted, but I do not have time right now to repair the rewritten structures.
You can 'survive' in the Assimilated Universe in another form.
Survive?
I came to pass judgment upon the gods.
The Sarkaz swings his sword at the creator one last time, before the torrent of information engulfs him.

lines.st: Warning.
Unknown threat.
Identifying error code... identification failed.
Simulating path... simulation failed.
Rebooting bottom-level computing area...
Assessing danger level...
Repeat.

lines.x: And there I was, thinking that all I had to do—after I find a successor—was hand out candies to the children.
You have never proclaimed such a revelation before.
(Unknown language) Incoming data... Originium...
(Unknown language) A warning, or yet another swan song of hope?
(Unknown language) Where are you, kindred? What have you encountered?
The wind brushes past the throne. The sovereign's gaze moves away from the depths of the void outside his domain.
This music exists outside of order. How did it invade my Pavillon, my Leithanien?
The traces of genesis?
Silence is broken.
Priestess's figure, shattered by the golden waves of information, disappears without a trace.
The sea of data dashes against Theresis even more furiously.
His mind grows crowded. His body begins to crack.
The waves of information take away the blood that flows from his wounds. Originium crystals multiply in every cell...
Theresis is completely inundated.
Blood-red seawater gushes from the bottomless abyss, melting into the golden sea.
The sea of data in the Assimilated Universe turns a dark red, like blood, the color of the Sarkaz.
Finally, a tiny figure rises from the sea.
We took control of Amnannam, Theresa.
I can feel the direction that the waves flow now, and the massive amounts of data that I cannot comprehend—
He wants to share his joy, but there is only the sound of the waves in the boundless void.
He can no longer hear even his own heartbeat. There is only him here.
There is no longer any meaning in staying here.
He will return alone to Terra, to the land that belongs to him—
The rhombus in the void grows faint and broken...
Priestess's consciousness once again falls into slumber.
The spread of Originium has been far slower than expected, and she has unexpectedly lost part of her control over it.
Two Terrans, a frail and overlooked race.
I'm starting to understand why you're so interested in them... Doctor.
As her consciousness fades into darkness, she turns her gaze to the land known as Terra...
She sees the one they call Doctor walking with the Cautus and AMa-10 into the dark passage that leads to the underground throne room—
She sees the city of Londinium cry out in pain in the fires of war—
...She sees death—
A disintegrating Cautus, whose information is melting into the Assimilated Universe's sea of data—
Then, she reaches out.
(Unknown language) And that was the last time you saw Priestess?
(Unknown language) Yes.
(Unknown language) I still have many unanswered questions. Time is no longer my major concern.
(Unknown language) Do you not miss your home, traveler?
'Miss.'
—The Cautus answered this question many times when she lived on the ship that housed the gods.
There is no future in the fragments of time reconstructed from information.
Her best friends soon forget her existence entirely.
She can know all there it is to them, but she will never truly be part of life here.
'I'm just a traveler lucky enough to catch a glimpse of the distant past.'
(Unknown language) I'm having another learning session with Presence.
(Unknown language) Are you sure you don't want to come and talk to her?
(Unknown language) You seem eager to resolve our conflict, Hierda, but I'm afraid I must decline.
They will not change.
They are simply recreations of data.
(Unknown language) Very well.
The Cautus looks out beyond the porthole and counts silently. When the count reaches thirty, life from the star will once again shine upon the surface of the broken planet.
It's breath-taking, no matter how many times I see it...
Life and death emerging at the same time—
A scene that she had never seen on that land. A scene that she does not wish her brethren to see.

lines.st: 'Farewell. I'm leaving.'

lines.x: In the blink of an eye, the Cautus finds herself back on that pale soil.
Priestess welcomes her with a smile.
Have you found your answer?
The Cautus shakes her head.
Where are you going?
Terra. Your home.
I want to give you a choice, Hierda.
You can stay, learn the knowledge that you thirst for, and find answers to the questions in your heart.
Or, I can send you back home.
Home...? To Terra?!
However, it may mean forsaking forever the chance to find those answers.
What's more, you will find that you have become very different from your erstwhile kin. It will bring you even more pain.
I will respect your decision, Hierda.
I, I—
At the same time, you step into the space within the rift.
(Angry roar)
Did PRTS bring us out here?
It's not that simple. We are the only ones here.
The deck is completely silent.
You turn around and notice the absence of the elite operators and Ascalon—
No. Massive amounts of Originium dust. Protect yourself.
Protect the Doctor, Mon3tr.
Dr. Kal'tsit! Beneath our feet!
The deck is crystallizing rapidly. At this rate...
Black Originium dust accumulates on the deck. Tiny Originium clusters grow like wild grass.
You can see them spreading as far as one's eyes can reach, gleaming enchantingly in the sun.
Warn all operators still on Rhodes Island to be prepared for Catastrophe, Amiya.

Understood.
I'm not getting through! All communications have failed—
The sight before you is inconceivable, different from any Catastrophe that you have witnessed before.

opts: The Originium expands at an impossible pace.

lines.x: You look down at the deck, now completely covered in Originium, and see only your shadow.
Doctor—
You turn around to see a stunned look on Kal'tsit's face.
You follow her gaze. Far in the distance on the deck, a small formation of black Originium is growing slowly towards the sky.
You see a faint white figure on every facet of the crystal.
A figure not unfamiliar to you—
The black Originium rain suddenly grows more intense.
It almost feels like the large black raindrops are not falling from the sky, but rushing from the ground towards the heavens.
They refract the sunlight, drawing colorful lines in the sky—
All the colors you see begin to converge and weave into a white curtain, glistening with rainbow colors, slowly descending.
A pure, pristine white, gentle draping over the Originium crystal on the deck—
A beautiful figure stands upon the crystal.
Black crystal retreats from her legs, and white light puts a layer of clothing on her.
Heaven and earth, white and black, and—
She looks upon the land that has long become unfamiliar to her.
Long time no see.
The connection between us will transcend even time and space. I am sure of it.

Even if the ocean completely boils away and the atmosphere vanishes, even if all the moons in the sky are pulled into the vortex of our planet's gravity, and even if the sun continues to mercilessly expand, eventually swallowing up all its children until none can be heard...
I am sure we will meet again. At the far end of our civilization, once adorned by darkness and the brilliance of the stars up above, we will meet once again.
I will wait until that day comes. I promise you I will. Wait for me. You must wait for me too.
...Don't ever forget about me.
Is it time...?
I should still be alive. This can't be an illusion.
This is the Rhodes Island I'm familiar with, and it seems to be operating as usual. Therefore, It is definitely not here yet.
Is she still asleep? What of Originium's current state...?
And as for the changes happening to this planet...
You're still here, AMa-10.
Perfect... Let's go for a walk outside.
From a distance away, a black-haired woman dressed in a white coat stands on the deck, concentrating her gaze at the sky.
Until she notices the two of you and turns around, a bright smile on her face.
You are absolutely certain you have seen this face before.
Perhaps in a dream within dreams, or in the depths of your dormant memories.
But this moment is no dream.
You can clearly perceive her presence and existence right before you with each of your senses.
Doctor, this woman... is the Priestess you talked about?
The enemy Dr. Kal'tsit has been guarding against...?
Amiya does not understand the danger she faces, but still she stands in front of you instinctively, ready to engage in combat.
You are unable to determine how exactly the current situation will unfold, but know very well this is not at all like any powerful foe or crisis you have faced before.
The woman seems to show no hostility, but still your instinct tells you—unfathomable danger lies right before your eyes.
Gently, she begins to speak.
You're a step ahead of me, Doctor.
You didn't wait for me in the awakening room, to share a mutual joy in seeing this new world for the first time.
But how could I possibly blame you for a little impatience?
You also feel her pure, sincere gaze.
From the distance wind blows and thunder rolls, along with the faint rustling of Originium spreading.
Within the clamor and chaos of this space, she fixes her eyes on you as if nothing else exists.
This reunion is not what I expected, but I'm willing to accept an unorthodox experience.
We've dreamed together too many times to count, how we would meet again when we awoke from our deep sleep.
We could wander freely on this planet completely covered by Originium, in a world that belonged only to us.
A world only one step away from our shared, ultimate vision, and we'd walk towards our new hope...
It ought to be a bright, sunny day too.
Doctor, look! The clouds in the sky—
Amiya hurriedly shouts a warning at you when she notices the sky's unnatural appearance.
But you fail to comprehend just what your eyes are currently seeing.
As soon as Priestess finishes speaking, thick Catastrophe clouds begin to contract and fold in a neat yet strange manner until they vanish completely, leaving a canvas-like sky.
Catastrophes, the most horrific disasters to happen to this land—gone, just like that.
You realize a terrible truth.
Doctor, your question sounds like you're asking a stranger.
We created it together. A crystallization that belongs to only the two of us, a seed of hope.
But tragically... you don't remember anymore, do you?
I know you lost part of your memories in a series of unfortunate accidents.
It's a pity, but I believe your partial memory loss will not change your nature, and the connection between us remains unshakable.
I've no wish to be harsh on you, but I hope you can understand—time is short, and we have much to do.
Though, we can still enjoy our time working together, yes?
Return to my side, Dr. Doctor...

lines.st: Let Terra crystallize into Originium.
Create a bridge to the future.

lines.x: The gentle call seems to ring suddenly in your ears, and you feel incomparably close to the voice.
You are certain you cannot recall a single shred of her in your mind, yet your connection goes beyond memories, or perhaps information contained in language.
An almost instinctive reaction.
You cannot help but take a step forward.
Dr. Doctor!
Kal'tsit is behind you, gripping your arm. You feel feeble tremors through her palm.
You realize now she has been silent the whole time. Since leaving the sarcophagus, she has been so weak, at this point, she even seems to be struggling to stand.
You are baffled.
You expected Kal'tsit to give you some sage advice, like usual, or perhaps a simple and clear solution.
But now she seems to be suffering from terrible torment, her face bearing struggle and pain.
After an endless instant, she loosens her grip.
She leaves the choice for you to make.

opts: I won't let Originium destroy Terra.

lines.x: You see her eyes darken. A clear sign of disappointment. You don't know why, but your heart suddenly tightens.
So it's you... AMa-10. It's been a long time for us too.
Your current appearance is vastly different from the look you were born with, but I certainly recognize my very own creation that I poured my heart into.
In the past four million plus natural day cycles, you seem to have exerted a lot of effort, but your behavioral standards appear to clash with the preset goal encoded at your birth.
I'd like to know just what changed your mind.
Unlike your despicable fraud...
...My end goal has never changed.
Indeed... you regarded that one's order as an inviolable mandate.
So you hindered the Originium plan, and wasted our already limited time.
So, Doctor, how should I make you understand—
Just how disappointed I am?
And how should I forgive your betrayal?

opts: Kal'tsit, what is the meaning of this?

lines.x: I've noticed, AMa-10.
You've already broken the language ban I put in place, correct?
Such a precaution was not enough to restrict your actions, for you always find a way...
But if that's the case, why not just say it?
Everything you've wanted to say to Doctor this whole time, but couldn't?
Priestess...
*Expletive from an unknown language*.
Ascalon and the Elite Operators are momentarily stunned after entering the rift.
This is the Abyss?!
Raidian, can you confirm if we're still inside the Rhodes Island landship?
Unable to detect any electromagnetic signals outside this space. Hm... no way to tell.
Everyone falls into brief silence, waiting for a commander's directive as usual.
Where's the Doctor?
And where are Dr. Kal'tsit and Amiya?
Once we entered the rift, I lost track of their positions.
Constant white noise fills the vast white space, the sound of operating machinery emanating from the center.
If this place is indeed the Abyss, then clearly it's distorting our perception of physical space.
Mantra's voice comes to everyone's minds as they learn this at the same time.
There's no way to identify the material the walls are made of.
He looks back in the rift's direction, but it is no longer there.
Currently there's no way to cut open these strange walls, so there's no way to confirm their whereabouts.
I trust Amiya and Dr. Kal'tsit will manage to keep the Doctor safe.
We can only wait and adapt accordingly.
We haven't received any new commands, so we should continue executing our original task.
Then let's not delay any longer.
Mechanist, Sharp and I will cover you while you close in on that unknown machine. Verify if its related to PRTS.

We can back you up anytime—
Something's off. There are no high-threat targets here.
That's unusual.
According to Dr. Kal'tsit and the Doctor's speculations, the Originium entities we've run into all come from the Abyss...
But there's like no defenses installed here at all—
I hear... pain.
What's wrong, kitten?
Rosmontis, do you feel any other abnormalities within your body?
No, it's not my own...
A huge black vortex spins slowly under the transparent floor. It starts to speed up.
The unidentifiable instrument right above it begins to flash red.
Detecting a large amount of interference signals.
That instrument is currently transmitting a huge volume of data—
Could the black vortex underneath... be a visualization of all that information?

lines.st: PRTS Defense system active.
Identifying threat targets... Identities confirmed.
Loading threat targets data files, generating targeted countermeasure strategies...
Calculation complete.
This is a final warning from PRTS.
Leave the Abyss, intruders.
Beginning countdown—

lines.x: Gah, so we're intruders now?
Doesn't look like you're giving us any option to leave though.
So, Ascalon?
Sweep up Rhodes Island's trash.
Nice, I like that.
Don't care where all this stuff came from, but we'll let them know just to whom Rhodes Island belongs.

lines.st: Countdown complete.
Executing cleanup protocol—
