lines.x: Dr. Doctor, ever since you woke up in Chernobog, I have refrained from confessing the whole truth about your past.
The ban placed on me is only part of the cause.
As for the other reasons... I have said before no one else should tell you about your past.
Language has no way of reconstructing objective facts, while emotions will distort its delivery.
So I needed you to see and judge for yourself, to re-evaluate this land on your own.
Only then could you formulate your own answers about how you regard this land, and about yourself.
I still believe it's important to do it this way.
The same way someone once treated me.

opts: I am the Doctor of Rhodes Island.
I am a soul walking this vast land.

lines.x: This may be the last chance I have to share that information, however.
Perhaps only now do I, too, have the confidence to share it after all that you and Rhodes Island have been through together.
Please trust that I talk only of your past, and it has nothing to do with your future.
Kal'tsit walks up by your side, facing you and Amiya.
She slowly begins to speak.
Dr. Doctor, you ought to already know that Originium, a bearer of both life and danger to Terra, is the legacy of a previous age.
It was one of countless methods used by the previous civilization against an ultimate threat.
After countless efforts and sacrifices, Originium was finally completed by Oracle and Priestess.
'Oracle' was your previous code name.

opts: ...I was the one who brought suffering upon this land?

lines.x: Please know that was not your intention.
Originium points to two futures for this land... Oracle hoped to leave Originium as a gift for future civilizations, to guide them forward...
To help them enter the next stage of civilization.
Over the past ten thousand years, Originium has helped civilization on Terra to develop, but also unexpectedly brought suffering to the land via Oripathy.
You were indeed tormented by this before.
I still remember... when the Doctor...
The Doctor has never given up the possibility of curing Oripathy.
However, someone forced a different future upon you. And for this... you made a choice that went against your original intentions.
Someone once told me to trust in your character, to look for your true self, beyond what memory shapes you. It was an incomparably difficult path for me to walk.
But I'm glad... it was the correct choice to make.
Dr. Doctor, that's all I wanted to tell you. They are only your past, and you will always be able to decide your own future.
Then I too... will fulfill my oath.
Dr. Doctor, I will protect you and Amiya until the end.
This is not the best opportunity to do so, but it is my final one.
I'm sorry, to entrust such a risk to you and Amiya... but this is a choice I must make.
You see Kal'tsit's mouth move, her final words.
Are you done? You've said what you wanted to say?
I'm astonished by how much you know about the past, and I don't completely agree with how you've described it... but that's no longer important.
AMa-10, what can you even achieve as you are now?
...Mon3tr!
(Excited whinny)
Destroy her.
She is the Priestess of a previous civilization. Creator of Originium, and attempting to assimilate everything within it.
And therefore, the enemy of all life on Terra.
So this is your choice...
I'm Kal'tsit, a doctor of Rhodes Island, and also the companion of Amiya and the Doctor.
You have long been worthless to me.
You seem a bit down, Doctor.
Care to share the reason with me?
QHU9112, another fading star system.
It's a new high tide. What surrounds us is merely the first wave, It's arriving much faster than we anticipated.
We don't have much time left...
But we're prepared, yes?
We've planted a seed of hope, and now we need only wait for it to grow a solution.
Don't fret. Treat it as a regular hibernation. No different than prior time travels, just a little longer.
And this time, I will be there by your side.
I hope after crossing the quiet, endless night, the first thing I see when awake is you.
We still haven't figured out a solution from our debates...
Originium is our last hope, and we have few alternatives. We must think twice before we choose our path.
Priestess gently holds the hand of the person by her side.
She does not speak her thoughts, but they transform into images within this mind-resonance room—visuals symbolizing happiness and hope.
Her gaze is sincere and clear.
I've never forgotten our matter of debate, Doctor. We shouldn't hold any reservations towards each other.
Look, this is in fact my mindset when we discuss our future. We should harbor the same set of expectations.
The identical lynchpins embedded in our minds are what tie us together.
Our minds are inseparably linked, until our consciousnesses follow suit with the universe's demise.
We do clash and argue endlessly, but that is precisely the reason why we most understand each other's disposition.
I know for certain it is in our nature to harbor hope for the future. We serve as anchors for one another in this hollow sea of stars.
Even if we can't agree on our differing views on Originium? Our planned paths for it can't coexist...
Priestess, I'm not sure...
Then let's continue to wait.
It's fine if you're not willing to hibernate yet. We still have some time.

We can read the classics stored inside DWDB together, or interpret the many verses left by dead planets in this universe.
Before you make up your mind, I'll never force you—
I'll never impose my own will on yours.
I'll wait, Doctor, until you make the choice to stand by my side.
Priestess...
Take it easy, Doctor. Your recent observations must have exhausted you.
Close your eyes and rest a while. I'll play you a chord on the gravitational forces of distant stars, and you'll enjoy a sweet dream.

lines.st: Goodnight, Doctor.
Until the end of time, when we have transcended the silence of the universe.
I will wait for you in that world.

lines.x: (Excited roar)
Mon3tr appears by your side, but its stance is slightly different than usual.
Without a command from Kal'tsit, it zips towards the enemy, although a cluster of Originium rises from the ground, fully encasing the monster within.
You really have surprised me, AMa-10.
You've broken free of my control at the price of severing your own gemini cycle system, even if this makes your life now utterly fragile.
However, do you really intend to kill me via this method?
I knew from a long time ago your consciousness was already assimilated into the information within Originium.
Under these circumstances, it is impossible to truly kill you.
But what Theresa and Theresis accomplished convinced me of something.
Your control over Originium is not absolute, and there still exist some methods to limit your authority over it.
I did meet the two you speak of, within the Originium.
Their efforts were indeed unexpected, and a pleasantly surprising struggle.
But what of it? How are you so sure now that killing me here won't be a futile act?
When Mon3tr attacked you, you chose to defend yourself.
How did you do it?
I see you have noticed.
Based on Theresa's research into Originium as well as the trap you set for Dr. Doctor, I added a few plans to your awakening room.
As long as you wake up here, your consciousness will be trapped in this body, unable to return to Originium's sea of information.
Perhaps you will eventually manage to break through this restriction, and regain control over Originium, but right now you are merely human.
To think you'd go this far to rebel against me—
Priestess, you are not Terra's creator. The life on this land has no obligation to bend to your will.
...This is my revenge on you, and my final act of resistance.
You see the woman standing across from you reveal a hint of hesitation, but immediately after, Originium surrounds her.
...It's too late.
(Agitated roaring)
Dr. Kal'tsit... it's my turn to protect you now.
Black Arts crash and smash the Originium cluster, freeing Mon3tr as it quickly resumes its attack.
Originium crystals begin to grow again, but it's too late to form a barrier strong enough to resist Mon3tr's claws.
You see a nervous look on Priestess's face, a show of fear from the creator towards her creation.
As if Priestess finally recognizes the threat she is facing, the Originium on the entire deck responds to her consciousness and begins to rapidly grow.
Mon3tr has already closed in on its prey.
Five on the right confirmed paralyzed.
'Perish.'
A bit scary at first, but in the end it just brought out enemies we've beaten before. Easy peasy.
Eat this now—
Am I just imagining it, or are our enemies spawning slower now?
This is PRTS's core system. The whole time it's been copying data of enemies we've faced in the past and pasting them here.
But now it's reading the data so slowly, like something's hindering it...
It seems like whatever the Doctor and the rest are doing is taking effect.
Let's seize this chance and smash it.
We need to reach the Abyss's depths... there are people waiting for us.
Mon3tr, meltdown.
Black Arts and lasers take turns shooting at the gigantic and complex crystal cluster. The fierce assault even manages to suppress the Originium from spreading further.
Until the light of Arts swallow Priestess's figure.
Smoke and dust scatter, leaving only Originium fragments on the ground.
And a tattered white coat.
I can't feel... any signs of life, or any emotions...
Maybe...
Everything happens so suddenly you haven't had the time to regard the series of events as reality.
You saw that fearsome foe reduced to ashes right before your eyes.
Yet you feel no relief at all.
Are we quite finished playing yet?
How—
Doctor, Amiya, fall back!
A powerful show of resistance, AMa-10. Your plan has proven effective.
As you say, there is indeed a chance for you to kill me now...
But how does a single substitute made of Originium fluster you so?
AMa-10, I have no intention of debating the value of your beliefs.
The principles you have always insisted on are so insignificant I can't even find a credible logical framework to assess their significance.
It is not uncommon for experiments to turn out like this—small errors ultimately growing into huge obstacles. I have no choice but to accept this result.
I'm very sorry, but I must treat you as an error, and wipe you out.
Priestess raises her hand.
Originium begins to grow once more. Thorned crystal branches tightly envelop you and Amiya in a second, nearly swallowing you both.
Amiya tries her best to shatter the surrounding Originium with her Arts, but the crystal clusters only grow faster.
Doctor! Get behind me—
Mon3tr—
It all happens in a flash of light.
You see the Originium crystal cluster near you explode. Amiya wanted to protect you, but time does not allow for her reaction.
You see the black monster rush over, blocking you both from the cluster with its large, solid body.
You see Kal'tsit...
What a pity.
Doctor, do you ever doubt the meaning in your own existence? I do.
I understand my mission clearly. It is difficult, but I recognize its meaning.
As for the future, I believe you will find an answer. I can only persevere in that belief.
Don't spare too much worry for me. Rhodes Island shouldn't be so fragile that one person's departure would leave it unable to proceed on.
My hope?
It is to protect yours and Amiya's hopes.
Though our definitions of 'protect' have nothing in common, it's an oath I will never abandon. I will be standing here with you, until the very end.
We are unable to forget the past, but a different future may await you.
I have found the meaning of my life.
Please lead Rhodes Island...
And keep going.
Doctor.

lines.st: Unconsciously, I seem to have grown accustomed to having someone at my side, guiding me.
Until now. I finally am sure of what I want to do.
No matter what happens, I will continue down this path. I will not abandon hope.
But...
...What about you?

lines.x: Dr. Kal'tsit—！
It all happens so quick, but you still manage to comprehend the cruel truth.
The foe you all face is incomprehensible by conventional means. She has authority over all Originium.
The bit of Originium on Kal'tsit's right shoulder is proof that she was born human, but also what steals her life away.
Originium crystals begin to spread as they swallow her entire body, only to shatter as the wind blows them away—your eyes fail to capture the entire process.
With strength, comes fragility.
A long life ends here.
She has kept her promise.
We've traveled far enough, and I fear there's no longer a point in continuing our exploration.
Ten thousand day cycles have passed since I entered hibernation, but Terra's changes have been limited.
The spark of life from Talos-II is still weak. The other seeds of hope planted by my former companions have shown no signs of sprouting either.
(Expressing dismay)
Are you disappointed? No... I have expected this result long ago, actually.
I miscalculated the pace at which everything would develop. It's not the right time, and there's only so much I can do.
Time... I just need more time.
I can't continue wasting the life of this body. I have to return to sleep inside the sarcophagus, and wait for an opportunity more ripe than this.
...I have to admit, I broke my promise, and this is a sort of betrayal.
But it is because I still love life that I must have hope. There is no other way.
Though I fear she's already anticipated my early awakening.
Inside that sarcophagus, I discovered a lynchpin-modifying program.
(Expressing confusion)
Technology born in a lost age, used to strengthen our thoughts and conviction. Without it, many would lose the will needed to make it through that period of endless despair.
She and I, we went through it together...
She knows and understands me best, of course, but it is also why our differing opinions are my deepest regret.
She anticipated my actions, and the hidden program within the sarcophagus activated when I woke up. It can modify my cognition, and the purpose of our 'debates'...
And I must return to that same sole sarcophagus to extend my life, even if that means...
I don't have much time left.
No, I shouldn't call you by that anymore.
It's a code name, an identifier for a mechanical creation. I should give you a true name.
Calcite... birefringent crystals capable of refracting a single light source into two polarized lights. A beautiful phenomenon, like you.
Calcite... Kal'tsit... that'll do. Do you like this name?
(Expressing joy)
There are some things I won't have time to do. I hope you can finish them for me.
Kal'tsit, I hope you can protect the life on this great land.
New civilizations are always fragile, but harsh environments will show no mercy.
It is arrogant to interfere with a civilization's development, but we don't have many chances left. This may be our last opportunity.
Civilizations tend to repeat the same mistakes—sinking into violence, unverifiable thought pitfalls, and dead ends of self doubt...
There's no escaping all of that, but just a tiny deviation is enough to wreck a whole civilization. The existence of civilization itself is already a sort of miracle...
So, Kal'tsit, I hope you will guide them.
Your gemini cycle grants you a practically infinite life. You can follow immature civilizations as they develop, and guide them onto a path forward with your knowledge.
It's an arduous mission, but you might manage it, you should—
A moment of silence.
The passionate speech comes to a halt. Oracle looks at the slender, delicate mechanical life, then gazes at the distant mountains half buried by sandstorms.
No...
...Forget what I just said, Kal'tsit. All of it.
Go, Kal'tsit. Go look around you, then see what lies by the most distant of mountains. Go seek what forms existence takes.
...I must go back. Wake me up when you need to.
Kal'tsit, I hope you'll... Or maybe I should say, the only mission I assign you now, is this:
Find yourself.
Search for traces of life. Search for hope and a future.
Kal'tsit... Go find your own answer.

lines.st: ...Kal'tsit.
Have you found the meaning of your life yet?

lines.x: (Excited roar)
Mon3tr appears by your side, but its stance is slightly different than usual.
Without a command from Kal'tsit, it zips towards the enemy, although a cluster of Originium rises from the ground, fully encasing the monster within.
You really have surprised me, AMa-10.
You've broken free of my control at the price of severing your own gemini cycle system, even if this makes your life now utterly fragile.
However, do you really intend to kill me via this method?
I knew from a long time ago your consciousness was already assimilated into the information within Originium.
Under these circumstances, it is impossible to truly kill you.
But what Theresa and Theresis accomplished convinced me of something.
Your control over Originium is not absolute, and there still exist some methods to limit your authority over it.
I did meet the two you speak of, within the Originium.
Their efforts were indeed unexpected, and a pleasantly surprising struggle.
But what of it? How are you so sure now that killing me here won't be a futile act?
When Mon3tr attacked you, you chose to defend yourself.
How did you do it?
I see you have noticed.
Based on Theresa's research into Originium as well as the trap you set for Dr. Doctor, I added a few plans to your awakening room.
As long as you wake up here, your consciousness will be trapped in this body, unable to return to Originium's sea of information.
Perhaps you will eventually manage to break through this restriction, and regain control over Originium, but right now you are merely human.
To think you'd go this far to rebel against me—
Priestess, you are not Terra's creator. The life on this land has no obligation to bend to your will.
...This is my revenge on you, and my final act of resistance.
You see the woman standing across from you reveal a hint of hesitation, but immediately after, Originium surrounds her.
...It's too late.
(Agitated roaring)
Dr. Kal'tsit... it's my turn to protect you now.
Black Arts crash and smash the Originium cluster, freeing Mon3tr as it quickly resumes its attack.
Originium crystals begin to grow again, but it's too late to form a barrier strong enough to resist Mon3tr's claws.
You see a nervous look on Priestess's face, a show of fear from the creator towards her creation.
As if Priestess finally recognizes the threat she is facing, the Originium on the entire deck responds to her consciousness and begins to rapidly grow.
Mon3tr has already closed in on its prey.
Five on the right confirmed paralyzed.
'Perish.'
A bit scary at first, but in the end it just brought out enemies we've beaten before. Easy peasy.
Eat this now—
Am I just imagining it, or are our enemies spawning slower now?
This is PRTS's core system. The whole time it's been copying data of enemies we've faced in the past and pasting them here.
But now it's reading the data so slowly, like something's hindering it...
It seems like whatever the Doctor and the rest are doing is taking effect.
Let's seize this chance and smash it.
We need to reach the Abyss's depths... there are people waiting for us.
Mon3tr, meltdown.
Black Arts and lasers take turns shooting at the gigantic and complex crystal cluster. The fierce assault even manages to suppress the Originium from spreading further.
Until the light of Arts swallow Priestess's figure.
Smoke and dust scatter, leaving only Originium fragments on the ground.
And a tattered white coat.
I can't feel... any signs of life, or any emotions...
Maybe...
Everything happens so suddenly you haven't had the time to regard the series of events as reality.
You saw that fearsome foe reduced to ashes right before your eyes.
Yet you feel no relief at all.
Are we quite finished playing yet?
How—
Doctor, Amiya, fall back!
A powerful show of resistance, AMa-10. Your plan has proven effective.
As you say, there is indeed a chance for you to kill me now...
But how does a single substitute made of Originium fluster you so?
AMa-10, I have no intention of debating the value of your beliefs.
The principles you have always insisted on are so insignificant I can't even find a credible logical framework to assess their significance.
It is not uncommon for experiments to turn out like this—small errors ultimately growing into huge obstacles. I have no choice but to accept this result.
I'm very sorry, but I must treat you as an error, and wipe you out.
Priestess raises her hand.
Originium begins to grow once more. Thorned crystal branches tightly envelop you and Amiya in a second, nearly swallowing you both.
Amiya tries her best to shatter the surrounding Originium with her Arts, but the crystal clusters only grow faster.
Doctor! Get behind me—
Mon3tr—
It all happens in a flash of light.
You see the Originium crystal cluster near you explode. Amiya wanted to protect you, but time does not allow for her reaction.
You see the black monster rush over, blocking you both from the cluster with its large, solid body.
You see Kal'tsit...
What a pity.
Doctor, do you ever doubt the meaning in your own existence? I do.
I understand my mission clearly. It is difficult, but I recognize its meaning.
As for the future, I believe you will find an answer. I can only persevere in that belief.
Don't spare too much worry for me. Rhodes Island shouldn't be so fragile that one person's departure would leave it unable to proceed on.
My hope?
It is to protect yours and Amiya's hopes.
Though our definitions of 'protect' have nothing in common, it's an oath I will never abandon. I will be standing here with you, until the very end.
We are unable to forget the past, but a different future may await you.
I have found the meaning of my life.
Please lead Rhodes Island...
And keep going.
Doctor.

Originium crystals begin to spread as they swallow her entire body, only to shatter as the wind blows them away—your eyes fail to capture the entire process.
With strength, comes fragility.
A long life ends here.
She has kept her promise.
Kal'tsit has disappeared from your vision, before you even have a chance to process what has happened.
The only thing left on the deck is a jacket.
(Pained wail)
Next, Mon3tr's massive frame begins to contract, shrinking into a black, crystalline cocoon.
AMa-10 was one of my proud creations.
This was not the result I had hoped for.
It had its own code name. I do not recognize any significance in the renaming.
Priestess walks towards the cocoon on the deck.
Step back, Doctor! I must protect Mon3tr!
Threads emerge from Amiya's staff, attempting to stall Priestess's steps.
But Originium continues to grow from the ground in strange angles, assimilating Amiya's Arts.
You can tell that Amiya is struggling.
Priestess, whose powers seem impossible to resist, and Kal'tsit's only legacy—
You want to save it?
I want to fix it too.
Not only does AMa-10 carry our hopes, it has also preserved a record that is very important to me.
You have yet to recover from the shock.
You do not even have the luxury of sorrow in the face of the threat before you.
What will Priestess do next? How should you protect Amiya?
Priestess apparently notices your thought process—
Why... are you afraid of me, Dr. Doctor?
She approaches you.
The Originium spreading under your feet has immobilized you and Amiya.
You see her up close for the first time.

opts: Just what are you trying to do?!

lines.x: ...I'm trying to protect you.
Just like what I promised to do.
I promised to protect both you and Amiya.
Until the moment you breathe your last breath, Doctor. That's my responsibility.
But I will never stop hating you. I am in no position to educate or criticize Rosmontis. After all, I might not be able to hold myself back from taking revenge.
There was a similar scenery and a similar conversation. There were different emotions during different eras. All of which are working together to bridge that large gap in your memories.
What... did you just remember?
'Priestess'.
No. This time, you remembered her first. You remembered Kal'tsit.
The physician whom you trusted so deeply still seems to be here.
You recall searching for the clues Kal'tsit left behind in Civilight Eterna...
You recall the words Kal'tsit left for you, when all the links are connected—
'I never forgot.'
(Whispering in unknown language) No matter where in this vast universe you came from, I bid you to witness the beacons we left behind, remnants of a civilization now gone to eternal rest.
(Whispering in unknown language) Perhaps it will be our blessing... and the final gift of hope.
You are certain that those eyes now see you, across the vast reaches of time.
Formulas?!
Dr. Kal'tsit mentioned that it reduces the loss of meta-information during transmission through special mediums...

opts: Obstruct the transmission of information through Originium!

lines.x: What should I do?
There is no time to hesitate, Amiya acts.
Once again, you share your thoughts with Amiya. All the memories of your researches come back to you.
Relying on intuition and prior research, you begin to decipher the formulas, convert, then reverse...
You understand that it will not be completely effective. But you must pray that it works—
For Kal'tsit.

opts: Remember the things in my mind, Amiya!

lines.x: Got it, Doctor!
Just weave it in my Arts, as Logos taught me—
Then—
STOP!
Shockwaves from Amiya's Arts repel the Originium that surrounds you.
This is...?
The Originium attempting to trap you again is unable to progress any further. Amiya's silhouette is reflected on the countless crystalline surfaces.
I'm trying to hold on—
But the Originium is pressing in even faster now...
I... I need to work harder! I can do it!
Amiya's eyes are resolute. You can't help but feel worried for her.
You do not understand the powers that you are toying with.
It seems my Arts aren't just stopping them, it's also detecting her status...
She's not as invincible as I thought—
Indeed, I've been roused too soon. The rate at which Originium has assimilated this planet falls below my expectations.
In addition, the reckless behavior of those two Terrans have prevented me from utilizing Originium as I planned...
But that does not mean you can change the inevitable with your clumsy usage of our technology.
Pink threads fly out from Amiya's staff, dancing in the space enclosed by Originium.
But you sense that Amiya is facing tremendous pressure.
And Priestess's figure has become completely obstructed by Originium...
You can see Priestess's eyes in every Originium fragment, every facet.
It is as though...
Theresa is teaching me how to cast these new Arts more efficiently, Doctor!
I think I've weakened Priestess's control over Originium. I should be able to delay her—
As Amiya's Arts run wild, you hear a reassuring voice in your head.
Doctor, Amiya, we're in the Abyss. The Originium interference was weakened for a moment.
It was...
Raidian has reestablished communications, but the situation is rough. I can barely use my Arts to help Raidian maintain the link.
Also, she can not detect Dr. Kal'tsit's biosignal.
Where are you? What's the situation?

opts: We're on the deck of Rhodes Island!

lines.x: You heard the Doctor.
Don't worry, Lancet-2 will help monitor your status.
I will not let you down, Dr. Warfarin!
Once you're out of the ship, establish an isolation zone around the ship according to Kal'tsit's original plan. Don't let any of those things get out!
You are not coming with us, Dr. Warfarin?
My patients are still here. I'll lead their evacuation myself.
Don't worry about me.
(Why am I not hearing from you, Kal'tsit?)
I'm working on it! Give me some time!
More importantly, once PRTS is shut down, its core data must be downloaded—
Kal'tsit has set up a backdoor.
It will be the key to eliminating the threat, once and for all.
Mechanist acknowledges.
We are moving through the Abyss.
Counting on you!
Is Kal'tsit hurt, Doctor?
Why am I not hearing from her?

opts: Amiya and I will fight to the bitter end.

lines.loc: Inside the AbyssAt the same time

lines.x: We have restored communications with the Doctor.
We stick to the plan.
I'm not detecting any more enemies.
Nor any new data transmission from the PRTS core.
It's too quiet.
Location of the core, Raidian?
Here you go.
Continue to push towards the core and attempt to deactivate it.
I'll take point! PRTS will definitely come up with new waves of enemies to meet us.
I'll take charge of analyzing the core.
'Aurora.'
This little light will guide our way—
What is it, Logos?
This is not my light...
A rhombus-shaped halo hangs in the darkness, swaying gently.
It is like a cold eye, gazing upon all present.
Alert...
Is this the 'Priestess' that Dr. Kal'tsit was talking about?
I don't think so.
I just detected a large amount of haphazard but familiar signals.
They come from Rhodes Island itself.
What... is this?
A massive machine emerges in the endless white noise—
All present are familiar with the components that make up its frame.
Closure has often showed off the parts that she used to modify Rhodes Island's modules. Some of them were made by the elite operators themselves.
PRTS has repurposed and weaponized Rhodes Island's facilities.
But where did they come from? They weren't supposed to be in the Abyss...
Originium.
Originium shards scatter irregularly across the unusual machine.
No longer do they simply consume; now, they are creating.
Are you telling me Originium can recreate the stuff that's on Rhodes Island?
Logos is reminded of the tower in the Assimilated Universe, and of 'her'.
It's not that simple.
PRTS does not have the capability to decode Originium data. Someone else is intervening.
Priestess.
Its data is connected to the core of PRTS in a way that I can't understand. These things are different from the Originium entities that we faced!
Looks like we're in for a fight.
I see no other way.
Ready?
Ready.
The rhombus-shaped eye glows even brighter.
Controlled by PRTS, the mimic machine charges up.

lines.st: No target restrictions. Combat style analysis complete.
No power restrictions.
PRTS final cleanup protocol loaded—

lines.x: This is a tough one.
It's analyzing our combat styles and nullifying our attacks.
We've tried to adapt our tactics many times—
Working in conjunction with Logos, Ascalon, and Misery's attacks have quickly destroyed the mimic machine's legs.
Keep it going. We must learn from each attack and be creative too—
The mimic machine has already fell countless times under the team's coordinated attacks, but each time it stands back up.
Look out! It's charging up again.
It keeps repairing itself with Originium.
We can't let it wear us down.
We need to destroy that rhombus eye.
I have just the arrow for the job.
Two arrows leave Stormeye's bow as the words leave his mouth.
One breaks the mimic machine's shield, but the other is stopped just before it reaches the rhombus-shaped eye...
The mimic machine has finished charging. Bright red light bursts out—
Then Rosmontis's Arts force the arrow into the eye of the mimic machine.
Do you hurt?
Kaboom.
Rosmontis's Arts block the eye of terror at the exact moment of its outburst, sending overflowing energy into every direction in the Abyss.
The spinning bone pen floats on top of Mechanist's head, shielding him from the rampant energy.
Thanks, Logos. Good thing you guys distracted the thing.
External attacks are ineffective. We're counting on you to find a way through from inside.
How are we doing, Mechanist?
After talking to Closure, we're certain that this is a special Sarcophagus.
It's not just a hibernation device; it's also what truly stores PRTS's data and supplies it with power.
The backdoor Dr. Kal'tsit left us will allow me to connect directly to PRTS's core server.
I'll destroy all the data once I've downloaded PRTS's core data.
Is this the only way to put that big guy to rest?
Yes.
How long do you need?
Mechanist glances at his equipment.
Two-hundred forty seconds!
Understood.
We'll buy the time for you.
The sound of the bone whistle echoes in the Abyss. Logos returns to the elite operators' ranks.
The mimic machine once again stands up before them.
Dr. Doctor trusts you implicitly. Even with a fractured memory—
Dr. Doctor believes you are hope.

opts: Don't let her distract you, Amiya!
We just need to buy a little more time for the others...

lines.x: You should get ready to retreat—
There is no way out for Dr. Doctor.
We will inevitably end up on the same path, our subjective sentiments notwithstanding.
You and I need not be in opposition, Amiya. I can make your wish come true.
You always trusted me.
Countless eyes look at you from the crystals. Priestess's eyes.
You made the same promise to me. We can find the answer to the ultimate question together.
Time is short, Dr. Doctor.
I don't need to lie to you.
This humanitarianism that you think you're advocating simply serves to extend the suffering of Terrans before the inevitable fall of Terra.
Any questions that you still have, I can answer them.
She's getting weaker, Doctor!
Most of the operators have evacuated. Maybe, maybe...
I want to try—
You want to kill me?
Is this what you want too... Dr. Doctor?
'Priestess'.
There are still so many secrets within her that you want to find out, about yourself, about Kal'tsit, about Originium, about that impending threat...
But you know that where she stands inevitably makes her the enemy of all Terra.
You missed your chance.
Stay, Dr. Doctor—
You could hardly imagine the ordeal that Amiya has gone through. She is already well past her limit.
All the threads shatter, and Originium once again closes in upon you.
You try to escape the crystals, carrying Amiya in your arms, but the rampant growth of Originium has sealed the path—
Made it in time.
Mechanist and Closure have successfully retrieved the core data of PRTS.
We will cover your retreat.
Now. Go!
Wait... there's one more thing!
With what little strength she has left, Amiya conjures a few strands of threads, and pulls the cocoon that was Mon3tr into her arms.
I need your help, Touch!
These patients were transferred from intensive care. We don't have enough emergency equipment.
I need you to stabilize their conditions first.
Try to gather all the wounded here, and leave the rest to me.
Casualty report?
Things are still very much in flux. We don't have a hard figure.
Current injury estimates are thirty-one serious, one hundred seventy-three minor. No deaths yet.
We were able to complete the evacuation ahead of schedule, thanks to the safe zone created by Logos and Mechanist, and the help that you and Pith provided.
...One dead. A patient.
I'll report in detail to the Doctor and Kal—
She stops in mid-sentence.
They have all been avoiding that name.
The one who had always tended to the wounded after each operation.
Kal'tsit.
Help the others establish the isolation zone. We need to keep an eye on the ship to make sure no enemies get out.
...Roger.
Warfarin—
I'm already doing my best to stay focused, Touch.
Now is not the time.
What's the situation with Dr. Doctor and Amiya?
We all need some time to process what happened.
We're at the isolation zone outside the ship. It's safe enough for now.
Is Amiya okay?
Rhodes Island—
That ship which you once called home is now completely encased in a massive Originium crystal.
The wind blows over it, blurring its outline with sand and dust. You know that you have lost Rhodes Island.
You hold Amiya in your arms. She is unconscious, but still holding that crystalline cocoon in her own arms.
Doctor. Rhodes Island...

opts: Our greatest threat at present still lies within.
We must prepare ourselves before we confront her again.
I'm not sure what this name truly stands for.
But I will not forgive her.

lines.x: What... happened to Dr. Kal'tsit?
The others who escaped the Originium-blockaded area begin to gather.
Yet no one feels relieved, despite the successful evacuation.
Ascalon's question weighs heavily on everyone's hearts.
No one could have seen this coming—
You feel dazed as you look at the black crystal that an unconscious Amiya still clings desperately to.
What next?
So... We've lost it?

opts: No. Rhodes Island is never lost.
All of you are here. Always have been.

lines.x: Can you hear me, Doctor? This is Vina.
I heard about Rhodes Island. I'm very sorry.
The nomadic plate that Dr. Kal'tsit purchased near Butchart is nearing completion.
I'll be coming to visit soon. Let me know if you need anything.
Take care, you and Amiya...

lines.loc: New Rhodes Island under construction, Butchart, on the Victorian borderOne month later

lines.x: Midnight asked me to bring this wine back from Siracusa, Popukar.
He said it was a... Brunello specialty. I'm not a wine expert, though. Is he getting better?
He's still locked up in Medical. Dr. Warfarin grounded him.
He got hurt dealing with those weird things coming out of the ship, which made Dr. Warfarin really nervous.
I'll give these to Midnight.
Uh... what exactly happened?
I heard a bunch of versions. Some say the ship came alive and turned into a monster...
Some say they saw friends and family they thought were dead... then our ship was gone, just like that—
Well, I'm not too sure either...
It all happened so fast. I was listening to Raidian and Dr. Kal'tsit—
Dr. Kal'tsit.
Her absence will take some getting used to.
Looks like we can complete the assembly earlier than scheduled.
The main connectors of this nomadic plate had already been modified to fit our equipment. The hardest part was already taken care of.
The construction has been going much quicker than expected, thanks to all the operators who provided help.
Logos's incantations saved us a lot of effort moving stuff, and Raidian saved a lot of time by checking the circuits for us.
Angelina even came back to help us do the heavy lifting!
All that stuff laid out on deck in an orderly manner in the blink of an eye—sorry, I'm getting ahead of myself.
It's fine. We're all excited to hear that things are going smoothly.
But I guess you're here for something else. Is there a problem?
Well, yes.
Some of the pipes are so narrow that even these guys couldn't get them checked.
(Comes out from behind the engineer looking frustrated)
Has Closure fixed these already?
(Lowers head in shame)
What happened on the ship wasn't your fault, little fella.
We need to think of a way—
The Doctor said I'm needed here.
—Ray?!
I was wondering why Closure rushed me over here, until I overheard your conversation.
If you need to check the pipes...
This little runt oughta come in useful.
(Slowly stretches its backfin)
Thank you for the detailed report on the incident, Closure.
No worries. It's just part of the deal.
Yes, I understand. I'll report back to Consuls Clementia and Gladiia and communicate your warning.
As for the name of the individual—
'Priestess'.
We will reference our own databanks.
If the name has also appeared in any records of Ægir, then the threat you encountered was no coincidence.
I'll let you know about the results as soon as possible. We will also review the Joint Marine Corps deployment of the other consuls.
Once you are ready, we will deliver the necessary equipment, including medical facilities and Catastrophe warning systems.
Thank you.
You're growing into your position, Closure.
Oh, please, spare me.
I would much rather be dealing with machines.
I didn't expect Kal'tsit's job to be this hard...
Are you getting ready to leave?
No time to waste.
Babel must be informed about the details of this incident. Kazdel will check the status of Amnannam on our behalf.
Remember to tell Wiš'adel that the Doctor recommends exercising maximum caution in reviewing the use of that piece of Originium.
Don't worry, the Doctor already hammered the point in. I'll be going, then.
You're not saying goodbye to the others?
They've got plenty to do as things stand.
There's just one thing. About what happened to Dr. Kal'tsit... should we tell Babel?
Tell Wiš'adel first. She'll want to know.
Let her decide whether to tell the others. I trust her on this.
Tracking you down wasn't easy, Closure. What's going on with the thing that I asked?
We got Nuova Volsinii's response. The new batch of medical equipment will be shipped in through their port.
The Engineering Department is expecting to complete the construction of the medical lab that you requested in three days.
Good. The medical center should be ready to accept patients, in that case.
Kal'tsit bought this plate a long time ago. It's much bigger than the old Rhodes Island, and it feels much more like an 'island'.
But it's going to take some time for things to go back to the way they were before.
Don't worry. I've made things modular from the get-go this time, which should make future upgrades much easier.
One more thing—
About her office...
Just rebuild it the way it was. Medical will maintain it.
You know, this really is going to take some getting used to.
I'll help the Doctor and Amiya with the administrative stuff as much as I can while she's gone.
I don't want to disappoint her.
I'll help with things in Medical.
Did the Doctor and Amiya talk to you about recovering the other modules?
Of course. They're very energetic about it. Everything is progressing smoothly.
But it will take time to fully rebuild Rhodes Island.
Retrieving the original equipment, building supply channels, restoring communications with local branches, external PR, debriefing returning operators—
The list of things that need to be done just goes on and on.
Which reminds me... about that patient, Chloe...
I've sent someone to the Londinium office to locate her family.
The paper that Chloe and I were co-authoring is almost complete. It will be submitted to all the major medical journals on Terra.
Her sacrifice has helped achieve a true breakthrough in the research of Originium's mutative properties, and the resulting new symptoms of Oripathy.
I must bring this to the attention of more professionals, and make sure that her memory lives on in the minds of more people...
Yes, we couldn't have done it without her.
Closure rubs her eyes.
Overwork is bad for you.
Yeah, the Doctor told me not to push myself too far. But...
Trying to paralyze yourself with work may do the job for a while, but you'll eventually end up harming yourself.
Doctor's advice.
...All of Rhodes Island is my responsibility now.
Besides, Kal'tsit had even more on her plate.
Then come to Medical one of these days. I'll get you checked up.
Your blood doesn't even smell of machine oil these days. HR is telling me to keep a close eye on you.
Okay... Thanks...
Are the Doctor and Amiya still observing 'Mon3tr' in that forest of yours?
Yes.
I still think it should be done on the ship. I can provide support—
This new Rhodes Island can't afford risks.
You think that Priestess may have tampered with what Kal'tsit left behind?
No one knows.
The rain seems to be getting heavier.
You prop up your coat, shielding Amiya from the sudden shower.
The edge of Amiya's skirt is already all wet, but she does not seem concerned.
She is fully focused on the grass field a short distance away.
Wrapped in Kal'tsit's clothing, the black crystal emanates a dark, flowing green light.
It is rather inconspicuous in the dark and wet surroundings.
The light is appearing more and more frequently in recent days.

opts: There are no apparent changes in the surrounding Originium.
Maybe Mon3tr has not been affected by Priestess.

lines.x: You wait and watch, fighting to suppress your hope for a miracle.
For the past month, Amiya has accompanied you on your regular trips to monitor the status of the crystal.
You all needed some time to process what happened.
And you cannot help but hope that the next time you come...
That she will be waiting here for you.
Doctor! Look!
Complex veins meander across the hard surface of the dark-green crystal. It sinks into the ground, then a highly regular shard begins growing, spreading into numerous branches—
The branches continue to grow until they come to a halt at some point, as though someone had cut off its end with scissors.
In this lush forest, the green crystal on the grass seems to become one with the land, as though it was always there.
That's not quite what Theresa witnessed...
The crystal does not stop growing. The branches increase in number, expanding like the verdant crown of a tree, and the veins grow ever more complex and varied.
Can you tell what those veins represent?

opts: They're all interconnected in a fluid cycle.
It's like a particular way of storing data.

lines.x: Storing data?
Kal'tsit's life.
The myriad branches once again merge together, forming a massive cocoon.
All possibilities collapse into one reality.
The symbiont that had been by Kal'tsit's side ever since she awoke will continue to carry her hope, even after she is gone.
It is both a burden and a gift.
Besides, she never had a choice. This was a power that she was born with—
You hear an unfamiliar voice.
...Kal'tsit?
The rain caresses her palms.
An everyday natural phenomenon, so alien to her sensations.
She smells the grass, touches the cold rain... she instinctively puts on the clothes in her hands.
Kal'tsit's clothes.
Kal'tsit...?
She calls Kal'tsit's name in uncertainty. Kal'tsit had always been by her side. She had never been alone before.
No new symbiont has emerged to watch over her, only the two of you in the distance.
She looks up blankly at the sky, trying to process all the memories she possesses, all the memories that belong to AMa-10...
The memories that belong to Kal'tsit.
She recalls her last memory.
On that familiar deck, she fought side-by-side with Kal'tsit against Priestess—
...You gave me freedom.
I can see your memories, all of them...
But I can't sense your presence anymore.
Kal'tsit. Your name... a name that I loved so much—
But it belongs only to you.
My name is Mon3tr.
You gave me this name.
These clothes... did Closure prepare them for me?
(Hmm. Not quite a perfect fit.)
Why are you looking at me like that, Dr. Doctor?
You mime out the shape of the Mon3tr that you know, much larger than the figure in front of you.
It's me, Doctor.
What? No, I don't think so.
Do you remember what happened?
Yes. I faced Priestess together with Kal'tsit, my maker...
Then I lost her.
You and Amiya fall silent.
This is not the outcome you expected, though it is undoubtedly a miracle.
Where are we?
New Rhodes Island?
We lost the old one in the chaos... Priestess has sealed it in Originium.
What?!
Hm—that's Priestess's doing?
(Doctor... does Mon3tr feel a little... weird?)
(It's like—)

opts: (Like she's getting used to her new body.)

lines.x: (I don't sense anything abnormal with her emotions.)
What happened to Priestess?
We're not sure. Rhodes Island is completely encased in Originium, making it nearly impossible to penetrate.
We're keeping it under observation until we're fully prepared for our next move.
Kal'tsit has left behind some important information... but my mind is a mess. I need your help.
Of course. But we'll need to get you checked out at the new base first.
This is important for your own health, and for the safety of Rhodes Island.
You'll need to keep a low profile, until we fully understand what's going on with you.
Right now, there are still a lot of operators hoping that we'll bring Dr. Kal'tsit home.
Give them some time, and give us some time... I'm sorry, Mon3tr.
I understand.

Kal'tsit would have approved of your decision.
I will cooperate with you, fully—
Say, uh, can I consider myself a Rhodes Island Operator now?
Just like Kal'tsit.
I asked Kal'tsit so many times before, but she kept coming up with excuses to refuse me!

opts: Do you agree with Rhodes Island's ideals?

lines.x: Of course.
Well... I can't say I don't have any misgivings about the present situation, but I've never doubted Kal'tsit.

opts: Rhodes Island will never turn away a friend who has always been by our side.

lines.x: Doctor? Where are you going?
Give the Doctor some space for a while, Mon3tr.
So you're sure there are no other issues, Dr. Warfarin?
I've conducted a full examination of Mon3tr under her guidance. I didn't find any trace of Priestess's influence.
But Mon3tr and Kal'tsit were always full of secrets. There's a lot of research that I want to try—
(Kal'tsit was never so cooperative with exams!)
But I have a paper to write.
You're not going to review the information Kal'tsit left with us?
I'd love to, but not now.
This is a moment for Dr. Doctor and Amiya to share. I can wait for a better time.
(Take good care of her, Doctor.)
(I was surprised by Mon3tr's frankness when I was with her.)
(She may have all of Kal'tsit's memories, but it's hard for me to treat them as the same person.)
You watch Mon3tr curiously fiddling with the command center equipment, as Amiya explains to her the additions to the newly rebuilt room.
She is very different from Kal'tsit, but they have the same strong, resolute eyes.

opts: We'll take good care of her, Warfarin.

lines.x: Come find me if you need anything, Mon3tr.
Doctor, Amiya said that the command system hasn't been fully restored?

opts: Closure is working on a new PRTS.
But deciphering the PRTS source code has proven to be a challenge.

lines.x: I can help.
Since Kal'tsit broke through her limit, I am now able to decipher the associated code.
Restoring the command system is a priority for our future operations.
Great! That's one headache gone.
I'll ask Closure to do something about it. For now...
I won't give up on searching for any messages Kal'tsit might have left—

lines.st: Retrieving memory log. Validating integrity.
Memory log restored.
Validating timestamp...
Attendant AMa-10 symbiont memory conversion complete.
Reading restricted log (γ0000)...

lines.x: If you've accessed this log, that means—
You are free.
You are not just 'Kal'tsit's symbiont' anymore. Nor are you a vehicle of the AMa-10 cycle.
...Kal'tsit.
Dr. Kal'tsit...
(Chuckling) I wonder what name you will choose.
Will you keep the name 'Kal'tsit'?
I like this name, but it belongs to you.
...'Mon3tr'. I choose the name you gave me.
To be honest, I don't want you to have to carry the burden represented by this name.
Someone gave me the freedom to seek the meaning of life. You deserve the same chance.
Go forth and find the meaning of your life. Let your heart answer for yourself your expectations for this land—
Is it hope, or destruction?
I'm sorry that I can't be with you anymore, until you've found the answer.
But I have so many questions...
Mon3tr remembers the questions that Warfarin raised to her while examining her—
'Why choose a similar form to Kal'tsit, instead of something more efficient and resilient?'
'Why hide your claws? So that others will not fear you?'
'Why have you chosen to remain human?'
I don't know... I just chose it on instinct.
There are many answers that come to my lips, but I just can't find the right words...
I can't think like you, Kal'tsit. Too much information, too many memories. My mind is a mess...
I want to find a way to bring you back, but—
But you will never doubt your own decision.
We've walked the centuries together. I know full well that you already know the answer.
You just need some time to understand yourself.
Keep moving forward.
The voice stops.
You and Amiya do not want to disturb.
You can feel Amiya trying to hold her emotions back too.
You step closer to her, to give her comfort.
Doctor, I...
...This is—
For the Doctor and Amiya?
Dr. Doctor, Amiya. I tried to envision the circumstances under which you will hear this recording.
I hope you don't find this baffling—
The destruction of Rhodes Island was not unexpected.
We face a foe far stronger than any other that we have faced before. I have failed.
But your war has only just begun.
Increasingly active Originium will bring new challenges. More pain, more suffering, more hatred, more war...
Remember the mission of Rhodes Island...
Gather the strength of the operators scattered across Terra. You will need unity, consolidated force.
This war is one of all Terra.

opts: There's always a Plan B, isn't there?
Is this all she had to say in the end?

lines.x: Sensing your emotions, Amiya grips your hand.
Doctor...
It's okay, Doctor.
If it hurts, say so.
I won't touch your emotions without permission... But I want to share the burden.
Me too... Doctor.
I promised Dr. Kal'tsit that I would do my duty and protect the Doctor. I'll do it.
This time, I will not cry until Rhodes Island's mission is complete.
...I really miss her.
The girl does not answer. She is focused on the databank before her, searching for something.
A massive amount of data scrolls before her eyes, and her gaze grows intent.
There's so much...
She left so much data that I never got to access.
Maybe there's a way... it's possible...
There is a way, Doctor...
We can bring Kal'tsit back.

lines.st: Reading restricted log (Unknown)...
For now, allow me to call you by the name that we chose together, Mon3tr.
People pour love into life and long for salvation whether in hope or fear. That's why they choose to pray to gods that they've never seen...
—That life may never end.
But how could it come true? Even the most tired river must eventually flow into the ocean.
When the final moment comes, when sun, moon and stars fade... when the swaying waves no longer murmur, when all sound and sights are gone.
When the colors of winter and spring are no more, and the boundary of night and day blurs—
And only eternal slumber in endless night remains.
It was a poem that my maker read to me when we were lying upon the frozen earth, both exhausted from playing.
Eternal life is destined to be eternally lonely. I have known how it feels.
I am sure you too can walk alone—
Mon3tr.

eps.name: Where the Land and a Fragment Belong

lines.x:
The auditorium is...
Huh? Is this not the place?
You're back too?
The auditorium is this way. Follow me!
I came back as soon as I got word from the Messenger.
So many people... it's like everybody's back...
The internal layout is being continuously improved, but people still get lost all the time.
We're going to put some signs up.
I couldn't believe it when I first heard that HQ was being moved to a nomadic plate... didn't have the access level to find out what exactly happened in the incident.
All I heard is something about Originium, and that the ship was abandoned and everyone was evacuated.
No need to worry. Like the Doctor said, we'll dig the old ship out.
Uh-huh... I saw Amiya on my way here. I asked about Dr. Kal'tsit.
She didn't go into details, only told me not to worry—
Before I got the chance to comfort her.
Red still harbors hope.
She is hiding in the shadows in a corner, the bag holding her baby tooth in her pocket.
Red wanted to sneak it into Kal'tsit's pocket when she came back, but it was someone else who did.
She wants to hear about Kal'tsit today. She knows that the Doctor and Mon3tr have been working on something in secret.
Uh... Dr. Warfarin? I thought it was the Doctor's presentation.
No need to look so nervous.
I'm simply here to give a talk on a paper. Medical Department's routine.
Dr. Doctor is currently busy with something else, and there is no one more suited than I.
This paper was co-authored by one of my patients, by the name of Chloe.
In the recent incident on Rhodes Island, she helped us complete a critical piece of Oripathy research.
Not only did this research turn the tides at the time, it also provides guidance for every medical worker with an interest in Oripathy moving forward.
Usually, this is the time when I talk about the authors, their biographies, their achievements...
When it comes to Chloe, however, I'm not sure where to start.
Her resume is largely a blank sheet. To be honest, I hardly took any interest in her past in the three years that I cared for her.
I left only a few comments in her file—
Oripathy patient from Victoria. Not always diligent about following doctor's instructions. Makes atrocious noises in the ward with her handmade clocks...
She was the one who proposed the key hypothesis of the experiment, and the one who provided a precious sample.
The sample was herself.
She asked me to harm her, in order to obtain more accurate data from the objective changes that occurred to her.
Few of the operators at the presentation have ever seen Dr. Warfarin so serious.
More than a few thought about sneaking out quietly at first, when they found out that she was the one presenting.
At the end, however, they all stayed, out of respect to Chloe's sacrifice, and because they all wanted to know what exactly happened to Rhodes Island.
As her doctor, I could not approve of her choice.
As a member of Rhodes Island, however, I am indebted to her courage—

lines.st: 'Her condition was unusual, special. Perhaps she had a chance to escape the despair of Oripathy, to live on in another way.'
'Perhaps she could have escaped death.'

lines.x: I-I've made my choice.
You want to go back to the land that belonged to you?
Yes.
You know what this means. You will face death once again.
Death has always been the conclusion to life.
In this place, I've seen so many 'gods' that I could not even fathom, and learned so much from them.
I want to go back and tell my people...
The end may be destined, but they need not live in fear of death and disintegration.
Perhaps I can soothe the fear that Originium brings them.
Is this all you want?
Yes.
The Cautus looks at Priestess with firm eyes. The miracle maker takes her hand.
Come with me, then.
I will take you home.
Is there anything here that you will miss?
The Cautus shakes her head.
Will I ever see you again?
I'm not a prophet. I can't see your future.
Priestess takes the Cautus's hand, and they walk into the shadow of the megastructure.
They walk towards the silent conclusion of countless civilizations, the cradle of new hope.

lines.st: '...Our joint research has validated a key conclusion.'
'A change is occurring to Originium, the nature of which we have yet to ascertain. It has already had a chain effect on our patients.'
'We have empirically demonstrated the existence of new symptoms of Oripathy for the first time since the large-scale infection incident in Victoria three years ago.'
'This will take Terra's Oripathy research into a new direction. Whether this is for the better or for the worse remains to be seen, as we continue to accumulate case studies and data.'

lines.x: The Cautus smells wind and sand as her eyes adjust to the light.
There is no doubt about it. This is her Terra. She is home.
She can still feel the warmth in her hand, but she is alone.
You're gone...
I can't believe there's someone else in this forsaken place—
...seek help from her... she looks even weaker than us...
I know it hurts... I'll find help... wait for me... hang in there...
The Cautus sees someone run towards her frantically.
H-Hello, could you contact the nearest hospital—no, it would be too late—
Would you happen to have any suppressants? It looks like you're Infected too...
The miner grabs Hierda's hand like a drowning man grasps at a straw.
She does not have any suppressants, nor does she know how to contact the nearest hospital.
But she does not want to take hope away from her brethren.
Is there any trouble? You can take me there.
Maybe I can help.
Maybe.
Thank you! Thank you so much!
My brother's over there!
Hierda is stunned by what she sees as they hurry around the hills.
A massive Originium crystal is stuck in between the walls of a vast canyon.
Hierda can see something sealed within the crystal.
A ship?
What... is that?
We found a new vein here, and we wanted to give it a try before we registered it...
I mean, look at how rich the color is! Besides, there might be something even more valuable within...
But listen, there's something not right with this place! It's like someone's watching us, and my brother got infected from a wound he picked up in an accident—
(Screams in pain)
It looks really bad. You know as well as I do that he's about to disintegrate... there's nothing that can be done.
I-I can't just leave him here. We have thirteen siblings waiting for us at home—
Listen to me. Run, as far away as possible.
B-But...
Don't look back. Run.
I've dealt with this before. Leave it to me.
(Mumbles with great difficulty) Go...
I understand your pain. I know the terror that overtakes us when the light fades from our eyes...
I've been there.
(Mutters in shock)
All I can do is walk with you to the end.
The Cautus holds the hardened hand of the dying, trying to give him one last sliver of warmth.
May your pain be eased, even just a little.
My pain... it-it's gone!
The Cautus does not understand what happened—The rocks are still spreading on the body of the dying.
But the color of the black rocks becomes increasingly pure, and they begin to glow orange.
H-How did you do that?!
They don't hurt any more. Why...?
The shocked man tries to move, and finds that movement comes naturally, as though the accident never happened.
Except for the Originium that continues to grow.
Perhaps there will come a day when it replaces his flesh and blood in its entirety.
What will become of him when that day comes?
I feel myself recovering. This is amazing...
H-Have I been freed from death?
He looks at the Cautus girl, only to see the same shocked expression in her eyes.
—Did you grant my wish? Or is it...
She does not see the one she wants to see, but catches a glimpse of a figure on a distant peak, gazing at them.
The figure turns around and leaves.
—This paper means a lot to me personally.
Chloe's efforts reminded me why Rhodes Island exists, and why Kal'tsit is committed to our path.
Unlike any period in its history, Rhodes Island today is not only a pharmaceutical company that fights the pain brought about by Oripathy—
It must deal with the Originium problem at the source too.
We brought hope to those who had lost it. In turn, they repay us with hope.
This paper is Rhodes Island's salute to Chloe, and a promise on our part—
—A promise to Kal'tsit.
You arrive after Warfarin's speech has concluded.

opts: Thank you for doing the presentation on my behalf.
She was a patient who saved her doctor.

lines.x: You look happy. Are you done on your end?
Yes, Dr. Warfarin.
With Mon3tr's help, we managed to identify some key pieces of information in the vast amounts of data that Dr. Kal'tsit left behind.
You mean...?!
Dr. Warfarin! I found a way!
My connection to her was severed, but her bio-identification signals and neuron data are still stored inside my brain.
Plus the neural network I had shared with her, in theory, all we'll need is a life support device powerful enough to—
Slow down, slow down! What exactly are you getting at?
What I mean is, although it's a purely theoretical possibility...
By using the power of the Sarcophagus...
We can bring Kal'tsit back!
I knew Kal'tsit wouldn't go so easily...
But the Sarcophagus? The one that was on Rhodes Island?

opts: We can't go back to Rhodes Island for a while.
There is only one other Sarcophagus accessible to us.

lines.x: It's like we've come full circle.
This will not be easy. Ursus remains a dangerous place for Rhodes Island.
But Kal'tsit has planned ahead and made arrangements at all our branches across Terra. Our first priority should be to restore communications with them.
We'll need their help for what we're trying to do.

opts: We'll make contact with Ursus first.
Then, we'll find a way to bring Kal'tsit back.

lines.x: So this is what Terra looks like now.
'Kolossal Prime Vein'? That's what they're calling that piece of Originium these days?
Hmm. Maybe it'll be a good choice for the next step.
The rate of Originium propagation is far below expected values, and AMa-10 has been a significant hindrance.
The project must be accelerated. Time is short.
The evolution of this planet is taking place too slowly.
More radical means are called for—
She walks deeper into the Abyss, now sealed in Originium.

lines.st: PRTS rebooting...
Reboot failed.
Error: Core code missing.
Error: Database missing. Search function unavailable.

lines.x: ...Looks like connection by PRTS won't be possible for now.
Perhaps—

lines.st: You don't know what you're doing, Dr. Doctor...
It's unfortunate that you have forgotten everything, our explorations of space, the shared pins that anchor our thoughts, and the 'debate' that has lasted so many millennia...
And your very first promise to me...
Time, the most cruel of mazes. It leads us astray, and separates us from each other.
But I believe, I've always believed, that you will once again recognize the truth of this universe, and our inevitable end.
I believe that you will come back to me when that time comes.
I will wait.

lines.x: You've led us on a long walk, Sankta.
Have you decided to become a bounty hunter too?
You talk too much, Sarkaz.
Your fate was sealed the moment you decided to attack that innocent Siracusan caravan.
But it was too close to the Kazdelian border. His Holiness has encouraged upholding justice through proper means, to maintain peace on the surface, and it was not my place to go against the rules.
This place will do.
You're too far from home, mercenary.
You should have stayed in Kazdel, and never came out of that pit of yours.
Hmph.
Oren raises his gun.
...You missed?
You—you—
My hand should not be trembling.
Yes, you should fear me, Sarkaz. It's over.
Wait! Wait—
M-Monster! What are you?!
Oren takes a questioning look at his gun, and sees a shocking sight in the reflection of the gleaming metal.
His halo flashes frantically above his head.
Dazed and confused, he looks at his reflection again, and the devil that he is about to execute—
They look exactly the same.
